passages.name: AnniesApartmentInvestigate
passages.content:
You look around the apartment for signs of anything personal. Everything looks stylised and unique to Annie, from the 50s furnishings to the pastel coloured flowers. Nothing, however, seems treasured. 

Until your eyes fall on a photo frame on the shelves, tucked behind a plant. You move over to it, brushing aside the leaves to see more.

The photo is of two girls sitting outside on a beautiful cloudless day, smiling brightly at the camera. One of them you instantly recognise as Annie, although she looks a year or two younger. The other you’ve never seen before. Her dark hair contrasts with her pale skin, her eyes are dark to the point where you can barely separate her pupils from their iris. 

The two of them sit on a grass verge, legs sprawled across each other, their temples leaning against each other. Both look carefree. Happy. Innocent.
 

 “Friend of yours?” you ask Annie, as she continues to mix you both some drinks.

 

 She looks over at you and her eyes linger on the frame for a moment. Then she turns back to cutting up a lime for the gin. “Penny,” she says, her back turned to you. “We went to Charlton together. We were... friends.”

 

 “Just friends?” you ask.

 

 
 Annie stops cutting and turns to face you. Her smile gone, but not replaced with anger or pain. To the best of your reading, she just seems confused. You catch sight of her blushing slightly.

 

 She glances at the frame again, then back to you. “Girls are made for boys, .” she says without feeling.

 

 She looks at you with confused eyes, almost faltering. “Of course,” she says quietly. “That’s what they taught us at school.”

 

 Annie turns back to preparing your cocktails. You look back up at the photo. In spite of her reaction, you are sure that this is personal to her. It could probably power the amulet.

 
 Annie stops cutting and turns to face you. Her flirty smile gone, but not replaced with anger or pain. To the best of your reading, she just seems confused.

passages.content_zh:
你環顧公寓，尋找任何私人的跡象。從 50 年代的家具到柔和色彩的花朵，安妮的一切看起來都很時尚且獨特。然而，似乎沒有什麼好珍惜的。 

直到您的目光落在架子上藏在植物後面的相框上。你走到它身邊，撥開樹葉以查看更多內容。

照片中，兩個女孩在晴朗無雲的日子裡坐在外面，對著鏡頭燦爛地微笑。其中一個你一眼就能認出是安妮，儘管她看起來年輕一、兩歲。另一個你以前從未見過。她的深色頭髮與蒼白的皮膚形成鮮明對比，她的眼睛黑得幾乎無法將瞳孔與虹膜分開。 

兩人坐在草地邊，雙腿叉開，太陽穴互相靠著。兩人看起來都無憂無慮。快樂的。清白的。
 

 「你的朋友？」你問安妮，她繼續為你們兩個調飲料。

 

 她看著你，目光在畫面上停留了一會兒。然後她又開始切檸檬來調製琴酒。 「佩妮，」她背對著你說。 “我們一起去了查爾頓。我們是……朋友。”

 

 「只是朋友？」你問。

 

 
 安妮停止切割並轉身面對你。她的笑容消失了，但取而代之的是憤怒或痛苦。從你的閱讀來看，她只是看起來很困惑。你看到她微微臉紅。

 

 她又看了一眼畫面，然後又看了你一眼。 「女孩是為男孩而生的。」她毫無感覺地說。

 

 她用困惑的眼神看著你，幾乎顫抖著。 「當然，」她平靜地說。 “這就是他們在學校教我們的。”

 

 安妮繼續準備雞尾酒。你回頭看看照片。不管她的反應如何，你確信這對她來說是私人的。它可能可以為護身符提供動力。

passages.name: AnniesApartmentPubDate
passages.content:
Annie raises a glass towards you and offers it. You thank her and take it.

 

 “Cheers,” she says, simply, and clinks her glass against yours. You both take a sip of the cocktail. It’s strong, but refreshing. 

 

 Annie sits down on the sofa and gestures for you to join her. "It's been so long since I've had a girl's night, . I've forgotten how much fun it can be just to unwind.”

 

 You take a seat and Annie smiles, her legs almost touching yours. “Don't get me wrong, I like male company," she tells you. "But it's just different, when it's just the girls."

 

 You nod. “It’s been nice.”

 

 A flash of sadness spreads across Annie's face. You can see the alcohol glazing over her eyes. "It can get real lonely around here. The guys are just after one thing. I don't mind it, but that's not all I am. I like just being in good company. Like you, ."

 

 Your eyes meet. You feel her body close to yours. She doesn't look away, but her smile is gone. Instead, her lips are slightly parted, as she stares back at you.

 You take a seat and Annie wriggles a little closer to you, her legs almost touching yours. “Have you had a good night, ?” she murmurs to you.

 

 You nod. “It’s been nice.”

 

 She puts a hand on your knee and grins. “Is there anything I could do to make it better?”

 

 Her hand drifts up to your thigh, slowly. As her hand moves, her eyes do not break with your own.

 

 “I want to make it better,” she whispers, leaning close in towards you, her glossy lips nearing yours. “Much better, .”
 
 

 You feel her hand brush against the bottom of your groin, just as she leans in for a kiss.

passages.name: AnniesApartmentBedroom
passages.content:
**Annie's Apartment - Bedroom**

[image: ./images/Annies_Apartment_Bedroom.png]

Annie’s bedroom is furnished in pastels with lace curtains, floral quilts and vintage trinkets that exude femininity. It smells of lavender sachets.

 
 
 
 
 

 You stir, your consciousness returning to the land of the living. You blink, glancing down at the comforting outline of your body underneath the bed sheets. Morning daylight shines in through the windows and the sounds of birds chirping can be heard even through the window glass.
 
 

 The smell of sweet perfume invades your senses and you remember where you are. Annie's Apartment. You grab your clothes from the floor and throw them on, glancing at yourself - your own self - in her mirror. You are ready to face the day ahead.
 
 
 
Annie sleeps soundly on her bed, right where you left her. With any luck, she'll just think she over-exerted herself.

 
 
 **Actions**

 [[Go to Annie's Apartment->AnniesApartment]]
passages.content_zh:
**安妮的公寓 - 臥室**

[圖：./images/Annies_Apartment_Bedroom.png]

安妮的臥室以柔和的色調佈置，配有蕾絲窗簾、花卉被子和散發著女性氣質的復古小飾品。聞起來有薰衣草香袋的味道。

 
 
 
 
 

 你甦醒了，你的意識回到了生者之地。你眨著眼睛，低頭看了一眼床單下自己舒適的身體輪廓。清晨的陽光透過窗戶照射進來，透過窗玻璃也聽見鳥兒的叫聲。
 
 

 甜美的香水味侵入您的感官，讓您記住自己身在何處。安妮的公寓。你從地板上抓起衣服穿上，在鏡子裡看了一眼自己──你自己。你已經準備好面對未來的一天了。
 
 
 
安妮在床上睡得很香，就在你離開她的地方。運氣好的話，她只會認為自己用力過度了。

 
 
 **行動**

 [[前往安妮公寓->安妮公寓]]

Despite it being soft, the bed is as unfamiliar as the body you're in is familiar. *Your body.* After all that’s happened, there's a comforting pull in that. Soon you find yourself drifting to sleep.

passages.name: AvianLaneLogainDialogue0
passages.content:
Logain flashes you a cocky smile as you approach him. “Hey squirt,” he says. “Didn’t expect to see you outside on a Saturday night.”

 
 
 Logain snorts as you approach him. “Just what my night needed. An unpleasant surprise.”

 

 He eyes you darkly, as if challenging you to speak. Taking the hint, you leave him alone.
 
 
 
 “Hey doll,” Logain greets you casually. “How’s your night going?”

 
 “Hey chief,” Logain greets you casually. “How’s your night going?”

 
 
 Upon seeing you, Logain's expression hardens. An eyebrow rises, daring you to engage with him. He’s not interested in talking with you. Not wishing to get into a conflict, you back away. 
 
 
 Logain smirks as you approach. "Ah, dad's favourite CCO. How's the night treating you?"

 

 "CCO?" you ask, frowning.

 

 "Chief Coffee Orderer," he replies, grinning. "The old man actually smiles when he talks about you bringing him an espresso in the morning."

 

 "How nice," you say, curtly.

 
 
 
A thought appears to cross his face. "Speaking of," he says. "How about you bring me something? I can make it worth your while."

 
 
 Logain looks at you as you approach. "You need something?" he asks, aggressively.

 

 The lack of his trademark grin tells you that he's not interested in talking to you.
 
 
 The teenager’s blue eyes lock with yours. His eyes narrow slightly.
 
 

 “Evening,” he says to you, guarded. He offers nothing more to build upon. You smile politely at him and step away. Whatever Logain is up to, he clearly doesn’t trust you enough to tell you about it.
 
 

 Logain isn’t ignoring you, but you notice that his attention drifts as soon as someone walks by.
 

 

 **Actions**

 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 <>
passages.content_zh:
當你走近洛根時，他向你露出了一個得意的微笑。 「嘿，噴，」他說。 “沒想到週六晚上會在外面見到你。”

 
 
 當你接近洛根時，他哼了一聲。 “正是我的夜晚所需要的。一個不愉快的驚喜。”

 

 他陰沉地看著你，彷彿在挑戰你說話。聽懂了暗示，你就離開他了。
 
 
 
 「嘿，娃娃，」洛根隨意地向你打招呼。 “你晚上過得怎麼樣？”

 
 「嘿，隊長，」洛根隨意地向你打招呼。 “你晚上過得怎麼樣？”

 
 
 看到你後，洛根的表情變得嚴肅起來。眉毛揚起，讓你敢於與他接觸。他沒興趣跟你說話。不想陷入衝突，你就後退了。 
 
 
 當你走近時，洛根傻笑著。 “啊，爸爸最喜歡的 CCO。今晚你過得怎麼樣？”

 

 「科科？」你皺著眉頭問。

 

 「首席咖啡訂購員，」他笑著回答。 “當老人說起你早上給他送一杯濃縮咖啡時，他真的笑了。”

 

 「真好，」你簡短地說。

 
 
 
他的臉上浮現出一個念頭。 「說到，」他說。 “你給我帶點東西怎麼樣？我可以讓你值得。”

 
 
 當你走近時，洛根看著你。 「你需要什麼東西嗎？」他咄咄逼人地問。

 

 他沒有標誌性的笑容，這表明他對和你說話不感興趣。
 
 
 少年的藍眼睛與你的目光鎖定。他的眼睛微微瞇起。
 
 

 「晚上好，」他警戒地對你說。他沒有提供更多可以建立的東西。你禮貌地對他微笑，然後走開。無論洛根在做什麼，他顯然都沒有足夠的信任你來告訴你這件事。
 
 

 洛根並沒有忽視你，但你注意到，只要有人走過，他的注意力就會轉移。
 

 

 **行動**

passages.name: AvianLaneJenniferSwitchDay1
passages.content:
**Avian Lane**

[image: ./images/Avian_Lane.png]

In most towns Avian Lane would be no more than an alleyway. The backs of buildings on the main street run down one side. On the other, rundown apartments are housed above a line of shops that are more closed than open.

You step out into the morning light of Avian Lane, quiet and peaceful. Jennifer’s work clothes feel more present than before, uniform hugging your curves, wind breezing against your legs.

You look back up to her living room window. She - you - stands there, smiling. Waves to you.

What have you agreed to?

Your hand goes to the amulet. Empty. Jennifer has drained it. You couldn’t transform if you tried. You could go back in, but even now, you can sense Jennifer’s power emanating from inside her apartment. You don’t know what she’s done since you saw her last, but you’ve never felt so powerless.

Listening to your heels clipping on the pavement, you walk to Jennifer’s work. Your work. For the day ahead working at the Evans' Department Store.

**Actions**

<>
passages.content_zh:
**鳥類巷**

[圖：./images/Avian_Lane.png]

在大多數城鎮，鳥類巷只不過是一條小巷。主街上建築物的背面沿著一側延伸。另一方面，破舊的公寓位於一排商店的上方，這些商店更多的是封閉的而不是開放的。

你走進鳥巷的晨光，安靜祥和。珍妮佛的工作服比以前更有現代感，制服緊貼你的曲線，微風拂過你的雙腿。

你回頭看向她客廳的窗戶。她——你——站在那裡，微笑著。向你揮手。

你同意了什麼？

你的手伸向護身符。空的。珍妮佛已經把它喝乾了。如果你嘗試的話，你也無法改變。你可以回去，但即使是現在，你也能感覺到珍妮佛的力量從她的公寓散發出來。你不知道自從上次見到她以來她做了什麼，但你從未感到如此無力。

聽著腳跟踩在人行道上的聲音，你走向詹妮弗的工作地點。你的工作。明天將在埃文斯百貨公司工作。

**行動**

<>

passages.name: EmilysRoomVanityTable
passages.content:
You step toward Emily’s vanity table, the smooth surface cluttered with cosmetic palettes, brushes, and an assortment of tubes and compacts. A large mirror is propped against the wall, reflecting the soft lighting of the room, making the space feel warmer, more intimate.
 

 You step toward Emily’s vanity table, the smooth surface cluttered with cosmetic palettes, brushes, and an assortment of tubes and compacts. A large mirror is propped against the wall, reflecting the soft lighting of the room, making the space feel warmer, more intimate.

 

 For a moment, you just stare. Make-up was always something Emily had, but you never really paid attention to it before. Now, standing here, there’s so much more than you have seen before.

 

 Your fingers hover over the collection of lipsticks, powders, and mascara, the tools of an everyday ritual you’ve never performed.

 

 You could try it. It would be useful to blend in. To aid your investigations.

 

 And that’s why you are here, right?

**Actions**

<>

<>
passages.content_zh:
你走向艾米麗的梳妝台，光滑的表面上堆滿了化妝品調色板、刷子以及各種各樣的管狀和粉盒。一面大鏡子靠在牆上，反射著房間裡柔和的燈光，讓空間感覺更溫暖、更親密。
 

 你走向艾米麗的梳妝台，光滑的表面上堆滿了化妝品調色板、刷子以及各種各樣的管狀和粉盒。一面大鏡子靠在牆上，反射著房間裡柔和的燈光，讓空間感覺更溫暖、更親密。

 

 有那麼一會兒，你只是凝視著。艾米麗一直都有化妝，但你以前從來沒有真正關注過它。現在，站在這裡，你看到的東西比你以前看到的要多得多。

 

 你的手指懸停在一系列口紅、粉餅和睫毛膏上，這些是你從未使用過的日常儀式的工具。

 

 你可以嘗試一下。融入其中會很有用。有助於你的調查。

 

 這就是你來這裡的原因，對嗎？

**行動**

<>

<>

passages.name: EmilysRoomMakeup
passages.content:
You pick up a brush, hesitating before dipping it into a powder compact. It looks easy enough, but the moment you try to apply it, the coverage is uneven, the color slightly off. The eyeliner drags, the mascara clumps.

 

 By the time you finish, you look in the mirror and grimace. It’s not great. But it’s a start.

 

 You sigh, reaching for a tissue. Maybe tomorrow, you’ll do better.

 

 Tomorrow?

 

 <> 
 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 This time at the vanity, things go smoother.

 

 You remember how the foundation should be blended, how the eyeliner needs a steady hand. The mascara still smudges a little, but overall, it’s an improvement.

 

 When you glance in the mirror, there’s a difference. You look like yourself, but refined, polished. More put-together.

 

 It feels... nice. Not necessary. But nice.

 

 You smile, watching your reflection look stunning as she does that in the mirror. One more attempt, you think, and you'll be just as good as a professional.

 

 Is that what you want, though?
 
 

 <> 
 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 

 By now, it’s almost automatic.

 

 Your hands move without thinking, each stroke of foundation, blush, and liner feeling as natural as brushing your teeth. You barely have to focus - you just do it.

 

 When you meet your reflection in the mirror, there’s no hesitation. This is just part of your routine now.

 

 Just another day.

 

 Another day of you wearing makeup.

 

 <>

passages.content_zh:
你拿起一把刷子，猶豫著是否要將其浸入粉餅中。看起來很簡單，但當你嘗試塗抹它時，覆蓋不均勻，顏色略有偏差。眼線拖沓，睫毛膏結塊。

 

 當你完成時，你看著鏡子，做了個鬼臉。這不太好。但這是一個開始。

 

 你嘆了口氣，伸手去拿紙巾。也許明天，你會做得更好。

 

 明天？

 

 <>
 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 這次到了虛榮心，事情就順利多了。

 

 你記得粉底應該如何混合，眼線筆需要如何穩定的手。睫毛膏仍然有點髒，但總的來說，這是一個進步。

 

 當你照鏡子時，你會發現有什麼不同。你看起來就像你自己，但精緻、優雅。更能拼湊起來。

 

 感覺……很好。沒有必要。但很好。

 

 你微笑著，看著鏡子裡你的倒影看起來美極了。你想，再嘗試一次，你就會像專業人士一樣出色。

 

 但這是你想要的嗎？
 
 

 <>
 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 

 到目前為止，這幾乎是自動的。

 

 你的雙手不假思索地移動，每一下粉底、腮紅和眼線都感覺像刷牙一樣自然。你幾乎不需要集中註意力——你只要去做就可以了。

 

 當你看到鏡子裡的自己時，你會毫不猶豫。現在這只是你日常工作的一部分。

 

 又是一天。

 

 又是你化妝的一天。

 

 <>

passages.name: EmilysRoomWardrobe
passages.content:
You pull open the wardrobe doors, the faint scent of perfume and fabric softener drifting out as your fingers brush against the hanging clothes inside. The space is tidy, organized, but what catches you off guard is the sheer variety. Far more than you ever expected.

 

 Emily was always practical, a little reserved, but as you scan the collection, you realise something's changed.

 

 The soft flow of dresses, the tailored skirts, the occasional flash of lace or bold colours. It’s more than just everyday wear. Some of these outfits are stylish, even daring, and for the first time, you wonder if Emily was changing before all of this started.

 

 Or if Charlton has just been working hard on her, too.

 
 
 As you open the wardrobe, you immediately notice it. The outfit you wore before is back in its place, neatly folded or hung as if it had never been touched. You hesitate, fingers brushing over the fabric. You didn’t put it back. You were sure of that. But the evidence is right in front of you.

 

 Sighing, you don't understand why you are so surprised. Magic, it seems, continues to have its way with you.
 
 
 You pull open the wardrobe doors, the faint scent of perfume and fabric softener drifting out as your fingers brush against the hanging clothes inside.

 

Actions

 <>

passages.content_zh:
你拉開衣櫃門，當你的手指拂過裡面掛著的衣服時，淡淡的香水和織物柔軟劑的氣味飄了出來。空間整潔、井井有條，但讓你措手不及的是種類繁多。遠遠超出您的預期。

 

 艾蜜莉一向很務實，有點保守，但當你瀏覽這些收藏品時，你會發現有些事情改變了。

 

 柔軟飄逸的洋裝、剪裁考究的裙子、偶爾閃現的蕾絲或大膽的色彩。它不僅僅是日常穿著。其中一些服裝很時尚，甚至大膽，你第一次懷疑艾米麗在這一切開始之前是否已經改變了。

 

 或者如果查爾頓也一直在努力訓練她。

 
 
 當你打開衣櫃時，你會立即註意到它。你之前穿的衣服又回到了原來的位置，整齊地折疊或懸掛起來，就像從未被碰過一樣。你猶豫著，手指拂過布料。你沒有把它放回去。你對此深信不疑。但證據就在你面前。

 

 嘆了口氣，你不明白自己為何如此驚訝。魔法似乎繼續對你起作用。
 
 
 你拉開衣櫃門，當你的手指拂過裡面掛著的衣服時，淡淡的香水和織物柔軟劑的氣味飄了出來。

passages.name: EmilysRoomDress
passages.content:
You sift through the wardrobe, fingers brushing against soft, flowing fabric before pulling out a dress. It’s light, simple, the kind of thing Emily would wear on a casual summer day. There’s something about it that feels effortless yet put together.

 

 You hold it up against yourself, tilting your head in consideration. It’s a safe choice. Not too much, not too little. Pretty, but practical.

 

 Setting it aside, you reach down for a pair of flats, the natural companion to the outfit. With everything laid out neatly on the bed, you inhale deeply before starting to change.

 

 As the fabric settles against your skin, the outfit feels… comfortable. Like you belong in it.

 

 And that thought lingers longer than it should.

 
 
 <> 
 
 Slipping into the dress, you barely give it a second thought. The fabric rests comfortably against your skin, the fit familiar, effortless. It’s just another outfit now, something you pull on without thinking, because why wouldn’t you? It’s easy, stylish, and it suits you. 

 
 
 <> 
 
 

 Actions

 <> 

 
 
 Your fingers trail over a few casual tops before stopping on something different. A skirt, fitted but not too tight, stylish without being overly bold. It does look quite short, though.

 

 You pull it from the hanger, holding it up in front of you. It’s not the kind of thing Emily wore all the time, but you'd seen her in it a few times. When she wanted to stand out a little.

 

 You find a flattering top to match, something that fits just right, and lay them both on the bed. The low-cut neckline gives you pause for thought. You know how much will be on display by wearing it. But something draws you to it, all the same.

 

 Reaching down, you pick out a pair of ankle boots with a high heel, completing the look.

 

 As you start to change, the outfit comes together effortlessly. When you finally look in the mirror, there’s something about it that feels… natural.

 

 Like this isn’t a disguise at all.

 

 Like maybe, just
passages.content_zh:
你仔細翻閱衣櫃，手指拂過柔軟、飄逸的布料，然後拿出一件衣服。它輕盈、簡單，是艾米莉在休閒夏日穿的那種衣服。它給人一種毫不費力卻又組合在一起的感覺。

 

 你把它舉在自己身上，歪著頭思考。這是一個安全的選擇。不算太多，也不算太少。漂亮，但實用。

 

 把它放在一邊，你伸手去拿一雙平底鞋，它是這套服裝的天然搭配。所有東西都整齊地擺放在床上，你深吸一口氣，然後開始換衣服。

 

 當布料貼合您的皮膚時，這套衣服感覺…舒適。就像你屬於其中一樣。

 

 而且這種想法持續的時間比應有的時間還要長。

 
 
 <>
 
 穿上這件衣服，你幾乎不會再多想。布料舒適貼合您的肌膚，貼合舒適，輕鬆自在。現在它只是另一套衣服，你不假思索就穿的東西，因為你為什麼不呢？它簡單、時尚，而且適合您。 

 
 
 <>
 
 

 行動

 <>

 
 
 你的手指滑過幾件休閒上衣，然後停在不同的上衣上。裙子合身但不太緊，時尚又不過分大膽。不過，它看起來確實很短。

 

 你把它從衣架上拉下來，把它舉在你面前。這不是艾米莉一直穿的那種衣服，但你見過她幾次。當她想脫穎而出的時候。

 

 你找到一件合適的上衣，合身、討人喜歡，然後把他們放在床上。低胸領口讓你停下來思考。穿上它你就知道會展示多少。但無論如何，總有一些東西吸引著你。

 

 彎下腰，你挑選了一雙高跟踝靴，完成了整個造型。

 

 當你開始改變時，衣服就會毫不費力地組合在一起。當你最終照鏡子時，你會感覺…很自然。

 

 就像這根本不是偽裝。

 

 就像也許，只是

Jennifer smiles at you when she sees you. “Hey!” she says, as she continues working. “I didn't expect for you to come visit!”

 

 “Just passing by,” you tell her.

 

 She smiles pleasantly, but her attention is drawn away by a customer coughing politely nearby. “Sorry ma’am. One second,” she tells them, before turning back to you.

 

 “I’m sorry. Busy day. Do you want to come around tonight, though?” she asks. “I'll cook something nice. We can catch-up!”

 

 You smile as Jennifer walks off to deal with the customer. 

 Jennifer smiles at you when she sees you. “Hey!” she says, as she continues working. “This is a nice surprise.”

 

 “Just checking in,” you tell her.

 

 She grins, but her attention is drawn away by a customer coughing politely nearby. “Sorry ma’am. One second,” she tells them, before turning back to you.

 

 “I’m sorry. Busy day. But come around tonight?” she asks. “I’ll make it worth your while. I'll cook something nice.”

 

 You smile as Jennifer walks off to deal with the customer. 

 “Oh hi Em,” Jennifer whispers as you approach. “What brings you to the store today?”

 

 “Just browsing,” you say, a little hesitant to say much in your current form. 

 

 “You should take a look at the clothing department,” Jennifer replies. “There’s some new stock that just arrived.”

 

 You nod, looking over at the racks and mannequins on the other side of the store.

 

 “I’m really busy today, but I’ll see you soon, okay? And tell your sister to come round - I'd love to catch-up!"
 
 “I’m really busy today, but I’ll see you soon, okay? And tell your brother I'm expecting him tonight."

 

 “Okay,” you mumble, not for the first time trying to make sense of the messy situation in your brain. You decide to leave Jennifer alone.

 “Hey Andrea,” Jennifer says as you approach. She looks you up and down, then leans in to whisper. "Are you sure you should be walking around dressed like that? Doesn't Sermon mandate you don't wear your uniform outside?"

詹妮弗看到你時會對你微笑。 「嘿！」她邊說邊繼續工作。 “沒想到你竟然會來拜訪！”

 

 「只是路過，」你告訴她。

 

 她笑得很開心，但她的注意力被附近一位禮貌地咳嗽的顧客吸引了。 「對不起，女士。等一下，」她告訴他們，然後轉向你。

 

 「抱歉。今天很忙。不過你今晚想過來嗎？」她問。 “我會做一些好吃的。我們可以趕上！”

 

 當詹妮弗走開去和顧客打交道時，你微笑著。 

 詹妮弗看到你時會對你微笑。 「嘿！」她邊說邊繼續工作。 “這是一個很好的驚喜。”

 

 「只是檢查一下，」你告訴她。

 

 她笑了，但她的注意力被附近一位禮貌咳嗽的顧客吸引了。 「對不起，女士。等一下，」她告訴他們，然後轉向你。

 

 「抱歉。今天很忙。不過今晚能來嗎？」她問。 “我會讓你的時間變得值得。我會做一些好吃的。”

 

 當詹妮弗走開去和顧客打交道時，你微笑著。 

 「喔嗨，嗯，」當你走近時，珍妮佛低聲說道。 “你今天來店裡做什麼？”

 

 「只是瀏覽一下，」你說，以你目前的狀態，有點猶豫是否要說太多。 

 

 「你應該看看服裝部門，」珍妮佛回答。 “有一些新貨剛到。”

 

 你點點頭，看著商店另一邊的貨架和人體模型。

 

 「我今天真的很忙，但我很快就會見到你，好嗎？讓你姐姐過來——我很樂意趕上！”
 
 「我今天真的很忙，但我很快就會見到你，好嗎？並告訴你哥哥我今晚在等他。”

 

 「好吧，」你咕噥道，這並不是第一次試圖弄清楚你大腦中混亂的情況。你決定讓詹妮弗一個人。

 「嘿，安德里亞，」當你走近時，珍妮佛說。她上下打量你，然後傾身低語。 “你確定你應該穿成這樣到處走動嗎？講道不是規定你不能在外面穿制服嗎？”

 

 是

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreChristinaDialogue0
passages.content:
Christina glares at you as you approach. An eyebrow lifts, almost daring you to speak. In spite of the hostile invitation, you decide to leave her alone.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 

 Christina looks up as you approach. “Oh hey, Em. Doing a little shopping?”

 

 You nod. “Something like that.”

 

 The cheerleader rolls her eyes just a little, but pulls out a white leather miniskirt from the clothing rack. “What do you think?” she asks you. “Sophisticated or slutty? I can’t decide.”

 

 You shrug, unsure what Emily would say, or what the right answer is. Christina laughs at your reaction.

 

 “Really, Em. We need to work on your sense of style.” Christina eyes you, appraisingly. “How would you like to do some retail therapy? I’m sure Daddy wouldn’t mind if we picked out some things for you. So. My treat.”

 

 You look back at Christina. Do you want to spend your time with her?

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 Christina politely smiles as you approach. “Oh hi there,” she says, the sing-song tones in her voice just a little too forced.

 

 “Hey Christina,” you reply. “What are you doing?”

 

 “Browsing the new ranges,” she tells you. “My father might be the businessman of our house, but he has terrible taste. Mum picks out most of the stock.”

 

 She eyes you with her cold blue eyes, but turns her attention back to the clothing racks. “I’ll see you at school, then,” she adds, not looking back at you.

 

 Understanding an implied dismissal when you’ve received one, you say goodbye and step away, leaving her to shop.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 

 Christina looks up from the clothing racks as you approach. Her icy eyes look over you, a smile forming. “Jennifer,” she says. “Why aren’t you working? Or have you been slacking off again in the storeroom?”

 

 You glare back at her, trying to hold your head high. At this, she titters. “Oh, Jen. Let’s not pretend. It’s been years since you’ve frightened me. Don’t pretend that we don’t know who you are, now.”

 

 The resolve within
passages.content_zh:
當你走近時，克里斯蒂娜怒視著你。眉毛揚起，幾乎不敢說話。儘管有敵意的邀請，你還是決定讓她一個人。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 當你走近時，克里斯蒂娜抬起頭來。 “哦嘿，Em。去買點東西嗎？”

 

 你點點頭。 “類似這樣的事情。”

 

 啦啦隊長翻了個白眼，但還是從衣架上拿出了一條白色皮革迷你裙。 「你怎麼認為？」她問你。 “精緻還是放蕩？我無法決定。”

 

 你聳聳肩，不確定艾蜜莉會說什麼，或是正確的答案是什麼。克里斯蒂娜對你的反應大笑。

 

 「真的，Em。我們需要努力培養你的時尚感。」克里斯蒂娜用評價的眼光看著你。 “你想不想進行購物療法？我相信爸爸不會介意我們為你挑選一些東西。那麼，我請客。”

 

 你回頭看看克里斯蒂娜。你想和她共度時光嗎？

 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>

 當你走近時，克里斯蒂娜禮貌地微笑著。 「哦，嗨，」她說，她聲音中的歌聲有點太強迫了。

 

 「嘿克里斯蒂娜，」你回答。 “你在幹什麼？”

 

 「瀏覽新系列，」她告訴你。 “我父親可能是我們家的商人，但他的品味很差。媽媽挑選了大部分股票。”

 

 她用冰冷的藍眼睛看著你，但又把注意力轉回衣架上。 「那麼我們學校見。」她補充道，沒有回頭看你。

 

 當你收到一封郵件時，你明白這是一種暗示性的解僱，所以你道別並走開，留下她去購物。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 當你走近時，克里斯蒂娜從衣架上抬起頭來。她冰冷的眼睛看著你，嘴角浮現微笑。 「詹妮弗，」她說。 “你怎麼不工作？還是又在庫房偷懶了？”

 

 你回瞪她，試著抬起頭。說到這裡，她竊笑起來。 「哦，珍。我們不要假裝了。你已經好幾年沒有嚇到我了。現在別假裝我們不知道你是誰。”

 

 內心的決心

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreChangingRoom
passages.content:
Christina motions toward the row of curtained-off changing cubicles, standing just outside with her arms crossed. “Go on, Em,” she instructs, tilting her head toward the nearest one.

 

 You hesitate for only a moment before stepping inside, pulling the curtain closed behind you. A breath of relief escapes as you realize Christina isn’t following. Even as Emily, the thought of undressing in front of her - especially here, in public - would be too much.

 

 Still, the reprieve is short-lived. The curtain rustles, and a sliver of Christina’s face appears at the side, peeking in. “Strip to your underwear,” she orders casually, as if it’s the most natural request in the world.

 

 You blink, caught off guard. “What?”

 

 Christina sighs, rolling her eyes. “Em, I have to see what we’re working with. Clothes should complement your natural shape. Now hurry up.”

 

 There’s no real room to argue. Exhaling slowly, you begin undressing, sliding out of your clothes until you’re left in just your underwear. The room suddenly feels cooler against your skin.

 

 When you mutter, “Done,” Christina doesn’t wait. With a swift motion, she pulls back the curtain, exposing you completely to her scrutinizing gaze. Her eyes flick up and down, appraising, as if she’s evaluating a dress on display. You shift uncomfortably under the intensity of it.

 

 “Like I thought,” she says at last, arms still folded. “You have some natural assets we can work with.”

 

 Before you can even process that, the door to the changing area opens.

 

 Jennifer steps in, holding a neat stack of clothes, and immediately freezes. Her eyes widen at the sight of you in just your underwear. “Oh,” she stammers, quickly averting her gaze. “Sorry. I didn’t mean to --”

 

 Christina dismisses her embarrassment with a wave. “Oh, don’t be such a prude, Jennifer. Just do your job and make sure no one else comes in here while we work. Understand?”

 

 Jennifer looks up at her, grimacing as she bites her tongue.
passages.content_zh:
克里斯蒂娜向一排用窗簾隔開的更衣室示意，她交叉雙臂站在外面。 「繼續，Em，」她一邊指示，一邊把頭轉向最近的一個。

 

 你猶豫了一會兒就走進去，拉上了身後的窗簾。當你意識到克里斯蒂娜沒有跟來時，你鬆了一口氣。即使是艾米麗，一想到在她面前脫衣服——尤其是在這裡，在公共場合——就太過分了。

 

 儘管如此，緩刑是短暫的。窗簾沙沙作響，克里斯蒂娜的一小片臉出現在一邊，向裡面窺視。 「脫掉內衣。」她漫不經心地命令道，彷彿這是世界上最自然的要求。

 

 你眨眼，措手不及。 “什麼？”

 

 克里斯蒂娜嘆了口氣，翻了個白眼。 “嗯，我得看看我們在做什麼。衣服應該與你的自然體形相得益彰。現在快點。”

 

 沒有真正的爭論空間。慢慢地吐氣，你開始脫衣服，脫掉衣服，直到只剩下內衣。房間裡的皮膚突然感覺涼爽。

 

 當你嘀咕「完成」時，克里斯蒂娜不會等待。她迅速拉開窗簾，讓你完全暴露在她審視的目光之下。她的眼睛上下掃動，打量著，就像在評價一件展出的衣服一樣。在它的強度下，你會感到不舒服。

 

 「就像我想的那樣，」她最後說道，雙臂仍然交叉著。 “你們有一些我們可以利用的自然資產。”

 

 你還來不及處理這個問題，更衣區的門就打開了。

 

 詹妮弗走進來，手裡拿著一疊整齊的衣服，立刻就僵住了。當她看到你只穿著內衣時，她的眼睛睜大了。 「哦，」她結結巴巴地說，迅速移開視線。 “抱歉。我不是故意的——”

 

 克里斯蒂娜揮揮手，消除了她的尷尬。 “哦，別這麼正經，詹妮弗。做好你的工作，確保我們工作時沒有其他人進來。明白嗎？”

 

 珍妮佛抬頭看著她，咬著舌頭，做了個鬼臉。

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreJenniferWork
passages.content:
**Evans' Department Store - Staff Room**

 [image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 Your heart thrums with a dull mix of dread and resignation as you push open the “Staff Only” door, stepping into the dimly lit back halls of Evans’. You walk quickly and quietly, certain that somebody is going to call you out for entering a private area, not open to the public.

 

 With some trepidation you move through the endless corridors. You pass a cleaner, polishing the floor. A lone electrician, fiddling with a fuse box. Two other sales assistants that you don’t recognise. 

 

 No one treats you as though you shouldn’t be there. Nobody stops you. Nobody questions you. Instead, they barely acknowledge you.

 

 In the cramped staff room, the bulletin board groans under the weight of today’s roster. Names scrawled in bright marker mock you:
 
 

 “Stock control - Jenna! The early zest gets the dress!” 
 

 A sigh escapes you. In the grand scheme of retail, inventory is the dullest rung on the ladder.

 

 You punch the time clock and steel yourself for a morning of dusty boxes and misplaced price tags. Does Jennifer really do this every day?
 
 
 **Evans' Department Store - Staff Room**

 [image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 You push open the Staff Only door and walk the back corridors of the department store, this time without the anxiety of being an imposter. In some ways the reality is worse. Despite working in the same store, people go out of their way to avoid acknowledging you.

 

 Entering the staff room, you check the schedule. Once again, you’ve been assigned to the stock room in the morning. You take a breath, then turn on your heels and walk out.

 <>

 [image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

passages.content_zh:
**埃文斯百貨公司 - 員工室**

 [圖：./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 當你推開「僅限員工」的門，走進埃文斯光線昏暗的後廳時，你的心充滿了恐懼和無奈的沉悶。你快速而安靜地走著，確信有人會因為你進入不對外開放的私人區域而叫你出來。

 

 你帶著一些恐懼穿過無盡的走廊。你經過一個正在擦地板的清潔工。一名孤獨的電工正在擺弄保險絲盒。另外兩位您不認識的銷售助理。 

 

 沒有人對待你就好像你不應該在那裡一樣。沒有人阻止你。沒有人質疑你。相反，他們幾乎不承認你。

 

 在狹窄的員工室裡，公告牌在今天的名單的重壓下發出呻吟聲。用明亮的記號筆潦草地寫下的名字嘲笑你：
 
 

 “庫存控制 - 珍娜！早起的熱情得到了裙子！”
 

 你嘆了口氣。在零售業的宏偉計劃中，庫存是階梯上最乏味的一級。

 

 你打卡打卡，為一早上的滿是灰塵的盒子和放錯地方的價格標籤做好準備。珍妮佛真的每天都這樣做嗎？
 
 
 **埃文斯百貨公司 - 員工室**

 [圖：./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 你推開員工專用門，走過百貨公司的後走廊，這次，你不用再擔心自己是冒牌貨了。在某些方面，現實情況更糟。儘管在同一家商店工作，人們還是會盡力避免認出你。

 

 進入教職員室，查看行程表。早上，你再次被分配到儲藏室。你吸了一口氣，然後轉身走了出去。

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreJenniferWorkResponses
passages.content:
“You see,” he begins, and you instantly know there’s a wave of bullshit coming your way. “The loading bay has been blocked by someone’s new car. These rich folk, think they own this town, don’t they? I can’t get my van in there.” He looks down at your body once again. “It’s too tight to fit in.”

 

 You fold your arms. “Then park on the double yellows and deliver,” you fire back.

 

 Martin’s lips twitch into a grin. He steps forward and you find yourself backing up, brushing against the unopened crate next to the door. “And risk a fine?” he says. “Fifty quid for a few boxes of ladies’ underwear? Not worth it.” His voice dips low, rough as gravel. “Council don’t care if I’m late, but they love writing tickets.”

 

 You let out a huff. “That’s not my problem. You’re paid to deliver.”

 

 He straightens, expression twisting into something almost like pity. “Funny,” he says, voice thick with mock regret, “I heard about you.” He leans in, boots scraping the concrete as he shifts his weight. “Big fall from grace, isn’t it?”

 

 His hand drifts to the crate behind you, pressing just enough to pin you there. “Makes me wonder,” he murmurs, eyes dark. “How far you’ll go to stay on top… even just in this shop?”

 

 **Actions**

 
 
 [The Appeaser] <>

 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 [The Appeaser] <> - You can't seem to stand up for yourself

 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 You can’t help it. Before you even think, the words spill out of your mouth. “Alright, Martin. What is it you want?”

 

 His grin turns predatory. “Something I’ve been hoping for all morning. Just a kiss.” 

 

 He looks at you. You feel like you don’t have a choice. Not with him. You can only nod.

 

 Before you can react, he sweeps you up on the crate. Your skirt rides up as he settles you down, his eyes now on the same level as yours. He pushes forward, parting your legs, skirt now up around your waist.

 

 He leans in close, voice low. “Just a little one. I’m sure you’ll enjoy it.”

 

 His lips crash against you
passages.content_zh:
「你看，」他開始說道，你立刻就知道有一波廢話正向你襲來。 「裝貨區被某人的新車擋住了。這些有錢人以為他們擁有這個小鎮，不是嗎？我的貨車開不進去。」他再次低頭看著你的身體。 “太緊了，穿不進去。”

 

 你交叉雙臂。 「然後停在雙黃燈上並交付，」你反擊道。

 

 馬丁的嘴唇抽動著，露出笑容。他向前走去，你發現自己向後退了一步，擦著門旁邊未打開的板條箱。 「還有被罰款的風險嗎？」他說。 「幾盒女士內衣五十英鎊？不值得。」他的聲音低沉，沙啞如碎石。 “市議會不在乎我是否遲到，但他們喜歡開罰單。”

 

 你嘆了口氣。 “這不是我的問題。你是付費來送貨的。”

 

 他挺直了身子，表情扭曲得幾乎像是憐憫。 「有趣，」他說道，聲音裡充滿了假裝遺憾，「我聽說過你。」他傾身向前，轉移重心時靴子刮擦著水泥地面。 “失寵了，不是嗎？”

 

 他的手伸向你身後的板條箱，壓力剛好足以將你固定在那裡。 「讓我感到奇怪，」他低聲說道，眼睛漆黑。 “即使只是在這家店裡，你會走多遠才能保持領先地位？”

 

 **行動**

 
 
 [綏靖者]<>

 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 [綏靖者] <> - 你似乎無法為自己挺身而出

 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 你無能為力。還沒等你思考，這些話就從你嘴裡脫口而出。 “好吧，馬丁。你想要什麼？”

 

 他的笑容變得充滿掠奪性。 “我整個早上都在期待的事情。只是一個吻。”

 

 他看著你。你覺得你別無選擇。不和他在一起。你只能點頭。

 

 還沒等你反應過來，他就把你掃到板條箱上了。當他讓你坐下時，你的裙子捲起來，他的眼睛現在和你的在同一水平線上。他向前推，分開你的雙腿，裙子現在繞在你的腰上。

 

 他靠得很近，聲音低沉。 “就一點點。我相信你會喜歡的。”

 

 他的嘴唇撞向你

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreReception
passages.content:
**Evans' Department Store**

[image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Reception.png]

A wood-paneled reception dominated by a lone desk, an old rotary telephone, and a frosted door with Terry Evans' name stenciled into it. The muted decor radiates sterile authority and quiet isolation.

 You slip away from the sales floor, heart thudding, and quickly ascend the narrow staircase to the upper level. The air grows still and hushed. Gone are the bright rails and glass displays, replaced by warm wood paneling, muted green accents, and quiet overhead lights that lend the reception a calm, somber precision.

 

 A lone wooden desk stands beneath a wall clock, an old rotary phone perched at its corner; framed abstract prints hang on the walls, their muted tones echoing the soft carpet underfoot.

 

 Behind the desk sits Mary Handle. A woman in her forties with motherly poise. Her blouse is crisply ironed, her hair pulled into a tidy chignon, and though laugh lines crease the corners of her eyes, she wears them like a badge of experience. When she looks up, her gaze is both welcoming and perceptive, as if she’s seen every kind of face pass through these doors.

 

 She looks up from the shift roster, offering you a warm, practised smile. “Jennifer,” she says, voice soft and steady. “Everything all right? Need to see Mr. Evans? He’s always happy to make time for his girls.” 

 

 You step forward, your heart ticking like the clock on the wall. You know Mary is Terrence’s mother and that the note you found at Jennifer’s apartment had her phone number on. You need to find out what it means, and you bet that Mary can help you make sense of it.
 
 
 Mary continues to patiently wait for you to talk. A soft creak from inside Terry’s office makes her jump. The pulse in your throat quickens.
 
 
 Mary waits for you to speak. Through the frosted door you hear heavy footsteps, followed by a growl of curses and muttered profanity that rattles the wood. Mary’s polite smile falters for th
passages.content_zh:
**埃文斯百貨商店**

[圖片：./images/Evans_Department_Store_Reception.png]

木板鑲板的接待處以一張孤零零的桌子、一部舊的旋轉電話和一扇磨砂門為主，上面刻著特里·埃文斯的名字。柔和的裝飾散發出無菌的權威和安靜的隔離。

 你從銷售樓層溜走，心臟狂跳，然後迅速登上狹窄的樓梯到上層。空氣變得寂靜無聲。明亮的欄桿和玻璃顯示器消失了，取而代之的是溫暖的木鑲板、柔和的綠色裝飾和安靜的頭頂燈，為接待處帶來了一種平靜、陰沉的精確感。

 

 掛鐘下方立著一張孤零零的木桌，角落放著一部舊的旋轉電話。牆上掛著裱框的抽象版畫，其柔和的色調與腳下柔軟的地毯相呼應。

 

 桌子後面坐著瑪麗·漢德爾。四十多歲的女人，帶著慈母般的泰然自若。她的襯衫熨得乾乾淨淨，頭髮梳成整齊的髮髻，雖然眼角有笑紋，但她戴著它們就像是經驗的徽章。當她抬起頭時，她的目光既熱情又敏銳，彷彿她看到了各種各樣的面孔穿過這些門。

 

 她從輪班名單上抬起頭來，給你一個溫暖、熟練的微笑。 「詹妮弗，」她說道，聲音輕柔而穩定。 “一切都好嗎？需要見埃文斯先生嗎？他總是很樂意為他的女兒們騰出時間。”

 

 你向前踏了一步，你的心像牆上的時鐘一樣滴答作響。你知道瑪麗是泰倫斯的母親，你在珍妮佛公寓發現的紙條上有她的電話號碼。你需要找出它的含義，並且你打賭瑪麗可以幫助你理解它。
 
 
 瑪麗繼續耐心地等待你說話。特里辦公室傳來輕微的吱吱聲，讓她嚇了一跳。你喉嚨裡的脈搏加快了。
 
 
 瑪麗等你說話。透過磨砂的門，你可以聽到沉重的腳步聲，接著是咒罵的咆哮聲和低聲髒話，讓木頭嘎嘎作響。瑪麗禮貌的笑容因這個原因而顫抖

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreSleepwear
passages.content:
Almost like a dream, you wander through the empty store, finding yourself quickly standing in the sleepwear section. Your mind races. Too often you've seen things in this section where your mind has jumped to what Jennifer would like wearing it. Now, the thought process is the same. But in the image, you’re Jennifer. And Jennifer is you.

 

 Do you want to appear sexy for your man? the snide voice that lives in your head snaps at you. Imagining what he’ll say when he sees you.

 

 You bite your lip. Your eye wanders to a rack of silk nightwear. You’ve seen Jen in a similar one before, but these seem like a different design. The hemline short, the top more open. Backless, tied together by lace trim.

 

 Something in you stirs. A picture of what it would be like to wear it. You can’t escape it. Looking at it is turning you on.

 

 You pick up a couple, and mull over what colour to buy.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 
 You pick out the pink colour. A small giggle escapes as you run it through the till, feeling the silk material in your fingers. You picture yourself in it. Standing in Jennifer’s bedroom. Looking cute. Girly. Feminine.

 

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

 

 **Actions**

 You pick out the red colour. A sultry laugh escapes as you run it through the till, feeling the silk material in your fingers. You picture yourself in it. Standing in Jennifer’s bedroom. Looking alluring. Sexy. Hot.

 

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreShopWorkout
passages.content:
The male selection is sparse but straightforward. One rack of folded shorts and T-shirts in muted greys and dark blues. No labels boast of trend or appeal. Every one lists out the benefits to exercise. Moisture-wicking, stretch-fit, durable.

 

 You find a pair of shorts in your size, along with a workout shirt. The fabric is light but tough, designed to move with you and soak up sweat without complaint. There’s nothing flashy about any of it. No mesh panels, no logos, no tailoring to accentuate anything.

 

 It’s not meant to show you off. It’s meant to let you workout. As you check the fit, you feel readiness. These clothes are for testing yourself. Pushing yourself. Winning.

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 You look around Evans’ Department Store for any sign of workout clothes. After some searching, you notice a sign pointing to “Activewear.”

 

 The first thing you notice is how small everything is. Cropped tops, high-cut leggings, sheer mesh panels. The whole collection seems less designed for exercise and more for visibility. You scan the tags. Nothing here promises support or durability. But they all will bring attention.
 
 
You aren’t sure how you feel about wearing anything here, especially as Emily. Even if you only wear it whilst working out, people will still see. The thought of what you will look like as they watch unnerves you.

 

 After flicking through racks, you realise there are realistically only three options available to you. On-brand for Evans, the options are distinguished by colour - Red, Black and Pink - but more than the colours, the trims and design on each couldn’t be more different.

 

 The red set is made of material that is slick, tight, and unmistakably meant to be looked at. The top has a deep scoop neck, and the leggings cut high across the hips. Wearing this would make your body impossible to ignore. When you fold it over your arm, you can’t help but think about being seen in it. And why it feels exciting to imagine it.

 

 In contrast
passages.content_zh:
男性的選擇很少但很簡單。一架折疊短褲和 T 卹，顏色為柔和的灰色和深藍色。沒有任何品牌能夠誇耀潮流或吸引力。每個人都列出了運動的好處。吸濕排汗、彈性貼合、耐用。

 

 您會找到一條適合您尺寸的短褲和一件運動衫。面料輕盈但堅韌，旨在隨您移動並毫無怨言地吸收汗水。其中沒有任何華而不實的東西。沒有網布面板，沒有標誌，沒有剪裁來強調任何東西。

 

 這並不是為了向你炫耀。它的目的是讓你鍛鍊。當您檢查合身性時，您會感到準備就緒。這些衣服是用來測試你自己的。逼迫自己。獲勝。

 

 行動

 <>
 
 你環顧埃文斯百貨公司，尋找任何運動服的跡象。經過一番搜尋後，您會注意到一個指向「運動服」的標誌。

 

 你注意到的第一件事是一切都很小。短上衣、高剪裁緊身褲、透明網布鑲片。整個系列似乎不是為了鍛鍊而設計的，而是為了可見性而設計的。您掃描標籤。這裡沒有任何內容承諾支援或耐用性。但它們都會引起關注。
 
 
你不確定在這裡穿任何東西感覺如何，尤其是作為艾米麗。即使您只在鍛煉時佩戴它，人們仍然會看到。一想到他們看到的你會是什麼樣子，你就會感到不安。

 

 瀏覽完貨架後，您意識到實際上只有三種選擇可供您選擇。在 Evans 品牌上，這些選項按顏色區分——紅色、黑色和粉紅色——但除了顏色之外，每款的裝飾和設計都截然不同。

 

 紅色套裝由光滑、緊密且明顯值得一看的材料製成。上衣採用深湯匙領設計，緊身褲採用高腰設計。穿上它會讓你的身體無法被忽視。當你把它疊在手臂上時，你會不由自主地想到被看見。以及為什麼想像它會讓人感到興奮。

 

 相比之下

passages.content:
“Oh hi Annie! How’s it going?” Florence smiles broadly as you approach the counter.

 

 You chatter and gossip with Florence for a few minutes, but she tells you nothing of note. Eventually, you tell her you have to leave. She gives you a pleasant smile and tells you to come see her soon for a catch-up.

 
 “Hey Andrea,” Florence greets you as you approach. “How are you? How’s work?” There is a genuine hint of concern in Florence’s voice, as she looks at you with a serious expression.

 
 “Hey Jen, what brings you here?” Florence says warmly. 

 

 You chat with Florence for a while. She asks you a number of questions about the latest ranges that are coming to Evans’ Department Store. You definitely don’t have much in the way of details, so you make a sharp exit when the conversation draws to a close.
 
 
 “Oh hi Emily!” Florence greets you as you approach the counter. “What brings you do Fresh Pot? Got a date?”

 

 With nothing specific to ask Florence as Emily, you make small talk for a few minutes, then politely say goodbye.

 
 
 "Oh, hello ", Florence deadpans. "How can I help you today?"

 

 The sarcasm hangs on every one of her words.

 
 “Morning !” Florence says warmly as you step up to the counter. “Your usual?”

 

 
 You nod. She picks down her paperback book, spine turned downward, and starts preparing your coffee.

 You nod. She stops wiping down the counter and starts preparing your coffee.

 
 

 Florence waits patiently for you to ask her a question.

passages.name: FreshPotDateDennis
passages.content:
You walk over to Dennis, hearing your heels clicking on the ceramic as you approach. He glances up and double-takes, his mouth agape as he looks you up and down. You can’t help but smile at his reaction.

 

 “My gosh, Emily,” he says. “You look…”

 

 He trails off. You realise he’s not going to complete the sentence. “Thank you,” you say to him. 

 

 You sit down in the booth opposite him. You can’t help but let out a giggle as he continues to stare at you. You sense some butterflies in your stomach in reaction to Dennis’ clear desire for you. Why do I feel flattered that a boy is lusting after me?

 
 You walk towards Dennis, smiling as he notices you. His eyebrows rise a little and you see a big grin spreading across his face as he looks you up and down.

 

 “You look nice,” he stammers out. “Thanks for coming, Em. I wasn’t sure if you would show up!”

 

 You slide into the booth on the opposite side to him. His eyes glance over your body before he realises what he is doing and looks back up at you. “Sorry.”

 

 “That’s alright,” you say quietly, unsure how to feel that a boy is checking you out. Especially as you did go to a bit of effort. 

 
 Dennis notices you as you approach. He gives you a look, then smiles politely. He is wearing a neatly pressed shirt and some awkward looking formal trousers. You can tell he’s disappointed at your lack of effort.

 

 “Hi Em. It’s nice to see you,” he mumbles.

 
 Dennis almost doesn’t notice you as you approach. When he sees you, he looks at what you are wearing and how little effort you’ve put into preparing. He himself is wearing a freshly pressed shirt and some formal trousers that look brand new. Disappointed, he looks down at the table.

 

 “Hi Em,” he mutters, not meeting your eye.

 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

 Flo comes over and the two of you order some drinks and food. Dennis tries to make small talk, but it quickly peters out. He looks flustered and nervous. You consider asking him some questions, but you fee
passages.content_zh:
你走向丹尼斯，靠近時聽到腳跟踩在陶瓷上的咔噠聲。他抬起頭，看了兩眼，張大了嘴，上下打量著你。看到他的反應你會情不自禁地微笑。

 

 “天哪，艾米麗，”他說。 “你看起來…”

 

 他的聲音漸漸減弱。你意識到他不會完成這句話。 「謝謝你，」你對他說。 

 

 你在他對面的包廂坐下。當他繼續盯著你看時，你忍不住咯咯笑起來。由於丹尼斯對你的明確渴望，你感到心裡有些緊張。為什麼我會因為一個男孩對我有慾望而感到受寵若驚？

 
 你走向丹尼斯，當他注意到你時微笑著。他的眉毛微微揚起，你會看到他臉上露出燦爛的笑容，上下打量著你。

 

 「你看起來不錯，」他結結巴巴地說。 “謝謝你來，Em。我不確定你是否會出現！”

 

 你滑進他對面的隔間。他的眼睛掃過你的身體，然後才意識到自己在做什麼，然後回頭看著你。 “對不起。”

 

 「沒關係，」你輕聲說，不確定如何感覺一個男孩在打量你。尤其是當你確實付出了一些努力的時候。 

 
 當你靠近時，丹尼斯注意到了你。他看了你一眼，然後禮貌地微笑。他穿著一件熨燙整齊的襯衫和一條看起來很彆扭的正式褲子。你可以看出他對你的不努力感到失望。

 

 「嗨，Em。很高興見到你，」他咕噥道。

 
 當你靠近時，丹尼斯幾乎沒有註意到你。當他看到你時，他會看你穿的衣服以及你在準備方面付出的努力有多麼少。他本人穿著一件新熨燙過的襯衫和一條看起來嶄新的正裝褲子。他失望地低頭看著桌子。

 

 「嗨，Em，」他低聲說道，沒有看你的眼睛。

 <>

 弗洛過來，你們兩個點了一些飲料和食物。丹尼斯試著閒聊，但很快就消失了。他看起來很慌亂、緊張。你考慮問他一些問題，但你付費

passages.name: FreshPotTransformFlo
passages.content:
You feel the amulet tingle underneath your clothes and let your hand move to touch Flo’s paperback. *Here we go*, you think.

 

 Flo frowns at you as you stare at her. You look her up and down, taking in her flame-red hair, her ruby red lipstick and her plucked eyebrows. You form a picture of her in your mind, dressed in her waitress uniform, her warm smile beaming. 

 

 “You okay, ?” you hear her say. 

 

 “From this item, I see you in your time of need. Now form shape to the person I see within.”

 

 The amulet burns against your chest and you feel the sensation of magical energy leaping across your body. You scream as you feel the waves rippling out from the amulet. Your scalp feels like it’s on fire and your body starts to crunch as changes sweep across it.

 

 
 Now familiar sensations register inside your brain. Your chest changes. Arms morph. Bones inside your body crunching. Your womb burns as it shifts and changes.
 
 
 Now familiar sensations register inside your brain. Chest expanding. Arms shrinking. Bones inside your body crunching. Penis burning as it shrivels back inside your body.
 
 
 

 And with a final shriek, the pain subsides. You look up, seeing Florence staring back at you in abject horror. A strand of flame-red hair falls across your eye. 

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You prepare for the change, but something feels off. The amulet's energy seems drained. 

 

 You curse inwardly, realising the amulet needs more time to re-charge.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
passages.content_zh:
你感覺到護身符在你的衣服下面刺痛，並讓你的手觸摸弗洛的平裝書。 *我們開始*，你想。

 

 當你盯著她看時，弗洛對你皺眉。你上下打量她，欣賞她火紅的頭髮、紅寶石色的口紅和拔掉的眉毛。你的腦海裡浮現出她穿著服務生製服、笑容燦爛的樣子。 

 

 「你還好嗎？」你聽到她說。 

 

 “通過這個物品，我在你需要的時候看到了你。現在為我在裡面看到的人塑造形狀。”

 

 護身符在你的胸口燃燒，你感覺到魔法能量在你的身體上跳躍。當你感受到護身符中蕩漾出的波浪時，你尖叫起來。你的頭皮感覺就像著火了，隨著變化席捲你的身體，你的身體開始嘎吱作響。

 

 
 現在，熟悉的感覺出現在你的大腦中。你的胸部發生變化。手臂變形。體內的骨頭嘎吱作響。當你的子宮移動和變化時，會感到灼熱感。
 
 
 現在，熟悉的感覺出現在你的大腦中。胸部擴張。手臂萎縮。體內的骨頭嘎吱作響。陰莖在收縮回到體內時會感到灼熱。
 
 
 

 隨著最後一聲尖叫，疼痛消失了。你抬起頭，發現佛羅倫斯正用極度恐懼的眼神看著你。一縷火紅色的頭髮垂落在你的眼前。 

 

 **行動**

 <>

 你準備好迎接改變，但感覺有些不對勁。護身符的能量似乎已經耗盡。 

 

 你心裡咒罵著，意識到護身符需要更多時間來充電。

 

 **行動**

 <>

passages.name: FreshPotTransformFlo2
passages.content:
“Fall asleep,” you say quickly, “and let your memories drift as you slumber.”

 

 Florence stares at you for just a second, then drops behind the counter. You round the counter and see her passed out on the floor. Once you are satisfied she’s taken no injuries from her sudden fall, you look up in the mirrored glass behind the counter to see your reflection.

 

 The perfectly made-up face of Florence looks back at you. Her bushy flame-red hair. Her luscious red lips. Her finely plucked eyebrows.

 

 You become aware of the waitress uniform you are wearing. The hem of her skirt brushing against the bottom of your bum. Her blue blouse tight, hugging your curves. Breasts held firmly in place by a bra. The squeeze of her feet in the glossy red heels.

 

 You smile to yourself, feeling giddy. Your brain seems foggy and for a moment you completely forget why you are holding a paperback book. You feel your *intelligence* has been impacted by your most recent change.

 

 You take a moment more to acknowledge your new form before turning your attention to the pressing matter of the unconscious Florence at your feet.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 
 You grab Florence with both your arms and drag her through the kitchen to the back room. The lack of strength in your new form is noticeable, whilst her heels are higher than you're used to. A few times you are perilously close to losing your balance. You shudder to think what would happen if you fell and were knocked out.

 
 You grab Florence with both your arms and drag her through the kitchen to the back room. The lack of strength in your new form is noticeable, as is moving backwards in high heels. A few times you are perilously close to losing your balance. You shudder to think what would happen if you fell and were knocked out.

 
 

 The back room is little bigger than a closet, barely able to contain the small desk covered in paperwork. With little choice, you prop Florence up in the desk chair, trying to make her as comfort
passages.content_zh:
“睡吧，”你很快地说，“让你的记忆在睡梦中飘散。”

 

 弗洛伦斯盯着你看了一会儿，然后就走到柜台后面。你繞過櫃檯，看到她昏倒在地板上。当你确信她没有因突然摔倒而受伤时，你抬头看柜台后面的镜子，看到自己的倒影。

 

 佛罗伦萨那张化着完美妆容的脸回望着你。她濃密的火紅色頭髮。她那性感的紅唇。她的眉毛修得很精緻。

 

 您開始意識到自己穿著的女服務生製服。她的裙子下擺擦著你的屁股。她的藍色襯衫緊身，擁抱你的曲線。乳房被胸罩牢牢固定在適當的位置。她的腳踩在閃亮的紅色高跟鞋裡。

 

 你對自己微笑，感到頭暈。你的大脑似乎一片模糊，一瞬间你完全忘记了为什么拿着一本平装书。您感觉您的“智力”受到了最近的变化的影响。

 

 你又花了一点时间来确认自己的新形态，然后将注意力转向脚下昏迷不醒的佛罗伦萨的紧迫问题。

 <>

 
 你用双臂抓住弗洛伦斯，把她拖过厨房，拖到后面的房间。你的新形态明显缺乏力量，而她的高跟鞋比你习惯的要高。有幾次你瀕臨失去平衡的危險。想象一下如果你摔倒并被击倒会发生什么，你会感到不寒而栗。

passages.name: StaceyBritneyFreshPot
passages.content:
You quietly slide into the booth behind Stacey and Britney in an attempt to listen to their conversation, but as soon as you sit down they fall silent and shoot you a look. It appears that if you want to listen into their conversation, you’ll need to be less conspicuous.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 Britney and Stacey see you approach. Stacey is cautious, but Britney smiles. She welcomes you to sit down with them.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 Stacey and Britney sit tucked into their usual corner booth, half-finished frappes sweating on the table. You move past them slowly, clearing cups, cloth in hand. They don’t notice. Or don’t care.

 

 “I swear he’s doing it on purpose,” Stacey mutters, arms folded tight. “He’s shutting down again.”

 

 Britney sighs, resting her chin on her palm. “He was doing so well. Then bam! Walls up. He’s not even looking at me anymore. And don’t say it’s nerves. I couldn't have been more flirty.”

 

 "How's that different to usual?” Stacey quips. Britney scowls at her.

 

 “Whatever," she replies. "Chrissie’s not happy. That's what matters. She said that if I couldn't bring a boy around, what value did I have?"

 

 Stacey grimaces. "It's got something to do with that book. I know it."

 

 "That biology one?" Britney bites her lip. "You think he’s choosing diagrams over me?"

 

 "No doofus," Stacey snaps. "I’ve seen him reading it during lunch. It's full of notes. He's been different since he's been reading it."

 
 
 Britney sighs. “So why don't we pick his locker? He doesn't take it everywhere.”

 

 "You know how to pick a locker?" Stacey says, raising her eyebrows.

 

 "People do it on TV all the time!" Britney bites back.

 

 "Well, if you manage to learn how to use a hairpin to pick a lock, let me know," Stacey says, rolling her eyes.

 

 "We could... ask that janitor for a key?" Britney offers.

 

 Stacey gives her a dark look. "I am not going back to that basement. Not after last time.”

 

 Britney exhales, frustrated. “We’
passages.content_zh:
你悄悄地溜進史黛西和布蘭妮身後的隔間，試著聽聽他們的談話，但你一坐下，他們就沉默了，並向你看了一眼。看來，如果你想聽他們的談話，你需要不那麼引人注目。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 布蘭妮和史黛西看到你走近。史黛西很謹慎，但布蘭妮微笑著。她歡迎您與他們坐下來。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 史黛西和布蘭妮坐在他們慣用的角落攤位裡，半成品冰沙在桌上流汗。你慢慢地從他們身邊走過，清理杯子，手裡拿著布。 They don’t notice. Or don’t care.

 

 「我發誓他是故意的，」史黛西雙手抱緊，低聲說。 “他又關門了。”

 

 布蘭妮嘆了口氣，把下巴放在手掌上。 “他做得很好。然後砰！堵住了。他甚至不再看我了。別說這是緊張。我真是太調情了。”

 

 「這和平時有什麼不同嗎？」Stacey quips. Britney scowls at her.

 

 「無論如何，」她回答。 “克麗絲不高興。這才是最重要的。她說如果我不能帶一個男孩過來，我還有什麼價值呢？”

 

 Stacey grimaces. “這和那本書有關。我知道。”

 

 "That biology one?" Britney bites her lip. “你認為他會選擇圖表而不是我？”

 

 「別傻了，」史黛西厲聲說。 “我見過他在午餐時讀這本書。裡面充滿了筆記。自從讀了這本書以來，他就變得不一樣了。”

 
 
 Britney sighs. “那我們為什麼不打開他的儲物櫃呢？他不會把它帶到任何地方。”

 

 「你知道怎麼選置物櫃嗎？」史黛西揚起眉毛說。

 

 「人們總是在電視上這樣做！」 Britney bites back.

 

 「好吧，如果你學會如何使用髮夾開鎖，請告訴我，」史黛西翻白眼說。

 

 「我們可以…向那個看門人要鑰匙嗎？」 Britney offers.

 

 史黛西陰沉地看了她一眼。 “我不會再回到那個地下室了。自從上次之後就不會了。”

passages.name: FreshPotFloWork
passages.content:
The morning shift at Fresh Pot is quiet. The windows are still misted from the early air, and the soft rattle of the espresso machine hums beneath the low murmur of conversation. You glance around the café and sigh, stepping into Florence’s routine. Wiping down surfaces. Replacing bakery stock. Folding napkins just so. You try to mimic the easy efficiency you've seen from her on your rare visits.

 

 Then the door creaks open… and Andrea walks in. You almost don’t recognise her at first.

 

 Gone is the lacy French Maid uniform that Charlton Hotel insists on. Instead, she’s wearing a simple cardigan and jeans. Her hair tied back loosely. It's the first time you’ve seen her dressed out of costume. For a moment, that feels more disorienting than the uniform ever did.

 

 She gives you a glance - soft, tired - and heads straight for one of the booths near the back.

 

 You wipe your hands, glance toward the empty counter, and cross the floor. “Hey,” you say gently. “You alright?”

 

 Andrea shrugs. She tries to play it off with a small exhale through her nose, but the slump in her shoulders tells you everything. “Didn’t sleep,” she mutters. “Lennox kept me late. Had me redoing the same load of linen three times. Then he asked if I’d ‘ironed the bows’ on my apron before I left.”

 

 You raise an eyebrow. She gives a hollow smirk. “I didn’t know I was supposed to. Guess I’ll add that to the list.”

 

 You sit across from her as she stares at the table. “And Sermon... he’s been watching me more. Following me with his eyes.”

 

 There’s a pause. She doesn’t look up. “He used to do the same with Amber. Before she quit. And we all know where she ended up.”

 

 Your stomach twists. You’re not sure who Amber is, or where she ended up. But you bet it isn’t good.

 

 The bell over the door rings again. Customers. The line at the counter is starting to build. You know you won’t earn tips unless you’re behind it, smiling sweetly and handing over cappuccinos with perfect
passages.content_zh:
鮮鍋的早班很安靜。窗戶上還殘留著清晨空氣中的霧氣，在低沉的談話聲中，濃縮咖啡機發出輕柔的嘎嘎聲。你環顧咖啡館，嘆了口氣，步入了佛羅倫斯的日常生活。擦拭表面。更換烘焙原料。折疊餐巾就這樣。你試圖模仿她在你罕見的拜訪中看到的輕鬆和高效。

 

 然後門吱吱地開了……安德里亞走了進來。一開始你幾乎認不出她。

 

 查爾頓酒店堅持的帶花邊的法國女僕制服已經一去不復返了。相反，她穿著簡單的開襟衫和牛仔褲。她的頭髮鬆散地綁在腦後。這是你第一次看到她脫裝打扮。有一瞬間，這感覺比制服更讓人迷失方向。

 

 她看了你一眼——軟弱、疲憊——然後直奔靠近後面的攤位。

 

 你擦了擦手，看了一眼空蕩蕩的櫃檯，然後穿過地板。 「嘿，」你輕輕地說。 “你還好吧？”

 

 安德里亞聳聳肩。她試圖用鼻子輕輕呼氣來彌補這一點，但她肩膀的塌陷告訴了你一切。 「沒睡。」她嘟咕噥道。 「倫諾克斯讓我遲到了。讓我把同樣的亞麻布重做了三遍。然後他問我在離開之前是否‘熨平了圍裙上的蝴蝶結’。”

 

 你揚起一邊眉毛。她空洞地笑了笑。 “我不知道我應該這樣做。我想我會把它添加到列表中。”

 

 你坐在她對面，她盯著桌子。 “還有佈道……他一直在觀察我。他的眼睛跟著我。”

 

 有一個停頓。她沒有抬頭。 “他曾經對安布爾做過同樣的事。在她辭職之前。我們都知道她最後去了哪裡。”

 

 你的胃在扭曲。你不確定安柏是誰，也不知道她去了哪裡。但你敢打賭這並不好。

 

 門上的門鈴又響了。顧客。櫃檯前開始排隊。你知道你不會獲得小費，除非你在背後微笑著，完美地遞上卡布奇諾。

passages.name: FreshPotWorkResponses
passages.content:
You glance back at the growing line of customers… then sit.

 

 Andrea doesn’t speak right away. She just lets out a breath and leans her head in her hand, fingers rubbing at her temple. “I know I need the job,” she murmurs eventually. “It’s not that I’m… ungrateful. It’s just…”

 
 
 She trails off, staring at the tabletop like it might have the answers.

 

 “I keep thinking about us. Back at school. You remember? Always thinking things would work out. The teachers were full-on. Sure. But I thought that was the worst of it. Turns out the real world’s just… slower to understand. But meaner. More humiliating. Is this really all there was waiting for us on the other side?”

 

 Your eyes flick towards the counter. The queue is creeping towards the door. A number of impatient customers give you dirty looks. But you turn back to the young woman opposite you. She exhales again, steadier now.

 

 “Sorry. Didn’t mean to dump all that on you. I just needed to sit for a second.”

 

 Before you can respond, a voice snaps from the counter. “What do you have to do to get a coffee around here?”

 

 Andrea rises, gathering her bag. “I should go. Thanks for listening, Flo.”

 

 She slips out the door. Shoulders still heavy, but maybe a little less.

 

 You turn, apologising with a smile as you move around the counter to serve the irritated customers. They barely acknowledge your efforts and tips are not forthcoming. But you feel that, at least, you’ve made Andrea’s day a little better.

 
 You sit beside her, trying to ignore the queue forming. She tells you a little more about her evening. About Lennox. And Pierre. On the surface, she is doing what you’d expect from a maid. Cleaning. Washing. Helping out in the kitchen. But the tone from the rest of the staff. You can’t imagine it is easy at all.

 

 A number of hisses and tuts come from the side, as customers increasingly look at you expecting services. Andrea smiles sweetly, but doesn’t talk much more. When she gets up,
passages.content_zh:
你回頭看了一眼越來越多的顧客……然後坐下來。

 

 安德里亞沒有立即說話。她只是呼出一口氣，把頭靠在手裡，手指揉著太陽穴。 「我知道我需要這份工作，」她最終低聲說道。 “不是我……忘恩負義。只是……”

 
 
 她的聲音逐漸減弱，盯著桌面，彷彿上面可能有答案。

 

 「我一直在想著我們。回到學校的時候。你還記得嗎？總是想著事情會成功。老師們都在全力以赴。當然。但我認為那是最糟糕的。事實證明，現實世界只是……理解起來較慢。但更刻薄。更羞辱。這真的是在另一邊等待我們的一切嗎？”

 

 你的目光掃向櫃檯。隊伍正慢慢向門口移動。許多不耐煩的顧客都會用鄙視的眼光看你。但你轉身面對對面的年輕女子。她再次呼出一口氣，現在更穩定了。

 

 “抱歉。我不是故意把這些都丟給你。我只是想坐一會兒。”

 

 還沒等你反應過來，櫃檯突然響起了聲音。 “你需要做什麼才能在這附近喝到咖啡？”

 

 安德里亞站起身來，收拾起她的包包。 “我該走了。謝謝你的聆聽，弗洛。”

 

 她溜出了門。肩膀仍然沉重，但也許減輕了一些。

 

 你轉身微笑著道歉，並在櫃檯周圍走動，為惱怒的顧客提供服務。他們幾乎不承認你的努力，也不會提供建議。但你覺得，至少，你讓安德里亞的日子過得更好了一些。

 
 你坐在她旁邊，試著忽略排隊的隊伍。她會告訴你更多關於她晚上的事情。關於倫諾克斯。還有皮埃爾。從表面上看，她正在做女僕的事情。打掃。洗滌。在廚房幫忙。但其他工作人員的語氣卻是。你根本無法想像這有多容易。

 

 隨著顧客越來越多地看著你期待服務，從側面傳來許多嘶嘶聲和嘖嘖聲。安德莉亞笑得很甜，但沒有多說什麼。當她起身時，

passages.name: FreshPotWorkEvents
passages.content:
With a cursory glance around the cafe, you sigh and get to work. You spend your time preparing take-away food and wiping down counters, trying to mimic some of the things you’ve seen Flo do when you’ve swung by for coffee.

 

 
 Walking around the cafe, you adjust to working in such high heels. On more than one occasion you almost slip, but thankfully there’s always a table or countertop for you to grasp on to. It never ceases to make customers chuckle when you stumble. 

 
 Walking around the cafe, you slowly adjust to working in heels. On more than one occasion you almost slip, but thankfully there’s always a table or countertop for you to grasp on to. It never ceases to make customers chuckle when you stumble. 

 
 

 In fact, there’s a lot that you do that make customers react. Without exception everyone treats you as an old friend. You find yourself returning their smiles and laughing at their jokes. Especially when you receive tips for your efforts.

 

 It does not go unnoticed how men and women treat you differently. Women are polite and chatty, but the men in their different ways are flirtatious and voyeuristic. You feel their eyes on your legs as you saunter around the coffee shop. You see them staring at your breasts as you lean over to serve them. 

 

 One particularly flirty customer is Dan, from Thompson’s Repairs. You inwardly sigh when you see him walk in. He smiles like a tiger when he sees you and doesn’t take his eyes off you as you prepare a coffee for him.

 

 “Damn fine cup of coffee” he says as you bring a coffee to his booth. As he reaches into his wallet to pay, you catch him looking you up and down. “Although with legs like those, I’d drink anything you served me.”

 

 You notice he is taking his time rifling through notes, counting out how much he’s going to pay you.

 

 **Actions**

你粗略地掃視了一下咖啡館，嘆了口氣，開始工作。你花時間準備外帶食品和擦拭櫃檯，試圖模仿你經過喝咖啡時弗洛所做的一些事情。

 

 
 在咖啡館裡走來走去，你就適應了穿著這樣的高跟鞋工作。不只一次，您差點滑倒，但幸運的是，總有一張桌子或檯面可供您抓住。當你絆倒時，它永遠不會停止讓顧客發笑。 

 
 在咖啡館裡走來走去，你慢慢適應了穿高跟鞋工作。不只一次，您差點滑倒，但幸運的是，總有一張桌子或檯面可供您抓住。當你絆倒時，它永遠不會停止讓顧客發笑。 

 
 

 事實上，您所做的許多事情都會引起客戶的反應。無一例外，每個人都把你當老朋友。你發現自己回應了他們的微笑，並為他們的笑話大笑。尤其是當您收到有關您努力的提示時。

 

 人們不會忽視男人和女人對待你的不同方式。女人彬彬有禮，健談，但男人卻以不同的方式調情和偷窺。當你在咖啡店裡閒逛時，你會感覺到他們的目光落在你的腿上。當你俯身為他們服務時，你會看到他們盯著你的胸部。 

 

 來自湯普森維修店的丹是一位特別性感的顧客。當你看到他走進來時，你在心裡嘆息。他一看到你就笑得像老虎，當你為他準備咖啡時，他的目光沒有離開你。

 

 當你端一杯咖啡到他的攤位時，他說：「這杯咖啡真是太棒了。」當他伸手錢包付錢時，你發現他上下打量著你。 “雖然有這樣的腿，但我會喝你給我的任何東西。”

 

 你注意到他正在花時間翻閱筆記，計算他要付你多少錢。

passages.name: Gym
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

Basic by any standards, Casey’s Gym is no more than a room covered in soft mats and littered with exercise gear. A full length mirror runs down one side of the room and a ballet barre runs down the entire length.

Gordon, one of the local labourers, sits at one of the weight lifting machines. His tight biceps flex as he pulls the plates high into the air, and then slowly lowers them down with control and confidence.

 
 
Casey stands in the centre of the gym, kicking gym mats together in a square. His hands are encased in blue boxing gloves, yet somehow a lit roll-up dangles on his bottom lip.

 
Casey stands behind the till, leafing through a boxing magazine. A lit roll-up hangs on his lips, which he occasionally takes a drag on.

Mrs. DeBussy stands beside the full-length mirror, clipboard clasped firmly to her chest. She wears a collared polo shirt tucked into thick black leggings, ballet slippers barely visible beneath the hem.

Jake, the school quarterback, is at the back of the gym, running through some dumbbell exercises. Jake seems to strain at the weight he's chosen, regularly just letting go of the dumbbell once he's lifted it, letting it crash to the floor.

 
 
*You watch Jake for a moment. If you really wanted to know what went on in those boys’ classes, Jake could be an option. If you could get your hands on something of his, something personal. Something to power the amulet. Getting him somewhere private wouldn't be a challenge.*

 

**Actions**

<>

<>

<>

<>

[[Go outside->OrchidRoad]]
passages.content_zh:
**凱西的健身房**

[圖：./images/Gym.png]

從任何標準來看，凱西的健身房只不過是個鋪著柔軟墊子、散落著健身器材的房間。一面全身鏡沿著房間的一側延伸，芭蕾舞桿沿著整個長度延伸。

戈登是當地的工人，他坐在一台舉重機前。當他將槓鈴片高高地拉到空中時，他緊繃的二頭肌會彎曲，然後帶著控制力和自信慢慢地放下它們。

 
 
凱西站在健身房中央，把健身墊踢成一個正方形。他的雙手戴著藍色拳擊手套，但不知何故，他的下唇上掛著一個點燃的捲軸。

 
凱西站在收銀台後面，翻閱一本拳擊雜誌。他的嘴唇上掛著一根點燃的捲煙，他偶爾會吸一口。

德比西太太站在全身鏡旁邊，寫字板緊緊地抱在胸前。她穿著一件有領馬球衫，塞進厚厚的黑色緊身褲中，芭蕾舞鞋在下擺下幾乎看不見。

學校四分衛傑克在體育館後面進行啞鈴練習。傑克似乎對他選擇的重量感到緊張，通常在舉起啞鈴後就鬆開它，讓它掉到地板上。

 
 
*你看了傑克一會兒。如果你真的想知道那些男孩的班級裡發生了什麼，傑克可能是一個選擇。如果你能得到他的一些東西，一些私人的東西。為護身符提供能量的東西。讓他去一個私人的地方並不是一個挑戰。 *

passages.name: GymJakeDialogue0
passages.content:
The jock’s eyes flick from the window to you, roaming your body. And yet he remains silent.

 

 “Hey Jake,” you say.

 

 “What do you want?” he spits. “Or did you decide you want to see what a real man looks like?”

 

 He stares back at you, almost daring you to say something. When you don’t reply, his eyes drift to over your shoulder. You glance back, noticing two girls watching from outside, whispering and giggling as they subtly eye Jake.

 
 
 
 The jock raises his eyebrow as you approach, his eyes flicking across your body.

 

 “Hey Jake,” you greet him.

 

 “Hey yourself, nerd,” he says, a wry smile resting on his face. His eyes drift over your shoulder for a moment, and when you glance behind you notice two girls watching from outside, whispering and giggling as they watch the jock.

 

 But when you turn back, Jake’s eyes are firmly settled on you.
 
 
 
 Jake's eyes drift up and down as you step near him. He gives you a smile that you assume he thinks is seductive. It comes across as predatory and lecherous.

 

 “Hey there,” he says to you. “Haven’t seen you around for a while.”

 

 You study him carefully, not sure of the relationship you currently have with him. “I’ve been busy,” you tell him. 

 

 He smiles, his eyes again lingering over your body. “What can I do for you, sweetheart?”

 

 Before you can reply, you hear muted girlish laughter from behind you. Turning, you see two girls watching through the window, whispering as they try to pretend they aren’t watching Jake.

 

 But when you turn back to him, you can’t help but notice his attention rests firmly with you.

 
 Jake looks at you with derision as you approach. “Yeah?” he snaps. “What are you looking at?”

 

 You are about to reply when you hear muted giggles from behind you. Turning, you note there are two girls looking into the gym from outside, whispering and eying both you and Jake with interest. 

 

 By the look of his lingering grin and drifting eyes, Jake has noticed too.
passages.content_zh:
運動員的目光從窗戶轉向你，在你的身體上漫遊。但他仍然保持沉默。

 

 「嘿傑克，」你說。

 

 「你想要什麼？」他吐口水。 “或者你決定要看看真正的男人是什麼樣子？”

 

 他回頭看著你，幾乎是在挑釁你說些什麼。當你不回答時，他的目光就會從你的肩膀上移過去。你回頭看了一眼，注意到兩個女孩在外面看著，她們一邊竊竊私語，一邊咯咯地笑，巧妙地看著傑克。

 
 
 
 當你走近時，運動員揚起眉毛，目光掃過你的身體。

 

 「嘿傑克，」你向他打招呼。

 

 「嘿，你自己，書呆子，」他說，臉上掛著苦笑。他的目光在你的肩膀上移了一會兒，當你回頭看時，你會發現兩個女孩在外面看著，一邊看著運動員一邊竊竊私語、咯咯笑。

 

 但當你回頭時，傑克的目光牢牢地落在你身上。
 
 
 
 當你走近傑克時，他的眼睛上下移動。他給你一個微笑，你認為他認為這是誘人的。它給人的印像是掠奪性和好色的。

 

 「嘿，」他對你說。 “有一段時間沒見到你了。”

 

 你仔細研究他，不確定你現在和他的關係。 「我一直很忙，」你告訴他。 

 

 他微笑著，目光再次落在你的身上。 “我能為你做點什麼嗎，親愛的？”

 

 還沒等你回答，身後就傳來少女般的輕笑聲。轉身，你看到兩個女孩透過窗戶看著，一邊竊竊私語，一邊試著假裝沒在看傑克。

 

 但當你回頭看他時，你會情不自禁地註意到他的注意力牢牢地集中在你身上。

 
 當你走近時，傑克用嘲笑的眼神看著你。 「是的？」他厲聲說。 “你在看什麼？”

 

 你正要回答，身後傳來低沉的笑聲。轉過身，你發現有兩個女孩從外面看著健身房，竊竊私語，饒有興趣地看著你和傑克。 

 

 從他揮之不去的笑容和飄忽不定的眼神中，傑克也注意到了。

passages.name: GymDeBussyDialogue
passages.content:
As you approach, DeBussy looks up, her face lighting with a beaming smile. “Oh! Emily. What a delight. I didn’t expect to see you here on a weekend. But then, you are one of my more proactive girls.”

 

 Her eyes drop momentarily to her clipboard before returning to yours. “Here to take part in my ballet instruction, are you?” she asks, voice syrupy. “A sharpened mind requires a graceful body, after all. And discipline is discipline, no matter the subject.”

 

 Her smile lingers, polite and practiced. “I’d be very pleased to include you. Provided, of course, you’re willing to take it seriously.”

 
 As you approach, DeBussy looks up. A polite smile forms. “Hello dear. I didn’t expect to see you, today. What brings you here?”

 

 She straightens a little, folding her arms over the clipboard. Her ballet slippers peek out beneath her leggings, though she makes no effort to draw attention to them. “Are you here to partake in my ballet lessons?” she asks when you don’t respond. “Typically I don’t encourage my students to partake. Weekends are for reflection. Quiet study. Perhaps even a little revision.”

 

 Her eyes meet yours, as if burrowing into your brain to work out your thoughts. “For you though,” she says after some time. “I will make an exception.” A thin smile forms on her lips, but she stands waiting for your answer. 

 
 
 DeBussy looks up as you approach, a smile appearing that can’t quite mask her confusion. “Good afternoon,” she murmurs. “You’re Emily’s , aren't you? What brings you here?”

 
 
 You pause before replying, a cold shiver spreading up your spine. “Yes. I’m Emily’s .”

 

 
 At this, DeBussy breaks into a smile. “Oh! She’s a bright girl! I shouldn’t say this, but between us two, she’s one of my favourites. So diligent. Observant. A sharp eye for detail. She’ll go far, with the right tutoring.”

passages.content_zh:
當你走近時，德比西抬起頭來，臉上綻放出燦爛的笑容。 “哦！艾米麗。真是太高興了。我沒想到週末會在這裡見到你。不過，你是我比較主動的女孩之一。”

 

 她的目光短暫地落在她的寫字板上，然後又回到你的寫字板上。 「你是來參加我的芭蕾舞指導的嗎？」她問道，聲音低沉。 “畢竟，敏銳的頭腦需要優美的身體。紀律就是紀律，無論什麼科目。”

 她稍微挺直身子，雙手交叉放在寫字板上。她的芭蕾舞鞋在緊身褲下露出來，儘管她並沒有試圖引起人們的注意。 「你是來參加我的芭蕾舞課的嗎？」她會在你沒有回應時問。 “通常我不鼓勵我的學生參與。週末是用來反思的。安靜的學習。甚至可能做一點複習。”

 你在回答之前停了下來，一陣冷顫從你的脊椎蔓延開來。 “是的。我是艾米麗的。”

 "Lifting," he mutters. "Chat after."

 Gordon stops his exercising, looking at you appraising your physique. After a moment of study, he seems to make up his mind.

 

 "Can't train like that," he says, locking the plates in a secure position. He looks you up and down, then clambers off the exercise machine. "You a member here?"

 

 
 
 You nod. “Yes.”

 

 Gordon grunts. “Good. You got workout gear with you?”
 
 

 
 
 You nod again. There is a small smile from Gordon, before he returns the gesture. “Go get changed. Come back when ready.”

passages.name: GymGordonTrainFemale
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

 
 Gordon doesn’t look up as you step back onto the mats, but his voice acknowledges you. “Alright. Let’s begin.”

 

 He’s moved a bench into place near the wall-length mirror. A set of pink dumbbells sit neatly arranged beside it. But even as you scan the equipment, you catch your reflection in the glass.

 

 You knew what Annie looked like. Everyone did. But seeing her from the inside, wearing this. It hits differently. You feel the sway in your hips. The bounce in your step.

 

 Without looking you can feel Gordon’s eyes on you, clinical and precise. Glancing over at him you notice he doesn’t leer, but his glance tracks your movements just like every other man in Charlton. Just like you remember watching Annie.

 

 He gives a nod toward the bench. “First set. Overhead presses.”

 

 You step into position. The moment you lift the weights, you feel the stretch of your arms pulling your top higher. As you push up and down, you feel your chest noticeably rising and falling with every breath. 

 

 Finishing the set, you stand up and shift on the balls of your feet, becoming aware of the way your back arches naturally. You find yourself naturally smiling at Gordon as he resets the machine. You know how inviting that smile can be.

 

 You should focus on the workout. But part of you wonders if being watched might not be so bad. In this body, it almost feels natural.
 
 
 Whilst you’ve been in the locker room, Gordon has been setting up for the exercise session. He’s placed a bench on the mats in front of the mirror and carefully placed a number of dumbbells to the side. You note the dumbbells are smaller than you’ve used before. Also, they are pink.

 

 “Ready?” Gordon says, making some final adjustments to his preparations. As far as you can tell, he doesn’t react to your change of clothes. His nonchalant attitude quickly puts you at ease a little, in spite of still feeling slightly exposed standing to the side in Jen
passages.content_zh:
**凱西的健身房**

[圖：./images/Gym.png]

 
 當你走回墊子時，戈登沒有抬頭，但他的聲音認出了你。 “好吧，我們開始吧。”

 

 他把一張長椅移到了牆上鏡子附近。旁邊整齊地擺放著一組粉紅色啞鈴。但即使當您掃描設備時，您也會在玻璃上看到自己的倒影。

 

 你知道安妮長什麼樣子。每個人都這麼做了。但從裡面看她，穿著這個。它的打擊不同。你感覺到臀部的搖擺。你腳步的彈跳。

 

 不用看，你就能感覺到戈登的目光在看著你，冷靜而精確。你瞥了他一眼，發現他並沒有斜視，但他的目光注視著你的動作，就像查爾頓的其他人一樣。就像你記得看安妮一樣。

 

 他向替補席點了點頭。 “第一組。過頂推舉。”

 

 你就位。當你舉起重物的那一刻，你會感覺到手臂的伸展將你的上衣拉得更高。當您上下推壓時，您會感覺到胸部隨著每次呼吸而明顯起伏。 

 

 完成一組後，您站起來並用腳掌移動，意識到您的背部自然拱起的方式。當戈登重置機器時，你發現自己自然地對他微笑。你知道那個微笑是多麼誘人。

 

 你應該專注於鍛鍊。但有些人想知道被監視是否也不是那麼糟。在這個身體裡，感覺幾乎是自然的。
 
 
 當你在更衣室的時候，戈登已經在準備訓練了。他在鏡子前的墊子上放了一條長凳，並小心地在旁邊放了一些啞鈴。您注意到啞鈴比您以前使用過的小。而且，它們是粉紅色的。

 

 「準備好？」戈登說道，對他的準備工作做了一些最後的調整。據你所知，他對你換衣服沒有反應。他漫不經心的態度很快讓你放鬆了一點，儘管在 Jen 的旁邊仍然感覺有點暴露

passages.name: GymGordonTrainFemaleOutcome
passages.content:
You follow Gordon’s instruction and step into a lunge, but tilt your hips slightly more than needed. As your front leg bends, you feel the shorts ride up just a little further.

 

 Catching your reflection in the mirror, you see a flash of bum cheek as you bend at the waist. You hold the pose longer than necessary and a smile curls on your lips when you see that Gordon has noticed too. 

 

 He calls the next set. You move slowly and deliberately. Leaning forward towards the mirror just enough your reflection shows a view down your top to him. You remember how Annie would lean and she’d smile over her shoulder without trying. You find yourself doing the same, looking back at him, biting your lip.

 

 Gordon's face doesn’t change, but you see his eyes lingering on the woman in the mirror. You’ve had that look on your face looking at Annie before. But the difference this time is the person in the mirror is now you. The young woman, flaunting her body and flashing private areas barely contained by the revealing clothing. How far would you go if he told you to do more?

 

 “Enough,” Gordon says, voice low but firm. “We’re done for today.”

 

 You blink. The moment breaks. You rise from your pose, adjusting the top as subtly as you can. Gordon is already re-racking the weights, his eyes unreadable.

 

 You glance up at the clock on the wall and note that it seems like a short session. You wonder if you made the right decision with him. You nod, and head back to the locker room to get changed.
 
 
 You follow Gordon’s advice and lean backward, but take the opportunity to further push your bum out. Facing back away from him, you can easily picture what Jennifer looks like from behind. Except in this instance, it’s you.

 

 Gordon continues to instruct you in different exercises to try and poses to drop into. Instead of focusing on the exercise though, your mind turns to how you can use it to emphasise your body. With each new stance a singular thought drifts through
passages.content_zh:
你按照戈登的指示，進入弓箭步，但臀部傾斜得比需要的稍微多一些。當你的前腿彎曲時，你會感覺到短褲又向上抬起了一點。

 

 當你彎下腰時，你在鏡子裡看到自己的倒影，看到一閃而過的屁股臉頰。你保持這個姿勢的時間超過了必要的時間，當你看到戈登也注意到了這一點時，你的嘴角露出了微笑。 

 

 他叫下一組。你緩慢而謹慎地移動。身體前傾，對著鏡子，鏡子裡的倒影正好能讓他看到你的上半身的景色。你記得安妮會如何傾斜，她會毫不費力地回頭微笑。你發現自己也在做同樣的事，回頭看著他，咬著嘴唇。

 

 戈登的臉色沒有變化，但你看到他的目光在鏡子裡的女人身上徘徊。你以前看著安妮時也曾有過這樣的表情。但這次不同的是，鏡子裡的人現在是你。年輕女子炫耀自己的身體，暴露的私處幾乎被暴露的衣服遮住。如果他告訴你做得更多，你會走多遠？

 

 「夠了，」戈登說，聲音低沉但堅定。 “我們今天就到此為止了。”

 

 你眨眼。這一刻破碎了。你從姿勢中站起來，盡可能巧妙地調整頂部。戈登已經開始重新舉重，他的眼睛難以捉摸。

 

 你抬頭看了一眼牆上的時鐘，發現這似乎是一個很短的會議。你想知道你和他的決定是否正確。你點點頭，然後回到更衣室換衣服。
 
 
 你按照戈登的建議向後傾斜，但藉此機會進一步將你的屁股推出來。背對著他，你可以輕易地想像出詹妮弗背後的樣子。除了這個例子之外，是你。

 

 戈登繼續指導您嘗試不同的練習和姿勢。然而，你的注意力不再集中在運動上，而是轉向如何利用它來強調你的身體。每一個新的立場都會有一個獨特的想法飄過

passages.name: GymGordonTrainMale
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

When you return to the gym, you find Gordon adjusting some dumbbells near an exercise mat. "Here", he says. "These should work."

For the next hour Gordon puts you through your paces, encouraging you when you need it, praising you when you push yourself, calling you out when you take it easy. He doesn't belittle you or put you down, even though the dumbbells are much lighter than what you've seen him use.

"And relax," he says to you, after the final set on his plan. You take several deep breaths, short of oxygen. Your body aches, but you have to admit, you feel better.

"Good work.” Gordon says. "Keep coming. You'll see the difference."

You thank him. Even though you feel tired, there's no question that the workout has made you feel stronger.

With your exercise complete, you have a rare moment to talk to Gordon where he isn't preoccupied with work or lifting.

當你回到健身房時，你會發現戈登正在運動墊附近調整一些啞鈴。 “在這裡”，他說。 “這些應該有用。”

在接下來的一個小時裡，戈登會帶領你完成自己的步伐，在你需要的時候鼓勵你，在你逼迫自己的時候表揚你，在你放鬆的時候大聲喊出來。他不會貶低你或貶低你，儘管啞鈴比你見過的他用的要輕得多。

「放鬆點，」在他的計畫最後一組結束後，他對你說。你做了幾次深呼吸，缺氧。你的身體很痛，但你必須承認，你感覺好多了。

「幹得好。」戈登說。 “繼續來吧。你會看到不同的。”

你謝謝他。儘管您感到疲倦，但毫無疑問，運動讓您感覺更強壯。

運動完成後，你有一個難得的機會與戈登交談，他沒有全神貫注於工作或舉重。

passages.name: GymJakeContest
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

 [image: ./images/Gym.png]

 
 
 

 
 
 You step back out onto the gym floor, Annie’s sneakers squeaking faintly against the polished mats. Every pair of eyes seem to lock on you. The girls outside the glass stare, lips curling in envy. Even Casey, lounging at the desk with a cigarette, tilts his head up. He smirks as he takes a slow drag, watching you.

 

 Jake is no different as you approach him. His eyes track down and back up your body. “Damn,” he says with a grin, “Bet you’d look even better without it, though.” 

 

 The words are as subtle as a sledgehammer, but they still cause a little flutter in your chest. What exactly the amulet has done to you to make even that feel nice?

 
 You step back out onto the gym floor. All eyes seem to turn toward you, but the reaction is muted. The girls outside the window watch you, whispering constantly at the sight of you. Casey looks up from the desk, his gaze measured. You feel like you're walking into a saloon bar in a western.

 

 Jake leans against the nearest machine, his gaze tracking you. “Looking good," he says with a twinge of hesitation.
 
 
 You step back out onto the gym floor, not eliciting much of a reaction. Casey doesn’t even glance up from his boxing magazine and the girls continue to chatter, oblivious to your presence.

 

 Jake does notice, though. His grin comes quick. “That colour’s asking for trouble, nerd. You show up in that, you better be ready to back it up.”

 Jake does notice, though. His grin comes quick. “Interesting look, nerd. That collar got an owner yet? I'd like to apply.”

 Jake does notice, though. His grin comes quick. "Pink frills? Looks like you’re here to shake pom-poms, not lift weights.”

 
 You step back out onto the gym floor, the mat under your shoes giving a faint, tacky squeak. It's subtle, but you see heads lifting. Casey actually looks up from his magazine with a slow look before he flips the page. The girls outside lean in a little closer to the windo
passages.content_zh:
**凱西的健身房**

 [圖：./images/Gym.png]

 
 
 

 
 
 你回到健身房的地板上，安妮的運動鞋在拋光的墊子上發出輕微的吱吱聲。每一雙眼睛似乎都在鎖定你。玻璃外面的女孩們瞪大了眼睛，嘴角翹起，露出羨慕的表情。連坐在辦公桌前抽煙的凱西也抬起了頭。他一邊笑一邊慢慢吸著水，看著你。

 

 當你接近傑克時，他並沒有什麼不同。他的目光追蹤並回到你的身體。 “該死，”他笑著說道，“不過，我敢打賭，如果沒有它，你會看起來更好。”

 

 這句話雖然像大鐵鎚一樣微妙，但還是讓你的胸口有些顫動。護身符到底對你做了什麼，讓你感覺良好？

 
 你退到健身房地板上。所有的目光似乎都轉向你，但反應卻很平靜。窗外的女孩們注視著你，一看到你就不停地竊竊私語。凱西從桌子上抬起頭，目光審視著。你感覺就像走進了西部片中的酒吧。

 

 傑克靠在最近的機器上，目光注視著你。 「看起來不錯，」他猶豫地說。
 
 
 你回到健身房的地板上，沒有引起太大的反應。凱西甚至沒有從他的拳擊雜誌上抬起頭來，女孩們繼續聊天，沒有註意到你的存在。

 

 不過，傑克確實注意到了。他的笑容來得很快。 “那個顏色是自找麻煩，書呆子。你穿著那個顏色出現，你最好準備好支持它。”

 不過，傑克確實注意到了。他的笑容來得很快。 “看起來很有趣，書呆子。那個項圈有主人了嗎？我想申請。”

 不過，傑克確實注意到了。他的笑容來得很快。 “粉紅色的褶邊？看來你是來搖絨球的，不是來舉重的。”

 
 你退到健身房的地板上，鞋子下的墊子發出微弱的、俗氣的吱吱聲。雖然很微妙，但你會看到人們抬起頭來。凱西實際上從雜誌上抬起頭，緩慢地看了一眼，然後翻了一頁。外面的女孩們靠得離窗戶近一點

passages.name: GymBoxingClass
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

 [image: ./images/Gym.png]

 

 You walk back into the gym. Casey’s finished adjusting the mats and is bouncing lightly on the balls of his feet, rolling his shoulders, shifting his weight, almost dancing. It’s a little uneven, his limp catching now and then, but he moves like someone who remembers at least how it used to feel.

 

 At the sight of you, he stops. “Alright,” he says, taking one last drag from the cigarette. Then, without ceremony, he stubs it out directly on the skirting board. “You ready?”

 

 Before you can answer, he tosses a pair of gloves your way. You fumble slightly catching them, but more from the unexpected speed than the weight. Casey chuckles, but in doing so stumbles a little over his bad leg. He grunts, recovers, and says nothing.

 

 You pull on the gloves slowly, tightening each strap. As you do, your eyes flick toward the window. The boys are still there. Their breath fogs faint patches in the glass. One of them points at Casey and says something you can’t hear, but it makes the others snicker. 

 

 Casey doesn’t look, but his shoulders square just slightly as if he’s trying not to hear what he’s already heard. He steps onto the mat and gestures for you to join him. “Hands up,” he says simply. “Keep your guard tight. Stay light on your feet.”

 

 He adjusts his stance, loose and low, one glove tapping lightly at his chin.

 

 “If you’re ready, we’ll begin.”

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 You step onto the mat, gloves raised. Casey starts circling you, light on his feet despite his leg. His eyes flick across your stance, watching every shift in your balance. Then, without warning, he throws a couple of slow, easy punches toward your gloves.

 

 They land with a soft thud. The technique is still sharp, but there’s no weight behind them. Not anymore.

 

 “Turn that lead foot in,” he mutters. “You’re open on the left. Chin down. Guard tight.”

 

 You adjust, and he nods. He keeps circling, throwing a few more lazy jabs. You

 At the sight of you, he stops. 「好吧，」他說著，吸了最後一口香菸。然後，他毫不客氣地把它直接丟在踢腳板上。 “你準備好了嗎？”
