passages.name: LibraryJenniferTerrence
passages.content:
You slip past the reading tables into the hushed depths of the library, where shafts of morning light slant through high windows onto worn carpets. At the rear, for the first time you notice the semi-opaque glass that runs down one wall isn't just glass. Upon closer inspection, there are doors built in.

 

 You listen in each one and strike gold with the third door. From inside comes the low murmur of two voices. One calm and measured, unmistakably Jennifer’s. The other tentative and wavering, perhaps Terrence. Their words are swallowed by the glass, but the urgency in their tones is clear.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 <> 
 
 Looking around, you spot something that makes you grin. Reruns of Agatha Christie and Poirot have served you well. At the end of the study rooms is a well placed vent.

 

 You kneel down and listen in. Sure enough, you can hear the conversation in the room.

 

 “This isn’t a game, Terrence,” you hear Jennifer say. “It’s survival. In class, if a teacher calls on you, just nod and pretend you understand. Don’t volunteer extra. Just fit in.”

 

 “I still don’t understand why it keeps happening,” the nervous stammers of Terrence easily identifiable. “I try to pretend to be like them. In the changing rooms, I even joked about girls. About how nice they looked in their swimsuits. They laughed at me!”

 

 You push your ear closer to the vent. Whatever this is, it’s not biology tutoring.

 

 You hear Jennifer sigh. “You’re trying too hard. Don’t try to mimic them. That always feels fake. With the bullies, same rule: avoid participating in their games. But when they crack a joke, smile and nod. Just let them think you agree.”

 

 Terrence takes a deep sigh. “But that Annie woman you talked about doesn’t blend into the background.”

 

 Jennifer’s reply sounds impatient. “That was her problem. She stood out. It was easier to see, because she was loud. Don’t be like her. Don’t stand out. Keep your head down, get through it
passages.content_zh:
你穿過閱讀桌，走進圖書館寂靜的深處，晨光透過高高的窗戶斜射到破舊的地毯上。在後面，您第一次注意到沿著牆壁延伸的半透明玻璃不僅僅是玻璃。仔細一看，裡面是有門的。

 

 你仔細聽每一扇門，然後用第三扇門敲金子。裡面傳來兩道低沉的聲音。一位冷靜而有分寸的人，毫無疑問是詹妮弗的。另一個猶豫不決、猶豫不決的人，也許是泰倫斯。他們的話被玻璃吞沒了，但語氣中的迫切感卻顯而易見。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 <>
 
 環顧四周，你發現了一些讓你會心一笑的東西。阿加莎克莉絲蒂和波洛的重播讓你受益匪淺。書房的盡頭有一個位置很好的通風口。

 

 你蹲下側耳傾聽，果然，你聽得到房間裡的談話聲。

 

 「這不是遊戲，泰倫斯，」你聽到珍妮佛說。 “這是生存。在課堂上，如果老師點名你，只需點頭並假裝你理解。不要額外自願。只要融入就行。”

 

 「我仍然不明白為什麼這種事總是發生，」泰倫斯緊張的結巴聲很容易辨認出來。 「我試著假裝像他們一樣。在更衣室裡，我甚至拿女孩開玩笑。說她們穿泳衣有多好看。他們嘲笑我！」

 

 您將耳朵靠近通風口。不管這是什麼，這不是生物輔導。

 

 你聽到詹妮弗嘆了口氣。 「你太努力了。不要試圖模仿他們。那總是感覺很假。對於惡霸，同樣的規則：避免參加他們的遊戲。但當他們開玩笑時，微笑並點頭。只要讓他們認為你同意即可。”

 

 泰倫斯深深嘆了口氣。 “但是你談到的那個安妮女人並沒有融入背景。”

 

 珍妮佛的回答聽起來很不耐煩。 “那是她的問題。她很引人注目。更容易看到，因為她聲音很大。不要像她一樣。不要脫穎而出。低下頭，度過難關。”

passages.name: LibraryCharltonGazette
passages.content:
It takes you only moments to spot the machinery in the otherwise technology free library. In the corner stands the Charlton Gazette microfilm reader: a utilitarian brushed-steel console with a stark monochrome screen, two empty spindles ready for reels, and a foot pedal for scrolling through each frame.

 

 Beside it, a low oak cart holds hundreds of tightly labeled film canisters. Each reel preserves an entire year’s worth of Gazette editions. You’ll need to be precise about dates and topics before threading the right reel into the reader.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You thread reel after reel into the reader, tapping the foot pedal to flick past years of issues. Back and forth you scroll… until a headline from four years ago snaps into focus:

 

 **Sophomore Jennifer Chambers aces every subject**
Written by Owen Oakswood (editor)

 

 In a feat rarely seen at Charlton School, sophomore Jennifer Chambers claimed the top score in Mathematics, Science, English and History, surpassing every previous class record and setting a new standard for her year.

 

 “I’ve taken her under my wing to help accelerate her education,” said Headmaster Stitch, also serving as the coordinator of Charlton’s Accelerated Learning Program. “Her dedication and grasp of complex concepts at such a young age is both expected and exceptional.”

 

 Whispers in the halls suggest that, beyond her academic triumphs, Jennifer is poised to succeed Skye Edison as head of the cheerleader squad when Edison graduates this spring. Proof that Chambers is already making her mark both in classrooms and on the field.
 
 <>
 

 **Actions**

 <>
passages.content_zh:
只需片刻時間，您就可以在無科技的圖書館中找到機器。角落裡立著查爾頓公報縮微膠卷閱讀器：一個實用的拉絲鋼控制台，帶有鮮明的單色屏幕，兩個準備捲軸的空軸，以及一個用於滾動瀏覽每一幀的腳踏板。

 

 旁邊有一輛低矮的橡木推車，裡面裝有數百個貼有標籤的膠卷。每個捲軸都保存了一整年的公報版本。在將正確的捲軸穿入閱讀器之前，您需要準確地了解日期和主題。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 您將一卷又一卷地穿入閱讀器，輕敲腳踏板即可翻閱過去幾年的期刊。你來回滾動…直到四年前的標題突然成為焦點：

 

 **二年級學生珍妮佛錢伯斯每科都名列前茅**
作者：歐文‧奧克斯伍德（編輯）

 

 二年級學生 Jennifer Chambers 在數學、科學、英語和歷史方面取得了最高分，超越了之前的所有班級記錄，為她的學年樹立了新標準，這在查爾頓學校是罕見的壯舉。

 

 「我將她置於我的保護之下，以幫助她加速學業，」兼任查爾頓加速學習計畫協調員的史蒂奇校長說道。 “她在這麼年輕的時候就表現出了奉獻精神以及對複雜概念的掌握，這既是意料之中的，也是非凡的。”

 

 大廳裡的竊竊私語表明，除了學業上的成就之外，珍妮佛還準備在愛迪生今年春天畢業時接替斯凱·愛迪生成為啦啦隊隊長。事實證明錢伯斯已經在課堂和球場上留下了自己的印記。
 
 <>
 

 **行動**

 <>

passages.name: LibraryStacksInvestigation
passages.content:
You walk through the library to dozens of stacks, holding hundreds - if not thousands - of books. Quickly you realise that the library uses a decimal filing system with seemingly no logic. You let out a small sigh. This could take a while. 

 

 It takes you longer than you would like to find some order to the library. You see that each book has a number, but the shelves simply stack order them from first to the last. In some places you find books with similar topics, but even when it’s clear there’s some commonality you can’t quite work out what the exact subject is. Never mind how it relates to the next group. If there’s a link at all.

 

 After an hour, you try a different tactic. Liz mentioned that you are looking for magazines published by Sally Hope Publishing. So instead, you walk down each aisle, looking for any traces of thin magazine spines standing out against the multitude of paperback and hardback books.

 

 You find yourself stopping constantly. You assume the head librarian must be a hoarder. It feels like the Charlton Library stocks everything from the invention of the printing press up until the 1970s. Which, you do note, seems to be the last decade any contemporary texts were added to the collection.

 

 Then something tickles your hand. You stop, suddenly aware of a warm sensation brushing your fingers. You look down, seeing your tips casually running across the spines of the book on a low shelf. Looking carefully, you see the faint yellow aura of magical energy seeping out from between two bound books.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 After a quick check that you aren’t being observed, you reach down and pull one of the books from the shelf. *Further excavations at Tovil Hole*, by Justin Humphreys. A photo of an underground cave fills the cover. 

 

 This book, you note, isn’t the source of the magical energy. That would be the flimsy magazine that was wedged between it and the preceding book.

 

 You pick up the magazine in your other hand. It’s old
passages.content_zh:
你穿過圖書館，看到幾十個書庫，裡面藏著數百本——如果不是數千本——的書。很快您意識到圖書館使用十進制歸檔系統，看似沒有邏輯。你輕輕嘆了口氣。這可能需要一段時間。 

 

 您在圖書館找到訂單所需的時間比您想要的要長。您會看到每本書都有一個編號，但書架只是按照從第一本書到最後一本的順序排列。在某些地方，您會找到具有相似主題的書籍，但即使很明顯存在一些共通點，您也無法完全弄清楚確切的主題是什麼。不要介意它與下一組有何關係。如果有的話。

 

 一小時後，您嘗試不同的策略。莉茲提到您正在尋找莎莉霍普出版社出版的雜誌。因此，你沿著每條過道走，尋找在眾多平裝書和精裝書中脫穎而出的薄雜誌書脊的痕跡。

 

 你發現自己不斷停下來。你認為圖書館館長一定是囤積者。感覺查爾頓圖書館藏有從印刷機發明到 20 世紀 70 年代的所有資料。您確實注意到，這似乎是最近十年所有當代文本被添加到該集合中的時間。

 

 然後有東西會讓你的手發癢。你停下來，突然感覺到一種溫暖的感覺刷過你的手指。你低下頭，看到你的秘訣隨意地穿過低架子上的書脊。仔細看去，只見兩本裝訂成冊的書之間，滲出了淡黃色的魔氣氣息。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 快速檢查了一下自己沒有被觀察後，你伸手從書架上拿出一本書。 *托維爾洞的進一步挖掘*，賈斯汀·漢弗萊斯 (Justin Humphreys)。封面上有一張地下洞穴的照片。 

 

 你注意到，這本書不是魔法能量的來源。那將會是一本薄薄的雜誌，夾在它和前一本書之間。

 

 你拿起另一隻手上的雜誌。已經舊了

passages.name: LibraryMagazines
passages.content:
You walk through the library and go to the stacks where you found the magazines. You spend a few minutes pulling out the magazines you identified, briefly looking at the coverlines again:

 

 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/PumpingIron.png]
 
 
 **A Boy’s Adventure, Issue #34**

 *Pumping iron - Max Caress, the school drop out who made millions lifting weights*

 
 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/CorporateLadder.png]
 
 
 **A Boy’s Adventure, Issue #27**

 *8 ways to climb the corporate ladder - Super charge your career in six months by telling her no*

 
 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/SuccessWorkplace.png]
 
 
 **New Women Magazine, Issue #11**

 *Success in the workplace - Tips to keep your mind sharp at work and keep your boss happy*

 
 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/KeepHim.png]
 
 
 **New Women Magazine, Issue #8**

 *Keep him on his toes - 8 ways to take the lead and keep him keen*

 
 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/Career.png]
 
 
 **New Women Magazine, Issue #5**

 *“I chose my career over my man” - A model reflects on breaking it off with her fiancé and focusing on London’s fashion scene*

 
 
 
 

 You consider reading one of the magazines, cautious that each one crackles with magic.

 
 You flick through the issues of the magical magazines. They feel warm to the touch. The coverlines seem to call out to you and the words look like they are shimmering with energy.

 

 As you view each cover, you notice an identical logo in the bottom right of each magazine. “Sally Hope Publishing.” *15 Village Green, Charlton, CA1 2BA.* The name is referenced elsewhere too. Whilst A Boy’s Adventure lists Oliver Oakwood as the editor, Sally is the editor of The New Woman too.

 

 You briefly digest the lead story for each issue:
 
 

 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/PumpingIron.png]
 
 
 **A Boy’s Adventure, Issue #34**

 *Pumping iron - Max Caress, the school drop out who made millions lifting weights*

 
 
 
 
 [image: ./images/Magazines/CorporateLadder.png]

 **男孩的冒險，第 34 期**

 *舉重運動員－Max Caress，一名輟學的舉重運動員，他賺了數百萬美元*

 你考慮讀一本雜誌，小心翼翼地確保每一本都充滿了魔法。

 
 你翻閱魔法雜誌的各期。他們摸起來很溫暖。封面線似乎在召喚你，文字看起來閃爍著能量。

 您簡單消化每期的主要故事：
 
 

 
 
 
 [圖：./images/Magazines/PumpingIron.png]
 
 
 **男孩的冒險，第 34 期**

 *舉重運動員－Max Caress，一名輟學的舉重運動員，他賺了數百萬美元*

passages.name: LibraryAVRoom
passages.content:
**Charlton Library - AV Room**

[image: ./images/library_avroom.png]

A small room decorated in the same sterile fashion as the rest of the library, but instead of books the shelves are full of video cassettes. One single TV with a video player lies on a table at the end of the room.

<>

 
*You look at Dennis for a few seconds. If you really wanted to know what went on in those boys’ classes, Dennis would do. If you could get your hands on something of his, something personal. Something to power the amulet. Getting him somewhere private wouldn't be a challenge.*

 

**Actions**

<>

[[Go back to the library->Library]]
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓圖書館 - 視聽室**

[圖：./images/library_avroom.png]

一個小房間的裝飾風格與圖書館其他部分相同，但書架上沒有書籍，而是放滿了錄影帶。房間盡頭的桌子上放著一台有視訊播放器的電視。

<>

 
*你看了丹尼斯幾秒鐘。如果你真的想知道那些男孩的班級裡發生了什麼，丹尼斯就可以。如果你能得到他的一些東西，一些私人的東西。為護身符提供能量的東西。讓他去一個私人的地方並不是一個挑戰。 *

 

**行動**

<>

[[返回圖書館->圖書館]]

passages.name: LibraryAVRoomDennisDialogue0
passages.content:
When Dennis sees you approaching, he flares his nostrils and turns his attention back to the movie. You sense he doesn’t want to talk to you.

 
 Dennis almost jumps as he sees you approach him. “Hi Emily,” he mumbles. "What are you doing here?"

 
 
 Dennis jumps as you make your presence known. At the sight of you, he smiles nervously and turns his attention back to the TV. You get the impression that he won’t even consider looking away from it until you’ve gone.

 

 Not wanting to torture him, you decide to leave.
 
 
 Dennis glances up at you as you move towards him. “Hey,” he says quietly, giving you a polite smile. Then he turns to look back to watch the movie.

 

 You consider interrupting him, but you aren’t sure what to ask and whether he’d say anything of value. Instead, you decide to leave him to watch his movie in peace.

 
 
 Dennis jumps as you make your presence known. At the sight of you, he smiles nervously and turns his attention back to the TV. You get the impression that he won’t even consider looking away until you’ve gone.

 

 Not wanting to torture him, you decide to leave.

 當丹尼斯看到你走近他時，他幾乎跳了起來。 「嗨艾米麗，」他咕噥道。 “你在這裡做什麼？”

 
 
 當你讓大家知道你的存在時，丹尼斯跳了起來。看到你，他緊張地笑了笑，然後把注意力轉回電視上。你會覺得，在你離開之前，他甚至不會考慮將目光移開。

 

 不想再折磨他，你決定離開。
 
 
 當你走向丹尼斯時，他抬頭看了你一眼。 「嘿，」他輕聲說，給你一個禮貌的微笑。然後他轉身看電影。

 

 你考慮打斷他，但你不確定該問什麼，也不知道他是否會說一些有價值的話。相反，你決定讓他安靜地看電影。

passages.name: LibraryAVRoomWatchTape
passages.content:
Dennis’s head lifts, the glow of the TV reflecting off his glasses. For once, he seems almost eager. He snaps the stop button on the player and starts riffling through the stacks.

 

 “Here. This one.” His voice carries a rare brightness as he holds up a sleeve, its glossy cover printed with swirling galaxies and the pale blue curve of Earth. “They used to show these in the girls’ lessons.”

 

 You take the case from him, frowning at the title. “And the boys? What do they get?”

 

 Dennis hesitates. His shoulders tense as if he wishes he hadn’t said anything. You don’t let him wriggle free.

 

 “Well?”

 

 He wets his lips, then sighs and digs deeper into the pile. After a moment, he pulls out another cassette. This one is harsher, its sleeve stark with monochrome rockets and cold steel gantries. Soviet rockets versus American. As you stare at it, the Soviet flag almost looks like it flutters on the cover. The paper inside the sleeve shimmers and at its edges you see the now familiar amber light seeping out. The sickly glow of magic.

 

 Dennis doesn’t look you in the eye as he sets it down. “Yeah. Different tapes.”

 
 
 [image: ./images/vhs_raceforthestars.png] [image: ./images/vhs_horizonsoftheuniverse.png]
 
 

 Your gaze drifts between the two boxes, their weight in the air heavy with choice. One promises wonder, the other competition. Neither feels safe.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 
 Dennis presses the tape into the machine. The reels whirr, and the screen resolves into a wavering shot of Earth. Delicate, suspended against the dark.

 

 The voiceover begins, soft and reverent, every syllable measured. “Our world drifts in silence, a fragile home balanced against the immensity of space. To see it clearly is to see how small we are. How precious. How gentle.”

 

 The stars bloom across the screen, glowing in soft spirals. The narration lingers not on conquest, nor on the mechanics of space, but on harmony. On balance and beauty.

passages.content_zh:
丹尼斯抬起頭，電視的光芒反射在他的眼鏡上。這一次，他看起來幾乎很渴望。他按下播放器的停止按鈕，開始翻閱書堆。

 

 「這裡。這個。」當他舉起袖子時，他的聲音帶著罕見的明亮，光滑的封面上印著旋轉的星系和地球的淡藍色曲線。 “他們過去常常在女孩的課堂上展示這些。”

 

 你從他手中接過箱子，皺著眉頭看著標題。 “那男孩們呢？他們得到了什麼？”

 

 丹尼斯猶豫了。他的肩膀繃緊，彷彿希望自己什麼也沒說。你不能讓他掙脫束縛。

 

 “出色地？”

 

 他潤濕了嘴唇，然後嘆了口氣，更深地陷入了堆裡。過了一會兒，他又拿出另一盤錄音帶。這款更嚴酷，它的套筒上刻滿了單色火箭和冷鋼龍門。蘇聯火箭與美國火箭。當你凝視它時，蘇聯國旗看起來幾乎就像在封面上飄揚。袖子內的紙張閃閃發光，在其邊緣你可以看到現在熟悉的琥珀色光芒滲出。病態的魔法光芒。

 

 丹尼斯放下它時沒有看你的眼睛。 “是的。不同的磁帶。”

 
 
 [圖：./images/vhs_raceforthestars.png] [圖：./images/vhs_horizonsoftheuniverse.png]
 
 

 你的目光在兩個盒子之間徘徊，它們在空氣中的重量充滿了選擇。一個承諾創造奇蹟，另一個承諾競爭。兩者都感覺不安全。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 
 丹尼斯將膠帶壓入機器。捲軸發出呼呼聲，螢幕上呈現出搖曳的地球鏡頭。精緻，懸浮在黑暗中。

 

 畫外音開始了，輕柔虔誠，每個音節都很有節奏。 「我們的世界在寂靜中漂流，一個脆弱的家與浩瀚的太空保持著平衡。看清楚它，就知道我們是多麼渺小。多麼珍貴。多麼溫柔。」

 

 星星在螢幕上綻放，發出柔和的螺旋光。敘述的重點不是征服，也不是空間的機制，而是和諧。關於平衡和美麗。

 

 丹尼斯保持 h

**McTafey's Bar**

[image: ./images/McTafeys_Bar.png]

Rhiannon McTafey stands behind the bar, serving people. She smiles and greets you as you enter.

 
Clive sits at the bar, drinking quietly and talking to Rhiannon whenever she’s not serving customers.

 
Martin also sits off at the back, leering at the few women in the bar.

 
 
 
 
Annie sits on a table by herself, dressed up and sipping on a drink.

passages.name: McTafeysBarMartinDialogue0
passages.content:
“Hey darl,” you hear Martin say as you glance in his direction. He sits on his own in the corner, a half-full beer glass on the table next to a couple of empty ones. He looks at you lecherously, his eyes crawling up and down your body. “Looking for some fun?”

 

 Without waiting for an answer, Martin clambers to his feet. You can tell from his sluggish movements that he’s had a lot to drink. Other patrons glance at the two of you as he stumbles over. Even drunk, Martin is an intimidating presence. As he closes the gap between you, you realise how much taller than you he now is. 

 

 He leans in close enough for you to smell the alcohol on his breath. “I bet you are. Girlies don’t come into places like this if they’re not after a little fun.”

 

 You glance around the bar. People are looking in your direction with concerned eyes, but no one is making a move to intervene. Not yet. 

 

 His hand shoots out and grabs you around the waist, pulling you close to his body. “What do you say, darl? Want to have some fun with me?”

 Martin sits on his own in the corner, a half-full beer glass on the table next to a couple of empty ones. He looks around the bar, eying the men darkly and leering over the few women that frequent the bar. 

 

 “Nerd!” he spits when he sees you. You can tell from his voice he’s already had more than a few. “Who said you were man enough to come into my bar?”

 

 “Your name isn’t above the door, Martin” you hear Rhiannon shout back at him from behind the bar. His eyes flick towards her, his face caught up in a snarl. But he looks back at you.

 

 “Where’s your sister?” he says, with an aggressive undertone. “She’s old enough to drink now, isn’t she?”

 

 The other patrons give the two of you a look, weighing up the likelihood of a scene. Martin takes your hesitation as a sign to continue, smirking. “Or what about that girlfriend of yours? The shop girl. Used to see her wandering the streets with her friends, flaunting around in that cheerlead
passages.content_zh:
「嘿，親愛的，」當你朝他的方向看去時，你聽到馬丁說。他獨自坐在角落裡，桌上放著一個半滿的啤酒杯，旁邊還有幾個空啤酒杯。他淫蕩地看著你，目光在你的身上上下爬行。 “想找點樂子嗎？”

 

 不等他回答，馬丁就站了起來。從他緩慢的動作就可以看出，他喝了很多酒。當他跌倒時，其他顧客看了你們兩個一眼。即使喝醉了，馬丁也是一個令人生畏的存在。當他縮小你們之間的差距時，你會意識到他現在比你高了多少。 

 

 他靠得很近，你可以聞到他呼吸中的酒精味。 “我打賭你是的。如果女孩們不追求一點樂趣，就不會來這種地方。”

 

 你環顧了一下酒吧。人們用關切的目光看著你的方向，但沒有人採取行動介入。還沒有。 

 

 他的手伸了過來，攬住了你的腰，將你拉近了他的身體。 “親愛的，你說什麼？想和我一起玩嗎？”

 馬丁獨自坐在角落裡，桌上放著一個半滿的啤酒杯，旁邊還有幾個空啤酒杯。他環顧酒吧四周，陰沉地看著男人們，斜眼看著酒吧裡常去的幾個女人。 

 

 「書呆子！」他一看到你就吐口水。從他的聲音中你可以聽出他已經吃過不少了。 “誰說你有男子氣概可以來我的酒吧了？”

 

 「馬丁，門上沒有你的名字，」你聽到萊安農在吧台後面對他喊道。他的目光掃向她，臉上陷入了咆哮。但他回頭看了你一眼。

 

 「你姊姊在哪裡？」他語氣咄咄逼人地說。 “她現在已經到了可以喝酒的年齡了，不是嗎？”

 

 其他顧客看了你們兩個一眼，權衡發生場景的可能性。馬丁將你的猶豫視為繼續的信號，傻笑著。 「或者你的那個女朋友呢？那個女店員。以前經常看到她和朋友們一起在街上閒逛，穿著啦啦隊的衣服到處炫耀。」

passages.name: McTafeysBarCliveDialogue0
passages.content:
Clive gives you an appraising look as you stand near the bar. “Hi there,” he says.

 

 You smile politely and wait patiently for Rhiannon to finish with other customers. You glance back at Clive, who watches you with some interest. 

 

 “Hey Rhiannon!” he suddenly says, loud but not shouting. “Can you get this lady a drink? On me.”

 

 “Thank you,” you reply back to him. He smiles and gestures for you to sit down. “Feel free to join, if you’re on your lonesome.”

 

 You carefully slide up onto the stool next to Clive. Rhiannon promptly comes over to take your order. When she returns with your glass, Clive chinks his glass against yours. “Here’s to good company,” he says, taking a sip.

 
 Clive gestures to the seat next to him when you approach the bar. ”Evenin’ Come. Join. Rhiannon, can you get Phillips a drink? On me.”

 

 Rhiannon takes your drink order and you take a seat at the bar with Clive as she brings it to you. Clive chinks your glass.

 

 “To good health,” he says, taking a gulp from his glass.

 
 

 

 
 Clive nurses his drink during the lull of the conversation, all the while smiling at you. Before you know it, he's ordered another drink and placed it in front of you.

 
 Clive takes another sip from his drink, at ease both in the casual atmosphere of McTafeys and moments of silence in conversations. Before you know it, he's ordered another drink and placed it in front of you.

 
 

 
 

 
 Clive finishes his drink and turns to you. ”What about you, little lady? Fancy another one? Nice to spend some quality time with a ravishing looking girl.”

 

 You look back at Clive. You already feel a little lightheaded from the drink. He grins at you with a disarming smile. 
 
 
 Clive finishes his drink and turns to you. “King of beers,” he says. “Man’s drink too. One more?”

 

 You take a look at your own glass, still half-full. Feeling a little lightheaded already, you have a feeling that Clive may not mean just one more. 
 
 
 

**Actions**

 
 <>
passages.content_zh:
當你站在吧台附近時，克萊夫用一種打量的眼神看著你。 「你好，」他說。

 

 你禮貌地微笑著，耐心等待萊安農與其他顧客結束。你回頭看了一眼克萊夫，他饒有興趣地看著你。 

 

 「嘿萊安農！」他突然說道，聲音很大，但沒有喊叫。 “你能請這位女士喝一杯嗎？請我來。”

 

 「謝謝你。」你回覆他。他微笑著示意你坐下。 “如果你感到孤獨，請隨意加入。”

 

 你小心翼翼地滑到克萊夫旁邊的凳子上。 Rhiannon 立即過來接受您的訂單。當她拿著你的杯子回來時，克萊夫用他的杯子碰了碰你的杯子。 「為好夥伴乾杯，」他喝了一口說。

 
 當你走近酒吧時，克萊夫指著他旁邊的座位。 “晚上來吧。加入。萊安農，你能給菲利普斯喝一杯嗎？請我來。”

 

 萊安農 (Rhiannon) 接受了您點的飲料，您和克萊夫 (Clive) 一起在吧台坐下，她將飲料端給您。克萊夫把你的玻璃杯弄得吱吱作響。

 

 「祝身體健康，」他說著，喝了一口杯子。

 
 

 

 
 談話間隙，克萊夫一邊喝著酒，一邊對你微笑。在你意識到之前，他又點了一杯飲料，放在你面前。

 
 克萊夫又喝了一口酒，無論是在麥克塔菲餐廳的輕鬆氛圍中還是在談話中的沉默時刻，他都感到很放鬆。在你意識到之前，他又點了一杯飲料，放在你面前。

 
 

 
 

 
 克萊夫喝完酒，轉向你。 “小女士，你呢？還想換一個嗎？很高興能和一位美麗迷人的女孩共度美好時光。”

 

 你回頭看看克萊夫。你已經因為喝了酒而感到有點頭暈。他對你微笑，臉上帶著令人放鬆的微笑。 
 
 
 克萊夫喝完酒，轉向你。 「啤酒之王，」他說。 “還有男人的飲料。再來一杯？”

 

 你看看自己的杯子，還有半滿。你已經感覺有點頭暈了，你有一種感覺，克萊夫可能不僅僅意味著一個。 
 
 
 

**行動**

passages.name: McTafeysBarBuy
passages.content:
You walk up to the bar and wait for Rhiannon to come over to you. “Any chance I could get a six pack to go?” you ask her.

 

 She looks at you, curiously. "Not sure you want a bottle of wine? Or even some mixers?"

 

 You shake your head. "Just the beer."

 

 Rhiannon smiles at you and shrugs. "Must be a special guy if you're buying him beer."

 

 She turns around to the fridge and squats down to get your order. You notice a few of the male regulars glancing down as her skirt rides up and reveals a bit more of her pale thighs.

 

 Unaware or unconcerned, Rhiannon shortly stands up and turns to you, putting the six pack on the bar. You thank her and pay for the beer. She takes the money and moves to serve another customer.

 You walk up to the bar and wait for Rhiannon to come over to you. “Any chance I could get a six pack to go?” you ask her.

 

 She smiles and nods. “Sure,” she says. She turns around to the fridge and squats down to get your order. You glance down and can’t help but note how her tight skirt rides up her legs, revealing a large amount of pale thigh.

 

 You return your gaze to her eyes when she gets back up and puts the six-pack on the bar. You thank her and pay for the beer. She takes the money and moves to serve another customer.

**Actions**

<>

 你搖搖頭。 “只要啤酒。”

 

 萊安農對你微笑並聳聳肩。 “如果你給他買啤酒，他一定是個特別的人。”

 

 她轉身走向冰箱，蹲下來拿你點的菜。你注意到，當她的裙子捲起並露出更多蒼白的大腿時，一些男性常客低頭看了一眼。

 

 萊安農沒有意識到或不在乎，很快站了起來，轉向你，把六塊肌放在吧台上。你感謝她並付了啤酒錢。她拿了錢，去服務另一位顧客。

passages.name: McTafeysBarAnnieJennifer
passages.content:
Annie looks at you as you approach her. Her expression doesn’t change, but you notice an eyebrow raise. “Jennifer,” she murmurs.

 

 You stare down at Annie, noting her demeanour is striking different. As long as you’ve knownher, you can’t remember a time where hasn’t smiled. Now though, in the form of Jennifer, she is expressionless. 

 
 Annie looks at you as you approach her. Her expression doesn’t change, but you notice an eyebrow raise. “Jennifer,” she murmurs.

 

 If she’s nervous about seeing your girlfriend, you wouldn’t know it. The only thing that seems off is her demeanour. As long as you’ve known Annie, you can’t remember a time where hasn’t smiled. Now though, in the form of Jennifer, she is expressionless. 

 

 Annie looks at you with wary eyes, but says nothing to dissuade you from staying.

passages.name: McTafeysBarAnniePubDate
passages.content:
You go to the bar and order a bottle of white wine with two glasses. Rhiannon gives you a friendly smile as you order. Returning to the table, you take a seat. She smiles when you hand her the drink and raises her glass towards you. "To some good old fashioned girl fun."

 

 She chinks your glass and takes a sip. “Thanks . It's been so long I've had a natter over some chardy.”

 

 You give her a polite smile and can't help but give her an appraisal. Her long legs strut out at the side of the table, her shoulders are tanned and smooth. Her long blonde hair sparkles in light. She objectively gorgeous. You can't help but feel envious.

 

 The lull in the conversation starts to become obvious. You consider how best to ease the tension.
 
 
 You go to the bar and order a couple of drinks, trying to ignore the look the barmaid Rhiannon gives you when you order. Returning to Annie’s table, you take a seat. She again smiles at you when you hand her the drink.

 

 She takes a sip and runs the tip of her tongue over her glossy lips. “Thank you,” she says to you. “You’re such a gentleman, .”

 

 You stare at her and you can’t help but glance at her long legs strutting out at the side of the table and her tanned bare shoulders. Her long blonde hair spills down over them, sparkling in the overhead lamps. She is objectively beautiful. And she knows it too.

 

 The lull in the conversation starts to become obvious. You consider how best to ease the tension.
 
 
 
 
 You go to the bar and order a bottle for you and Annie. The barmaid Rhiannon is pleasant. "Another girl's night out?" she asks you.

 

 You take the bottle back to the table and sit down. Annie smiles warmly at you, taking a sip of her drink.
 
 
 You go to the bar and order a round for you and Annie. The barmaid Rhiannon is pleasant, but her eyes flick over to Annie as she serves you.

 

 You take the drinks back to the table and sit down. Annie smiles coyly at you, but says nothing.

 
 
 

 **Actions**

passages.name: AnniePubDate2
passages.content:
You quickly exit McTafey’s bar, getting only a few idly glances from men as you and Annie leave. You notice the look of disappointment on some of their faces.

 

 Outside night has set in on Avian Lane. Annie’s heels clip and clop on the concrete pavement, the only sound aside from the pigeons cooing in the dark street.

 

 “Just here,” Annie tells you, taking your attention away. She stops outside a small door in-between some shops, unassuming and nondescript.

 

 She giggles, unlocks the door and takes a step inside, gesturing for you to follow into the darkness.

 You quickly exit McTafey’s, feeling as though all eyes are upon you. Leaving with Annie. Jennifer is not exactly a social butterfly, but you make a list of everyone you saw in the bar, trying to work out if anyone would be talking to her.

 

 Outside night has set in on Avian Lane. Annie’s heels clip and clop on the concrete pavement, the only sound aside from the pigeons cooing in the dark street. You glance over to Jennifer’s apartment opposite, a light from the living room still on.

 

 “Just here,” Annie tells you, taking your attention away. She stops outside a small door in-between some shops, only a few buildings down from your girlfriend’s apartment.

 

 She flashes you a smile, unlocks the door and takes a step inside, gesturing for you to follow into the darkness.

passages.name: OrchidRoadMichelleDialogue0
passages.content:
Michelle eyes you darkly as you approach. “Yes?” she says, incensed by the interruption. 

 

 Sensing her disdain for you, you don't pursue conversation.
 
 
 
 
 ”Oh hi, Em!” Michelle brightly chirps when she sees you. “I didn’t know you were coming today! Have you seen the new dresses Evans has in store? How divine!”

 
 
 ”Oh hi ”, she says perkily when she sees you. “How are you?”
 
 
 Michelle smiles politely upon seeing you, but turns back to the window of the salon. In your current form, Michelle apparently doesn’t recognise you as someone she’d have a conversation with. 

 
 
 Michelle tries to focus on you, but her eyes keep wandering back to the Salon window.

 「喔嗨，艾姆！」米歇爾一看到你就高興地嘰嘰喳喳地叫起來。 “我不知道你今天會來！你看到埃文斯店裡的新衣服了嗎？太棒了！”

 
 
 “哦嗨”，當她看到你時，她高興地說。 “你好嗎？”

 米歇爾試圖將注意力集中在你身上，但她的目光不斷地飄回沙龍的窗戶。

passages.name: Salon
passages.content:
**The Groomed Gal Salon**

[image: ./images/Salon.png]

The salon is small and narrow, smelling of hairspray and the scent of beauty products. A number of chairs face a mirror that runs down one side of the shop. Near the entrance is a comfy pink sofa and a glass coffee table, littered with magazines about fashion, make-up and home decorating.

 

 
 
 
 
Rhiannon, a young woman with dark red hair and striking green eyes, stands behind the counter reading a magazine. She looks up and slyly smiles as you enter. “Oh, hey Jen!" she says. “You here for a trim and pamper, just a natter, or maybe a bit of both?”

 

 You stare back at the young woman, noting how familiar she seems with you. This could be a good opportunity to learn about Jennifer, but do you really want to put yourself through a salon experience to do that?

 
 
Rhiannon, a young woman with dark red hair and striking green eyes, stands behind the counter reading a magazine. She looks up and smiles wickedly as you enter. “Oh, hey Annie!" she says. “My favourite customer. Fancy a pampering session?”

 

 You stare back at the young woman, noting how familiar she seems with you. This could be a good opportunity to learn some things from Annie’s perspective, but do you really want to put yourself through a salon experience to do that?
 
 
 
Rhiannon, a young woman with dark red hair and striking green eyes, stands behind the counter reading a magazine. She looks up and slyly smiles as you enter. “Oh, hello,” she says. “Can I help you?”

 
 
 
 
 
Sophie, the well-groomed middle-aged manager of the salon is sweeping the floor as you enter. “Jennifer,” she says curtly, her sharp eyes looking directly into yours. “How can I help you?”

 

 You stare back at the middle-aged woman, noting a clear cautiousness, but there’s familiarity there. This could be a good opportunity to learn about Jennifer, but do you really want to put yourself through a salon experience to do that?

 
 
Sophie, the well-groomed middle-age
passages.content_zh:
**美少女沙龍**

[圖：./images/Salon.png]

沙龍又小又窄，瀰漫著髮膠和美容產品的香味。許多椅子麵向一面鏡子，鏡子沿著商店的一側延伸。入口附近有一張舒適的粉紅色沙發和一張玻璃咖啡桌，上面散落著時尚、化妝和家居裝飾的雜誌。

 

 
 
 
 
瑞安農 (Rhiannon) 是一位有著深紅色頭髮和醒目的綠色眼睛的年輕女子，她站在櫃檯後面閱讀雜誌。當你進來時，她抬起頭來，狡猾地微笑。 「哦，嘿，珍！」她說。 “你來這裡是為了修剪和呵護，只是閒聊，或者兩者兼而有之？”

 

 你回頭看著那個年輕女子，注意到她看起來對你有多熟悉。這可能是了解珍妮佛的好機會，但您真的想透過沙龍體驗來做到這一點嗎？

 
 
瑞安農 (Rhiannon) 是一位有著深紅色頭髮和醒目的綠色眼睛的年輕女子，她站在櫃檯後面閱讀雜誌。當你進來時，她抬起頭來，邪惡地微笑著。 「哦，嘿安妮！」她說。 “我最喜歡的顧客。想要享受一次護理服務嗎？”

 

 你回頭看著那個年輕女子，注意到她看起來對你有多熟悉。這可能是從安妮的角度學習一些東西的好機會，但你真的想透過沙龍體驗來做到這一點嗎？
 
 
 
瑞安農 (Rhiannon) 是一位有著深紅色頭髮和醒目的綠色眼睛的年輕女子，她站在櫃檯後面閱讀雜誌。當你進來時，她抬起頭來，狡猾地微笑。 「哦，你好，」她說。 “我可以幫你嗎？”

 
 
 
 
 
當你走進去時，沙龍裡穿著考究的中年經理蘇菲正在掃地。 「詹妮弗，」她簡短地說，她銳利的眼睛直視著你的眼睛。 “我怎麼幫你？”

 

 你回頭看著那位中年婦女，明顯看出了謹慎，但又有些熟悉。這可能是了解珍妮佛的好機會，但您真的想透過沙龍體驗來做到這一點嗎？

 
 
蘇菲，打扮得體的中年人

passages.name: SalonHairDye
passages.content:
“I was looking for a change,” you tell Rhiannon. “Maybe try blonde?”

 

 Her green eyes sparkle. “I think that would look great for you. Why don’t you take a seat.”

 

 You sit down in one of the salon chairs as Rhiannon loads a number of products onto a cart and wheels them over. “Have you done this before?” she asks, as she puts a cover over your body. 

 

 You shake your head. Rhiannon gives you a crooked smile. “Oh don’t worry. You’re going to love it.”

 

 Over the next hour, Rhiannon begins to treat your hair. You relax as she washes your locks, chatting away about anything that comes into her mind. Before long, you find yourself talking about school, the teachers and the other students. She has a wicked sense of humour and a saucy laugh, but is aware enough to steer away from subjects that make you feel uncomfortable.

 

 On occasion, you glance at yourself in the mirror, seeing glimpses of the changes of the dyes Rhiannon is applying. But it’s only when she finishes washing your hair for a second time and turns you back to the mirror that you see the extent of the transformation she’s made to you.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 

 “I was looking to get my hair dyed” you tell Sophie. “I was thinking blonde”

 

 Sophie’s eyes look at you, a hint of eyebrows lifting, but she retains her composure. “We can certainly help with that,” she says. “Take a seat.”

 

 You sit down in a chair as Sophie loads products onto a cart and pushes them alongside you. “It’s not uncommon for girls your age to experiment with their hair,” she says. “A change is as good as a rest, after all. Have you done this before?”

 

 You shake your head. Sophie smiles sympathetically. “Don’t worry. I’m sure you’ll look great as a blonde.”

 

 Time passes quickly as Sophie washes your hair and applies the dyes to your locks. She makes polite conversation, asking how school is going and what your plans are after you graduate. You learn that her daughter, Rhiannon, left Charlton Upper Sch
passages.content_zh:
「我一直在尋求改變，」你告訴萊安農。 “也許嘗試一下金發女郎？”

 

 她的綠色眼睛閃閃發光。 “我覺得這對你來說會很好看。為什麼不坐下來呢？”

 

 你坐在一張沙龍椅上，瑞安農 (Rhiannon) 將一些產品裝到推車上，然後將它們推過去。 「你以前做過這個嗎？」她邊問邊蓋上被子給你。 

 

 你搖搖頭。萊安農給了你一個扭曲的微笑。 “哦，別擔心。你會喜歡它的。”

 

 在接下來的一個小時裡，Rhiannon 開始照顧您的頭髮。當她洗你的頭髮時，你放鬆下來，聊她想到的任何事情。不久之後，你發現自己在談論學校、老師和其他學生。她有一種邪惡的幽默感和俏皮的笑聲，但她有足夠的意識來避開那些讓你感到不舒服的話題。

 

 有時，你會照照鏡子，瞥見萊安農正在塗抹的染料的變化。但只有當她第二次洗完你的頭髮，把你轉回鏡子前時，你才能看到她對你的改變有多大。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 

 「我想染頭髮」你告訴蘇菲。 “我以為是金髮女郎”

 

 蘇菲的眼睛看著你，眉毛微微揚起，但她保持著鎮定。 “我們當然可以提供幫助，”她說。 “坐吧。”

 

 您坐在椅子上，蘇菲將產品裝載到手推車上，然後將它們推到您旁邊。 「對於你這個年紀的女孩來說，嘗試自己的頭髮並不罕見，」她說。 “畢竟，改變和休息一樣好。你以前這樣做過嗎？”

 

 你搖搖頭。蘇菲同情地微笑。 “別擔心。我相信你作為金發女郎會看起來很棒。”

 

 當蘇菲為你洗頭、為你的頭髮上染髮劑時，時間過得很快。她進行禮貌的交談，詢問學校進展如何以及畢業後的計劃。您得知她的女兒萊安農 (Rhiannon) 離開了查爾頓高中

passages.name: SalonHairDyeResult
passages.content:
You stare at your reflection, barely recognising your sister, Emily. Your hair is golden blonde, radiant and glistening. But the difference it makes to you is deeper. Instead of a mousy bookish girl, the blonde hair makes you look carefree and bubbly. Whereas previously Emily would have looked at home in a library, the girl in the mirror looks like she should be cheering alongside a football game.

 

 “It really suits you” Rhiannon tells you. “I’m sure the boys at school will love it.”

 

 You nod, politely. Not sure if you want to consider the ramifications of what you thought would just be a colour change. You thank Rhiannon, pay her, and leave the store, aware of your newly dyed hair swaying as you walk.

 “Look at you,” Sophie says. “You look like a different person.”

 

 You nod, politely. Not sure if you want to consider the ramifications of what you thought would just be a colour change. You thank Sophie, pay her, and leave the store, aware of your newly dyed hair swaying as you walk.

passages.content_zh:
你盯著自己的倒影，差點認不出你的妹妹艾蜜莉。你的頭髮是金色的，容光煥發，閃閃發光。但它帶給你的改變是更深層的。金色的頭髮讓你看起來不再是個陰鬱的書呆子女孩，而是無憂無慮、活潑開朗。以前艾蜜莉會在家裡的圖書館裡看，而鏡中的女孩看起來應該是在為足球比賽歡呼。

 

 「它真的很適合你」Rhiannon 告訴你。 “我相信學校裡的男孩們會喜歡它。”

 

 你點點頭，很有禮貌。不確定您是否要考慮您認為只是顏色變化的後果。你感謝了萊安農，付了錢，然後離開了商店，注意到你新染的頭髮在你走路時搖曳。

passages.name: SpaTreatment
passages.content:
“I was told that you offer spa treatments?” you ask Sophie.

 

 “Oh yes,” she demurs. “It includes a manicure, pedicure, facial cleanse and exfoliating. You will feel like a new you! Please, take a seat.”

 

 The middle-aged salon manager throws a cowl over you once you sit down. You watch her in the mirror as she wheels over a portable basin, then gently pulls your neck back so you’re looking up at the ceiling.

 

 Sophie goes to work on you. She washes your head, then rubs your face down and applies a cream that is cold and sets quickly in your skin.

 

 Turning her attention to your hands, you feel the rough sensation of having your nails filed and polished. You wince as she works.

 

 
 ”So, how are you finding school?” she asks, trying to distract you from your discomfort.

 

 You only give Sophie a few noncommittal comments, but it’s enough for her to run with. She begins to tell you about her daughter, Rhiannon, and how she used to get in trouble all the time at Charlton. “Nothing significant,” Sophie clarifies. “But god rest my mother, she inherited her sharp tongue. Oh, the language that used to come from her father when Mr. Stitch used to ring. Thank the lord he and Mr. McTafey get on so well, or who knows what would have become of her.”

 
 "So, how are you finding working at Thompson's Repairs?" she asks, trying to distract you from your discomfort.

 

 "It's okay," you reply. "Hard to be the only woman in the office. Except for Annie, that is."

 

 She nods. "Charlton is a very unique place. You'll find that some of the men are a little old-fashioned. Just remember to flutter your eyes and smile. A woman can get a long way around here by appeasing the men in charge."

 

 She smiles to you through the mirror. You politely return her expression.

 

 "This treatment will help with them," she continues. "You're already fitting right in here. A little beauty can go a long way. And I'm sure there's an eligible bachelor just waiting in the wings for
passages.content_zh:
「我聽說你們提供水療護理？」你問蘇菲。

 

 「哦，是的，」她反對道。 “它包括修甲、修腳、面部清潔和去角質。您會感覺煥然一新！請坐。”

 

 當你坐下後，中年沙龍經理就會為你蓋上頭巾。你在鏡子裡看著她滾過一個便攜式臉盆，然後輕輕地把你的脖子向後拉，這樣你就能抬頭看著天花板。

 

 蘇菲去為你做事。她幫你洗頭，然後擦你的臉，塗上一種冰涼的霜，很快就能在你的皮膚上凝固。

 

 將她的注意力轉向你的雙手，你會感受到銼指甲和拋光的粗糙感覺。當她工作時，你皺起了眉頭。

 

 
 「那麼，你找到學校怎麼樣了？」她問道，試圖分散你對不適的注意力。

 

 你只給了蘇菲一些不置可否的評論，但這足以讓她接受。她開始向你講述她的女兒萊安農(Rhiannon)，以及她在查爾頓總是惹上麻煩。 「沒什麼重要的，」蘇菲澄清。 「但是上帝安息，我的母親，她遺傳了她尖刻的舌頭。哦，當史迪奇先生按鈴時，她父親的語言就來自於她。感謝上帝，他和麥克塔菲先生相處得這麼好，否則誰知道她會變成什麼樣子。”

 
 「那麼，你在 Thompson's Repair 的工作怎麼樣？」她問道，試圖分散你對不適的注意力。

 

 「沒關係，」你回答。 “很難成為辦公室裡唯一的女性。除了安妮之外。”

 

 她點點頭。 “查爾頓是一個非常獨特的地方。你會發現有些男人有點守舊。只要記住眨眨眼睛並微笑即可。通過安撫負責的男人，女人可以在這裡走得很遠。”

 

 她透過鏡子對你微笑。你禮貌地回應她的表情。

 

 「這種治療將對他們有所幫助，」她繼續說道。 「你已經很適合這裡了。一點美貌可以大有幫助。而且我確信有一個合格的單身漢正在等待著

passages.name: SalonJennifer
passages.content:
Sophie ushers you into a plush chair behind her polished station. The air smells of lavender and warmed oils as she drapes a towel around your neck. Each brushstroke of mask feels odd. Like a ceremony for someone else’s face.

 

 She smooths your brow with practiced restraint. “How is work at Evans’, Jennifer?” Her tone is polite but measured. “It must feel… different from being a cheerleader.”

 

 In the mirror Sophie’s eyes harden ever so slightly. “Few of you squad girls end up as a shop girl. But you always did march to your own drum, didn’t you?”

 

 “Rhiannon still speaks warmly of you. Well, you and Skye. I suppose you haven’t kept in touch? Skye’s at that esteemed university now. Quite above your current station.”

 

 You feel tense. Almost angry. This isn’t the relaxing experience you imagined. In fact, it feels like you’re being lectured. Silence fills the space as Sophie applies a gentle moisturizer. Her touch cold.

 

 Finally she steps back, holding a hand-mirror before you. The reflection shows cheeks flushed with health, lips curved in a softer shape. You feel a tender stir of something you haven’t felt in ages. A warm sense of pride, maybe?

 

 Sophie stands behind you, looking at your reaction. “I hope this… brings you some pleasure,” she says quietly. “Lord knows, things haven’t given much to you lately, have they?”

 

 You meet her eyes in the glass, looking for some sort of kindness in her expression. But her raised eyebrows reveal there’s nothing there except judgement. 

 <>
 
 You sink into the leather-backed chair as Sophie fastens a silky cape around your shoulders. The scent of chamomile shampoo drifts up as she lifts your blonde hair and begins to tease through a lightweight conditioner.

 

 When she reaches behind her for a towel, Sophie glances at you in the mirror. “You know, Mr. Thompson’s been delighted with business lately,” she says, voice smooth. “Jeanette mentioned how helpful you were last month.”

 

 You force a smile.
passages.content_zh:
索菲带您坐到她抛光的工作站后面的一把豪华椅子上。當她把毛巾掛在你脖子上時，空氣中瀰漫著薰衣草和溫熱油的味道。面具的每一筆都感覺很奇怪。就像為別人的面子舉行的儀式。

 

 她熟練地克制著撫平你的額頭。 「詹妮弗，埃文斯的工作怎麼樣？」她的語氣禮貌但有分寸。 “這一定感覺……與當啦啦隊長不同。”

 

 鏡子裡索菲的眼神變得更加堅定。 “你們班的女孩中很少有人最終成為店員。但你們總是按照自己的鼓點前進，不是嗎？”

 

 「雷安農仍然熱情地談論你。好吧，你和斯凱。我想你們還沒有保持聯繫嗎？斯凱現在在那所受人尊敬的大學。比你現在的地位高得多。”

 

 你感到緊張。差點生氣了。這不是您想像中的輕鬆體驗。事實上，感覺就像是在聽別人說教。蘇菲塗抹溫和的保濕霜，整個空間一片寂靜。她的觸感冰涼。

 

 最後她退後一步，在你面前舉著一面鏡子。倒影顯示，臉頰因健康而紅潤，嘴唇呈現柔和的弧度。你感受到一種多年未曾感受過的溫柔的觸動。也許是一種溫暖的驕傲？

 

 蘇菲站在你身後，看著你的反應。 「我希望這……能帶給你一些樂趣，」她平靜地說。 “上帝知道，最近事情並沒有給你帶來太多好處，不是嗎？”

 

 你在玻璃裡對視著她的眼睛，在她的表情中尋找著某種善意。但她揚起的眉毛顯示除了判斷之外什麼都沒有。 

 <>
 
 當蘇菲在你的肩膀上綁上一件絲質斗篷時，你坐進了皮背椅。當她撩起你的金髮，並開始用輕盈的護髮素梳理時，洋甘菊洗髮精的香味飄然而至。

 

 當蘇菲伸手去拿毛巾時，她看了一眼鏡子裡的你。 「你知道，湯普森先生最近生意興隆，」她聲音平穩地說。 “珍妮特提到你上個月有多樂於助人。”

 

 你強顏歡笑。

passages.name: SchoolAVCupboard
passages.content:
**Charlton School - AV Cupboard**

[image: ./images/School_AV_Cupboard.png]

The cupboard is small and cramped. It’s full of old CRT televisions on wheeled stands and clunky VHS players. The walls are lined with shelves with stacks of old tapes bearing handwritten labels, all referencing documentaries and educational TV shows.

**Actions**

<>

<>
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - AV 櫃子**

[圖：./images/School_AV_Cupboard.png]

櫥櫃又小又狹窄。裡面擺滿了帶輪子的老式 CRT 電視機和笨重的 VHS 播放器。牆上排列著架子，架子上放著成堆的舊磁帶，上面有手寫的標籤，所有磁帶都引用了紀錄片和教育電視節目。

**行動**

<>

<>

passages.name: SchoolAVCupboardInvestigation
passages.content:
Drawing down on the well of arcane energy within you, you focus your affinity on the television that Dennis brings into the biology room.

 

 A manic crackle of sound jolts you back. You stare at the screen, dumbfounded. Something stares back out at you, grinning. Your mind struggles to comprehend it, but you are sure that whatever that inhuman crackle sounded like, it was the sound of this being laughing at you.

 

 The yellow burn of magic outlines the frenzied ghost in the TV. It appears to roll up and down, like watching an out-of-tune TV station. Though made up entirely of jagged lines that flicker and jolt, you can see its teeth snarling out at you.

 

 Even more concerningly, the two-dimensional screen somehow is pressing towards you.

 

 **Actions**

 
 
 <>

 <>

 
 <>
 
 You try to draw down on the pool of energy within you, but instead of feeling the radiance of magic, all you feel is empty.

 

 Staring back at the television that Dennis wheels into the biology room, all you see is your reflection staring back. Nothing seems out of the ordinary.

 

 The realisation comes quickly. In order to use magic, you need to be a girl.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 
 
 
 You take a step back, closing your eyes to concentrate in spite of the growing terror within your being. The laughter tries to distract you, but you manage to summon a flow of energy from your inner reserves. 

 

 
 “I cause this object with power beyond its power,” you whisper. “Unseen and unheard, its effects will be felt by all but me.”

 

 You open your eyes to see the unmoved television bearing down on you. The creature is centimetres from your face, but it's ceased snarling. Instead, it eyes you curiously, impish and surprised.

 

 The dimensions of the room slowly recede into reality. You hear the creature chuckle as it looks out at you, but it does nothing more.

 
 “I cause this object with power beyond its power,” you say. “Unseen and unheard, its effects will be felt by none.”

 

 You o
passages.content_zh:
你利用體內的奧術能量，將你的親和力集中在丹尼斯帶進生物室的電視上。

 

 一陣狂躁的爆裂聲將你驚醒。你盯著螢幕，目瞪口呆。有什麼東西回望著你，微笑著。你的大腦難以理解它，但你確信無論那非人的爆裂聲聽起來是什麼樣，那都是這個生物在嘲笑你的聲音。

 

 黃色的魔法燃燒勾勒出電視中瘋狂的鬼魂。它看起來上下滾動，就像看跑調的電視台一樣。儘管它完全由閃爍和搖晃的鋸齒狀線條組成，但你可以看到它的牙齒向你咆哮。

 

 更令人擔憂的是，二維螢幕不知何故正向你逼近。

 

 **行動**

 
 
 <>

 <>

 
 <>
 
 你試圖汲取體內的能量，但你並沒有感受到魔法的光芒，反而感到空虛。

 

 回頭看著丹尼斯推進生物室的電視機，你看到的只是你的倒影。似乎沒有什麼異常。

 

 認識來得很快。為了使用魔法，你需要成為一個女孩。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 你後退了一步，閉上眼睛集中註意力，儘管你內心的恐懼與日俱增。笑聲試圖分散你的注意力，但你設法從內在儲備中召喚出一股能量流。 

 

 
 「我讓這個物體擁有了超越其力量的力量，」你低聲說道。 “除了我之外，所有人都會感受到其看不見、聞所未聞的影響。”

 

 你睜開眼睛，看到一動也不動的電視正朝你走來。這個生物距離你的臉只有幾厘米，但它已經停止了咆哮。相反，它會好奇地、頑皮地、驚訝地看著你。

 

 房間的尺寸慢慢回歸現實。你聽到這個生物看著你時咯咯地笑，但它什麼也沒做。

 
 「我用超出其能力的力量來創造這個物體，」你說。 “看不見、聞所未聞，它的影響無人能感受到。”

 

 你哦

passages.name: SchoolAVRoom
passages.content:
**Charlton School - AV Room**

[image: ./images/School_AV_Room.png]

Your eyes need a moment to adjust as you step into a room. The only light comes from a dim lamp and the very edges of the windows, which are otherwise covered with black paper and duct tape. Cracked movie posters from old 1940s war movies decorate the walls and the benches are covered in film projectors in states of disrepair.

 
Dennis sleeps behind the teacher's desk where you left him, looking at peace as he breathes shallowly.

 <>
 <> 
 
 
 
*You look at Dennis for a few seconds. If you really wanted to know what went on in those boys’ classes, Dennis would do. If you could get your hands on something of his, something personal. Something to power the amulet. Getting him somewhere private wouldn't be a challenge.*

 
 
 

**Actions**

<>

 
 Unlock the AV Cupboard - *It's too late to continue your investigation.*

 
 <>

 

[[Leave the classroom->SchoolCorridor]]
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - AV 室**

[圖片：./images/School_AV_Room.png]

當您走進房間時，您的眼睛需要一段時間來適應。唯一的光線來自昏暗的燈和窗戶的邊緣，否則窗戶上都覆蓋著黑紙和膠帶。牆上裝飾著 20 世紀 40 年代老戰爭電影中破裂的電影海報，長凳上擺滿了年久失修的電影放映機。

 
丹尼斯睡在你離開他的老師桌後面，淺淺地呼吸著，看起來很平靜。

 <>
 <>
 
 
 
*你看了丹尼斯幾秒鐘。如果你真的想知道那些男孩的班級裡發生了什麼，丹尼斯就可以。如果你能得到他的一些東西，一些私人的東西。為護身符提供能量的東西。讓他去一個私人的地方並不是一個挑戰。 *

 
 
 

**行動**

<>

 
 解鎖 AV 櫃子 - *現在已經太晚了，無法繼續調查。 *

 
 <>

 

[[離開教室->學校走廊]]

passages.name: SchoolAVRoomDennisDialogue0
passages.content:
When Dennis sees you, he focuses his attention singularly on what he’s doing. It’s clear he doesn’t want to talk to you.

 Dennis looks flustered as you approach him. “Hi Emily,” he mumbles. 

 
 
 
*You look at Dennis for a few seconds. If you really wanted to know what went on in those boys’ classes, Dennis would do. If you could get your hands on something of his, something personal. Something to power the amulet. Getting him somewhere private wouldn't be a challenge.*

 

 Dennis jumps as you step towards him, the glow of the TV flickering across his face. The moment he sees you, his whole body stiffens. He grips the arm of the chair, knuckles white.

 

 “Jake,” he mutters, barely audible. His eyes flick nervously toward the screen, then stay locked there, as if moving them might invite trouble.

 

 You can feel the tension radiating from him. He doesn’t want to talk. He doesn’t want to be here with you.

 

 Not wanting to torture him, you decide to leave him alone.
 
 
 Dennis continues to work, idly listening for what you have to say.

 

 **Actions**

 當你接近丹尼斯時，他看起來很慌張。 「嗨艾米麗，」他咕噥道。 

 
 
 
*你看了丹尼斯幾秒鐘。如果你真的想知道那些男孩的班級裡發生了什麼，丹尼斯就可以。如果你能得到他的一些東西，一些私人的東西。為護身符提供能量的東西。讓他去一個私人的地方並不是一個挑戰。 *

 

 當你走向丹尼斯時，他跳了起來，電視的光芒在他臉上閃爍。當他看到你的那一刻，他整個人都僵硬了。他握住椅子的扶手，指節發白。

 

 「傑克，」他低聲說道，聲音幾乎聽不見。他的眼睛緊張地掃向螢幕，然後一直鎖定在那裡，彷彿移動它們可能會帶來麻煩。

 

 你能感覺到他身上散發出的緊張氣氛。他不想說話。他不想和你在一起。

 

 你不想折磨他，決定放過他。

passages.name: SchoolAVRoomTransformDennis
passages.content:
The amulet tingles underneath your clothes. You clutch on to the photo of Emily that Dennis loves. *Here we go again*.

 The amulet tingles underneath your clothes. You clutch on to the receipt from Emily's date with Dennis. *Here we go again*.
 
 
 
 “From this item, I see you in your time of need. Now form shape to the person I see within.”

 

 You picture Dennis in your mind. Short and scrawny, with a severe case of acne. His thick-rimmed glasses. His clothes look dated, worn and ill-fitting. Nervousness and anxiety always painting his face.

 

 The air around you ripples and the magic rushes out from within, enveloping your body and illuminating the darkened room with its light.

 

 Your bones begin to crack and stretch with a deep, grinding pain. Through the agony you feel your shoulders growing, arms lengthening and legs stretching out, muscles pulsating as they are stretched by a mystic force.

 

 Clothes writhe across your skin, seams tearing and reforming. The swish of your skirt constricts, forming around your legs and stretching down towards the floor. The air feels different against you. Less skin exposed, heavier cotton, coarser fabric, a collar of a shirt now snug against your throat.

 

 Your chest contracts, and suddenly, you squeal in pain as you feel your loins burn as skin pushes out and grows into a male member. You hear your squeal, a high-pitched tone but nevertheless lower than what it was before.

 

 The magic shudders, then releases. The noise in the room returns to just the quiet volume of the film playing on the TV. You glance at it, seeing its muted reflection of the door. Dennis stares back, dressed in a Charlton School jumper and rough uniform trousers. Narrow-faced, average build, the slouch of a boy who hides behind his books.

 

 You take a deep breath. The reflection matches your action. You try to smile to validate this new reality, but you find yourself filled with anxiety. Something about this form makes thoughts of relief
passages.content_zh:
護身符在你的衣服下面刺痛。你緊緊抓住丹尼斯喜歡的艾蜜莉的照片。 *又來了*。

 護身符在你的衣服下面刺痛。你緊緊抓住艾蜜莉和丹尼斯約會的收據。 *又來了*。
 
 
 
 “通過這個物品，我在你需要的時候看到了你。現在為我在裡面看到的人塑造形狀。”

 

 你在腦海中想著丹尼斯。身材矮小，骨瘦如柴，痤瘡很嚴重。他的厚框眼鏡。他的衣服看起來過時、破舊且不合身。他的臉上總是寫滿了緊張和焦慮。

 

 周圍的空氣波動，魔法從內部湧出，包裹住你的身體，照亮了黑暗的房間。

 

 你的骨頭開始斷裂並伸展，伴隨著深深的磨痛。在痛苦中，你會感覺到肩膀在增長，手臂在伸長，雙腿在伸展，肌肉在被神秘力量拉伸時脈動。

 

 衣服在你的皮膚上翻滾，接縫處撕裂並重新變形。你的裙子咻咻地收縮，圍繞著你的腿，一直延伸到地板上。空氣對你來說感覺不一樣。更少的皮膚暴露，更重的棉質，更粗糙的面料，襯衫領子現在緊貼著你的喉嚨。

 

 你的胸部收縮，突然，你痛苦地尖叫，因為你感覺你的腰部被灼燒，皮膚被推出並長成男性成員。你聽到自己的尖叫聲，雖然聲音很高，但比之前的聲音低。

 

 魔法顫抖，然後釋放。房間裡的噪音又恢復到電視播放電影的安靜音量。你瞥了一眼，看到了門的柔和倒影。丹尼斯回頭凝視著他，他穿著查爾頓學校的毛衣和粗糙的製服褲子。臉型窄，身材中等，像躲在書本後面的男孩那樣無精打采。

 

 你深吸一口氣。反射與你的行動相符。你試著微笑來證實這個新的現實，但你發現自己充滿了焦慮。這種形式的某些東西讓人感到寬慰

passages.name: SchoolBasement
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Basement**

[image: ./images/School_Basement.png]

The basement is active with the constant hum of generators and rushing of water through pipes. Only a few bulbs give light to the otherwise dark place. In the corner, back behind some bulky machinery, is a small alcove.

You look around the dank and dark basement, waiting for your eyes to slowly adjust to the meagre amount of lighting. The sounds of electricity and plumbing are overbearing. You look around, seeing little of interest other than the alcove halfway down the long stretch of wires and piping. Anything within the alcove is hidden from view.

**Actions**

<>

<>
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - 地下室**

[圖片：./images/School_Basement.png]

地下室充滿了發電機不斷的嗡嗡聲和水管中的奔湧聲。只有幾個燈泡照亮了原本黑暗的地方。在角落裡，在一些笨重的機器後面，有一個小壁龕。

你環顧潮濕黑暗的地下室，等待眼睛慢慢適應微弱的光線。電力和管道的聲音令人難以忍受。你環顧四周，除了長長的電線和管道中間的凹室之外，幾乎看不到什麼有趣的東西。壁龕內的任何東西都隱藏在視線之外。

**行動**

<>

<>

passages.name: SchoolBasementAlcovePhotos
passages.content:
You reach up and unpin the photos of Heather from the wall, putting them in your satchel. You are certain that Olaf will realise, but hope he doesn’t have the smarts to link the theft to you.

Glancing at the photos, you ponder what to do with them. Heather wouldn't like even knowing they exist, let alone you having them. You could always burn them at home and free her from someone else using them against her.

Or could you use them against her yourself?

**Actions**

<>

<>
passages.content_zh:
你伸手從牆上取下希瑟的照片，把它們放進你的書包裡。你確信奧拉夫會意識到這一點，但希望他不要聰明地將盜竊案與你聯繫起來。

看了一眼照片，你就會思考如何處理它們。希瑟甚至不喜歡知道它們的存在，更不用說你擁有它們了。你總是可以在家裡燒掉它們，這樣她就不會被其他人用它們來對付她。

或者你自己可以用它們來對付她嗎？

**行動**

<>

<>

passages.name: SchoolBasementAlcove
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Basement Alcove**

[image: ./images/School_Basement_Alcove.png]

The alcove is as dark and cramped as the rest of the basement. The constant hum of electricity is only punctuated by gushing water through the mess of piping. A cot with a stained mattress is pushed against the wall.

 
 
 You peer around the corner of the alcove, only entering when you are satisfied no one else is here. The pungent smell of cleaning products fills the air. Buckets are thrown around haphazardly, each still full of dirty water that looks like it’s sat resting for a long time. The constant deep thumping of pistons echoes down the piping that runs down the back of the alcove, drowning out any other sounds you might hear.

 

 The stains on the mattress smell of things you’d rather not consider. Rust and discolouration covers the surface of the iron cot frame. You’re surprised it hasn’t collapsed through corrosion.

 

 Aside from a number of drawers and metal cupboards, one other thing takes your interest. Pinned above the bed on the wall is a number of photos. From the brief glance you give them, some are old. Some are new.

 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - 地下室壁龕**

[圖片：./images/School_Basement_Alcove.png]

凹室和地下室的其他部分一樣黑暗和狹窄。持續不斷的電流嗡嗡聲只被雜亂的管道中湧出的水打斷。一張帶有污漬床墊的嬰兒床被推到牆上。

 
 
 你向壁龕的角落望去，只有當你確信這裡沒有其他人時才進入。空氣中瀰漫著清潔產品的刺鼻氣味。水桶隨意地扔得到處都是，每個水桶裡還裝滿了髒水，看起來像是放置了很長時間的水。活塞持續不斷的低沉撞擊聲在壁龕後面的管道中迴響，淹沒了您可能聽到的任何其他聲音。

 

 床墊上的污漬散發出你不想考慮的東西的味道。鐵床框架的表面出現鏽蝕和變色。你會驚訝它沒有因腐蝕而倒塌。

 

 除了一些抽屜和金屬櫥櫃之外，還有一件事引起了您的興趣。床上方的牆上釘著許多照片。從你瞥見它們的那一刻起，有些已經很舊了。有些是新的。

 
 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>

passages.name: SchoolBasementOlafDialogue0
passages.content:
Olaf stares you down, waiting for you to explain yourself. You see his eyes wandering down your body, stopping to look at your legs and socks. He runs his dirty fingernails through his hair, tucking it behind his ears.

 
 Olaf eyes you curiously, scratching at his neck with a grimy fingernail. “Been taking more photos? Those last ones. All trollops. Good. Like trollops. Like stockings." He spits on the floor. “Got any new treasures for me today?”

 
 Olaf watches you, his half-formed grin absent. His lips press together and eyes dart toward the shadows, as though to make sure you’re alone. “Don’t see you down here often," he spits. "Basement’s not for jocks.”

 

 
 After a few minutes, you hear the faint jangling of keys over the deafening noise of the industrial machinery. Olaf rounds the corner, momentarily taken aback by seeing you. “You. Again,” he mutters. He brushes his greasy black hair back with his hand and wipes it down his dirty blue overalls. 

 
 After a few minutes, you hear the faint jangling of keys over the deafening noise of the industrial machinery. Olaf rounds the corner, momentarily taken aback by seeing you. “Mannet,” he mutters. He brushes his greasy black hair back with his hand and wipes it down his dirty blue overalls. "Got something for me?"

 

 He scratches at his neck with a grimy fingernail, leering. “You been keeping that camera busy?” His laugh is wet, phlegmy. “Got any new treasures for me today?”
 
 
 After a few minutes, you hear the faint jangling of keys over the deafening noise of the industrial machinery. When Olaf rounds the corner, his half-formed grin falters at the sight of you. His lips press together, yellow teeth disappearing as he straightens a little too quickly. “Quarterback” he rasps, voice hoarse but suddenly cautious. “Didn’t hear you coming.”

 

 He scratches at his neck with a dirty fingernail, eyes darting toward the shadows as though to make sure you’re alone. “Don’t see you down here. Basement’s not for jo
passages.content_zh:
奧拉夫俯視著你，等待你解釋自己的狀況。你看到他的目光掃過你的身體，停下來看看你的腿和襪子。他用骯髒的指甲梳理頭髮，把頭髮別到耳後。

 
 奧拉夫好奇地看著你，用骯髒的指甲搔著脖子。 「拍了更多照片嗎？最後那幾張。都是流浪漢。很好。就像流浪漢。就像長筒襪。」他吐在地板上。 “今天有新的寶物給我嗎？”

 
 奧拉夫看著你，臉上沒有半張笑容。他的嘴唇抿在一起，眼睛盯著陰影，彷彿要確定只有你一個人。 「不常在這裡見到你，」他吐槽。 “地下室不適合運動員。”

 

 
 幾分鐘後，你會聽到工業機械震耳欲聾的噪音中傳來微弱的鑰匙聲。奧拉夫轉過街角，看到你時立刻吃了一驚。 「你。又來了，」他低聲說。他用手將油膩的黑髮向後梳，然後用髒兮兮的藍色工裝褲擦拭。 

 
 幾分鐘後，你會聽到工業機械震耳欲聾的噪音中傳來微弱的鑰匙聲。奧拉夫轉過街角，看到你時立刻吃了一驚。 「曼內特，」他低聲說。他用手將油膩的黑髮向後梳，然後用髒兮兮的藍色工裝褲擦拭。 “有東西給我嗎？”

 

 他用骯髒的指甲搔著脖子，斜眼看著。 「你一直讓相機忙著嗎？」他的笑聲濕漉漉的，黏稠的。 “今天有新的寶物給我嗎？”
 
 
 幾分鐘後，你會聽到工業機械震耳欲聾的噪音中傳來微弱的鑰匙聲。當奧拉夫拐過街角時，一看到你，他的笑容就僵住了。他的嘴唇抿在一起，黃色的牙齒消失了，因為他挺直得有點太快了。 「四分衛，」他粗聲粗氣地說，聲音沙啞，但突然變得謹慎起來。 “沒聽到你來。”

 

 他用骯髒的指甲抓撓脖子，眼睛掃向陰影，彷彿要確定只有你一個人。 “在這裡看不見你。地下室不適合工作。”

passages.name: SchoolBasementAlcoveInvestigation
passages.content:
Your eardrums throb from the deafening sounds of machinery, but you ignore it as you lean over the mattress and look at the pictures pinned to the wall. You feel your mouth drop as you realise what you’re looking at.

 

 Photos. All of female students. Seniors. Some you recognise, others new to you. And every one of them is in different states of undress.

 

 Some of the subjects are unaware of the camera, like shots from the vents in the girls' changing room, or above the cubicles in the toilets. Women chatting, smiling, stripping. Unaware. 

 

 You recognise one person immediately. Heather Oats, from PE, for some reason taking off her clothes in the gym as she cleans the floors after school.
 
 
 
Heather would certainly be upset if you could take those photos and post them somewhere public. You’re sure Christina would approve.

 

 More disturbingly are those where they are almost definitely aware. There is a marked change in style. Instead of the camera being hidden, the subject looks down into the camera lens, staring back out from the photo at you. Behind each girl are the pipes and generators in the basement. More specifically, the pipes and generators directly surrounding you.

 

 Each photo is part of a set, fanned out across the wall like a deck of cards. Going from left to right, you watch each girl strip down to nothing, but in each shot posing luridly. 

 

 A hand suddenly grabs your shoulder. You squeal as you are forcefully spun around.

 

 You jump as you look up at the hulking figure of Olaf, the janitor of the school. This is his domain you’re in. His sweaty and greasy long hair falls towards you, framing his acne-covered face.

 

 “Who you?” he shouts over the drumming of pipes and plumbing. “What you doing here?”

 “Mannet,” he shouts over the drumming of pipes and plumbing. “My little supplier.”

 “Quarterback” he rasps, voice hoarse but suddenly cautious. “Didn’t hear you coming.”

 
 

 **Actions**

 <> 

 You glance at the photos again
passages.content_zh:
震耳欲聾的機器聲音讓你的耳膜顫動，但當你靠在床墊上看著牆上釘著的照片時，你忽略了它。當你意識到自己在看什麼時，你會感覺到自己的嘴巴垂了下來。

 

 照片。都是女學生。前輩們。有些是您認識的，有些是您陌生的。而且每個人都處於不同的脫衣狀態。

 

 有些拍攝對象沒有意識到相機的存在，例如從女孩更衣室的通風口或廁所隔間上方拍攝的照片。女人們聊天、微笑、脫衣。不知道。 

 

 你一眼就能認出一個人。來自體育學院的希瑟·奧茨（Heather Oats）出於某種原因在放學後在體育館裡脫掉衣服清潔地板。
 
 
 
如果您可以拍攝這些照片並將其發佈到公共場所，希瑟肯定會感到不安。你確信克里斯蒂娜會同意。

 

 更令人不安的是那些他們幾乎肯定意識到的情況。風格上有明顯的變化。拍攝對象並沒有隱藏相機，而是低頭看著相機鏡頭，從照片中回頭凝視著你。每個女孩的身後都是地下室的管線和發電機。更具體地說，是直接圍繞在您周圍的管道和發電機。

 

 每張照片都是一組照片的一部分，像一副紙牌一樣散開在牆上。從左到右，你會看到每個女孩都脫得精光，但在每個鏡頭中都擺出令人震驚的姿勢。 

 

 一隻手突然抓住了你的肩膀。當你用力旋轉時，你會發出尖叫聲。

 

 當你抬頭看到學校看門人奧拉夫的魁梧身影時，你跳了起來。這就是你所在的他的領地。他滿是汗水、油膩膩的長髮向你垂落，襯托出他佈滿粉刺的臉。

 

 「你是誰？」他的喊聲蓋過了管道和水管的敲擊聲。 “你在這裡做什麼？”

 「Mannet，」他在管子和管道的敲擊聲中喊道。 “我的小供應商。”

 「四分衛，」他粗聲粗氣地說，聲音沙啞，但突然變得謹慎起來。 “沒聽到你來。”

 
 

 **行動**

 <>

 你再看一眼照片

passages.name: SchoolBiologyRoom
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Biology Room**

[image: ./images/School_Biology_Room.png]

The classroom is decorated with numerous diagrams of human anatomy. Pictures line the walls, a number of demonstration models sit at the side. You can’t help but notice that in all situations, the men are depicted as muscular, tall and well endowed, whilst the women are petite, delicate and yet all depicted with enormous breasts.

 **Actions**

 <>
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - 生物室**

[圖：./images/School_Biology_Room.png]

教室裡裝飾著許多人體解剖圖。牆上掛滿了圖片，旁邊坐著一些示範模型。你不禁會注意到，在所有情況下，男性都被描繪成肌肉發達、身材高大、天賦異禀，而女性則嬌小、精緻，但都被描繪成擁有巨大的乳房。

 **行動**

 <>

passages.name: BiologyClass0
passages.content:
You look over at the TV with interest. It looks like a nature documentary. Tigers and lions and apes, out in the wild. A soothing voice narrates over the top of it.

 

 *“In the natural world, one thing is paramount. Procreation. The act of mating drives every other need. Males fight and hunt food to attract mates. Females plume and groom, and build shelter to raise offspring for the cycle to begin again.”*

 

 The video shows pictures of animals, male and females, lying together. Snuggling. Touching each other. Then begins to show a female animals lying down, squatting on all fours, pushing their heads into the ground and the bums into the air.

 

 “A female submits to the male on all things concerning intercourse. His role is to claim her, to fight for her, and to plant his seed in her so she can raise his offspring.”

 

 The video cuts to animals fornicating. Wolves being mounted, stallions having their way with horses in barns. 

 

 “Their role is to do anything for him to be attracted enough to impregnate her. She lives for his seed, and has no other role other than to submit to him.”

 

 You glance around the room. Every single student is intently watching the video. Their faces are all grinning. 

 

 You glance over at Mr. Harrow, who meets your eye quizzingly. He gestures to the screen.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 

 You look over at the TV with interest. It looks like a nature documentary. Elephants walking across the plains of Africa. A soothing voice narrates over the top of it.

 

 “The male of the species demonstrates strength and stamina in competition. The victor secures the right to pass on his superior genes.”

 

 Onscreen, two bull elephants clash, tusks grinding. Dust explodes around them as one stumbles back and flees. The winner wheels and clambers onto a waiting female, pressing her down with brute force.

 

 A ripple runs through the room. The boys lean forward, eyes narrowing, shoulders hunched with sudden focus.
passages.content_zh:
你饒有興趣地看著電視。它看起來像一部自然紀錄片。老虎、獅子和猿，在野外。一個舒緩的聲音在上面敘述著。

 

 *「在自然界中，有一件事是最重要的。繁殖。交配行為驅動著其他一切需求。雄性透過戰鬥和捕食來吸引配偶。雌性梳理羽毛和梳理毛髮，並建造庇護所來養育後代，以便循環重新開始。」*

 

 影片顯示了雄性和雌性動物躺在一起的照片。依偎。互相撫摸。然後開始展示一隻雌性動物躺著，四肢蹲著，把頭埋在地上，把屁股伸到空中。

 

 「女性在所有涉及性交的事情上都服從男性。男性的角色是索取她，為她而戰，並將自己的種子種在她體內，以便她可以養育他的後代。”

 

 影片切入動物通姦的場景。狼被騎上，種馬在穀倉裡與馬嬉戲。 

 

 “他們的角色是為他做任何事，讓他被吸引到讓她懷孕。她為他的種子而活，除了服從他之外沒有其他角色。”

 

 你環顧房間。每個學生都在聚精會神地觀看影片。他們的臉上都帶著笑容。 

 

 你看了一眼哈羅先生，他疑惑地看著你。他指著螢幕。

passages.name: BiologyClass
passages.content:
Not wanting to stand out, you face back to the screen and continue watching. Rough animal sex is now shown in the clips. The male animals savagely penetrate their mates. Some of them slump on the backs of their counterparts after clearly they’ve shot their loads.

They have been claimed by an assertive mate, who in return of having them serve every whim, will protect her and her offspring as long as she stays young, attractive and healthy. Their role fulfilled, they desire nothing more than to be a receptacle for their man’s seed, giving his life-giving nectar the reverence it deserves.”

 

 You reflect on what you’re watching. They are talking about men and women now, even though it's showing animals.

 

 You can’t imagine Jennifer agreeing this is her role in your relationship, but it’s hard to deny that this is how it is in the animal kingdom. You are all animals at heart, after all.

passages.content_zh:
不想引人注目，您背對著螢幕繼續觀看。粗暴的動物性行為現在出現在剪輯中。雄性動物野蠻地插入它們的伴侶。他們中的一些人在顯然已經完成了任務後倒在了同伴的背上。

 

 「這些女性得到了她們的獎賞。她們被一位自信的伴侶認領，作為回報，她們會滿足她們的每一個突發奇想，只要她保持年輕、有吸引力和健康，她就會保護她和她的後代。她們的角色已經完成，她們只想成為她們男人種子的容器，給予他賦予生命的花蜜應有的尊重。」

 你無法想像詹妮弗會同意這就是她在你們關係中的角色，但很難否認這就是動物王國的情況。畢竟，你們本質上都是動物。

passages.name: BiologyClassBoys
passages.content:
The power in your veins won’t stay still. It claws at you, demanding that you prove yourself.

 

 Before Jake’s foot can land again, you shove your chair back and launch forward. The scrape of wood on tile makes heads turn just in time to see you throw yourself at Terrence.

 
 
 
 He raises his arms to block as you slam into him, desk rattling under the impact. But despite his attempts to defend himself, you both fall to the floor.

 He gives a strangled cry, eyes wide, trying to cower as you take him off his seat. You both fall to the floor, Terrence shielding his face with his trembling hands.

 
 
 The pack howls, boys jeering and stamping feet, the noise echoing off the walls. Jake grins wide, his approval sharp and cruel. “That’s more like it, Mannet!”

 

 You land a blow into Terrence's stomach before you realise he's already defeated. Taking a step up, you look down on him, curled up on the floor in a ball. Testosterone floods your body. Your limbs shake with adrenaline. Part of this disgusts you, but you find yourself smiling all the same.

 

 Looking over to the front of the classroom, Harrow’s smile glows from the glare of the TV. He makes no move to stop you, only tilts his head in satisfaction. "Good demonstration, Dennis. Take your seat."

 

 The curse hums inside you like victory. You’ve shown your place in the pack. As you take your seat, the other boys grin. Even Jake, who eyes you with curiosity.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 

 The feral heat rages through you, but you will yourself to drag it another way. Not directing it at Terrence. But at Jake.

 

 You push back your chair and step in between Jake's foot and Terrence's back, wedging your body like a shield. "Enough!" you bark, hearing Dennis' timid voice rising above a whisper.

 

 The room goes quiet for a beat, the sudden hush filled only by the narration of the tape. Jake stares back at you, teeth on display.

 

 
 "Leave it," you hear Terrence mutter from behind you. His voice trembles, bu
passages.content_zh:
你血管裡的力量不會停滯不前。它會抓著你，要你證明自己。

 

 在傑克的腳再次落地之前，你將椅子向後推並向前發射。木頭在瓷磚上的刮擦聲讓人回頭一看，正好看到你撲向特倫斯。

 
 
 
 當你撞到他身上時，他舉起雙臂擋住，桌子在撞擊下嘎嘎作響。但儘管他試圖自衛，你們倆還是倒在了地板上。

 當你把他從座位上拉下來時，他發出一聲窒息的叫聲，睜大眼睛，試圖退縮。你們倆都倒在了地板上，泰倫斯用顫抖的雙手摀住了臉。

 
 
 狼群嚎叫，男孩們嘲笑、跺腳，噪音在牆壁上迴盪。傑克咧嘴大笑，他的認可尖銳而殘酷。 “這更像是這樣，曼內特！”

 

 在你意識到泰倫斯已經被擊敗之前，你對他的腹部進行了一擊。你上前一步，俯視著他，他在地板上蜷縮成一團。睪丸激素充斥你的身體。你的四肢因腎上腺素而顫抖。其中一部分讓你感到厭惡，但你發現自己仍然在微笑。

 詛咒在你內心嗡嗡作響，就像勝利一樣。你已經表明了你在群體中的位置。當你入座時，其他男孩都笑了。即使是傑克，他也好奇地看著你。

passages.name: BiologyRoomSpying
passages.content:
You lean carefully toward the narrow window in the biology room door, keeping your body angled just out of sight. Through the glass you spot rows of boys, all facing the flickering glow of a television screen near the front of the room.

 

 Harrow is there, standing just to the side of the old TV unit. His arms are folded, trademark smile flickering in the dim light. His eyes track the room like a conductor reading the mood of an orchestra.

 

 
 The room flickers with the light of the old television. The boys are slouched in their chairs, legs sprawled, posture relaxed. Whatever is playing on the screen doesn’t seem to be having much of an effect.

 

 A bull elephant mounts a female onscreen. One of the boys barks a laugh.

 
 
 Another leans toward his friend and mutters something that you can't hear, gesturing vaguely toward the screen. More laughter. A crumpled bit of paper sails across the room and hits the back of someone’s head.

 

 Harrow's smile tightens as his gaze flicks from one boy to another, then briefly to the screen. He looks agitated. Confused.

 

 Onscreen, a lion pins down a lioness. “Didn’t even buy her dinner first,” you hear someone say loud enough that you hear it through the door. You see others snigger.

 

 Harrow doesn’t speak, but his eyes narrow. The lesson continues, but no one’s really paying attention.

 

 You watch for a few moments longer, but nothing abnormal happens. Whatever impact the curse had, you've nullified it. If you want to learn what's happening in the segregated boys lessons, you need the curse to be in-effect.

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 Even without sound, the atmosphere feels different. The boys sit straighter. Shoulders taut. There’s a charge to them, like a pack waiting for a signal. It doesn’t look like any biology class you’ve ever attended.

 

 Onscreen, a bull elephant mounts a female with sheer force. One boy leans forward in his chair, elbows on knees. Another grips the sides of his desk, knuckles pale. Thei
passages.content_zh:
你小心翼翼地靠向生物室門上的窄窗，保持身體的角度不被視線所及。透過玻璃，你可以看到一排排男孩，他們都面對著房間前面電視螢幕閃爍的光芒。

 

 哈羅就在那裡，站在舊電視機的旁邊。他雙手抱胸，標誌性的微笑在昏暗的燈光下閃爍。他的目光掃過房間，就像指揮家解讀管弦樂團的情緒一樣。

 

 
 房間裡閃爍著舊電視的燈光。男孩們無精打采地坐在椅子上，雙腿伸開，姿勢放鬆。無論螢幕上正在播放什麼，似乎都沒有太大效果。

 

 銀幕上一頭公象騎著一頭雌象。其中一個男孩哈哈大笑。

 
 
 另一個人靠向他的朋友，咕噥著一些你聽不見的東西，模糊地指著螢幕。更多笑聲。一張皺巴巴的紙片穿過房間，擊中某人的後腦勺。

 

 哈羅的笑容收緊了，他的目光從一個男孩身上掃到了另一個男孩身上，然後又短暫地落在了屏幕上。他看上去很激动。使困惑。

 

 螢幕上，一頭獅子正在壓制一頭母獅。 「連晚餐都沒有先給她買。」你聽到有人大聲說道，隔著門都能聽到。你看到别人窃笑。

 

 哈羅沒有說話，但他的眼睛瞇了起來。教訓還在繼續，但沒有人真正關注。

 

 你又觀察了一會兒，但沒有發現任何異常情況。無論詛咒產生什麼影響，你都已經消除了它。如果你想了解隔離男孩的課程中發生了什麼，你需要詛咒生效。

 

 行動

 <>
 
 即使沒有聲音，氣氛也感覺不同。男孩們坐得更直了。肩膀繃緊。他們有責任，就像一群人在等待訊號。它看起來不像你上過的任何生物課。

 

 銀幕上，一頭公像以驚人的力量騎上一頭雌象。一個男孩在椅子上身體前傾，手肘放在膝蓋上。另一個人抓著桌子的兩側，指關節蒼白。泰伊

passages.name: BiologyRoomAVHelp
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Biology Room**

 [image: ./images/School_Biology_Room.png]

 

 The TV cart rattles as you wheel it through the door. The sound cuts through the chatter at once, every head in the room turning toward you. For a heartbeat there’s silence, followed by some mutterings and grins as glee sparks across the rows of desks in the classroom.

 

 Dennis is at his desk near the front. He doesn’t look up, his shoulders hunched, eyes fixed on the wood grain of the desk in front of him.

 

 “Ah, there you are,” Harrow calls as he takes position beside the TV stand, his theatrical smile spreading wide. He sweeps an arm to the class. “As promised, the surprise I mentioned. Young Miss Phillips has volunteered to share Dennis' burden and assist with the equipment for our continuing journey into the wonders of the natural world.”

 

 A voice pipes up from the back from one of the football players. A weaselly boy called Manny. “She can help me with my equipment any time.”

 

 The room explodes in sniggers, boys elbowing each other, grins sharp with implication. Your heart sinks. Their looks are fixated on you. It’s exactly what you feared this class would be.

 

 Harrow doesn’t rebuke Manny. He just chuckles, lifting a hand as if indulging a joke at a dinner table. “Now, now. Let’s focus on the topic at hand. Today’s lesson continues our exploration of nature. To be specific, natural reproduction.”

 

 The words spark a chorus of whoops and hollers. The boys’ eyes snap back to you, grins spreading, energy sharpened.

 

 Harrow crosses to your side, his smile dazzling, voice dropping lower for you alone. “Pop the tape in, won’t you? Then just stand off to the side. Things can get a bit rowdy. After all, boys will be boys.”

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 
 You slide the cassette into place. The machine hums, the blinds rattle down, and the room dips into shadow. The boys shuffle but don’t really change. A few lean forward, but most are slouched, legs sprawled, arms folded
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - 生物室**

 [圖：./images/School_Biology_Room.png]

 

 當你推著電視推車進門時，它會發出嘎嘎聲。聲音立刻打斷了喧鬧，房間裡的每個人都轉向你。教室裡先是一片寂靜，然後是一些竊竊私語和笑容，教室裡的一排課桌上閃爍著歡樂的火花。

 

 丹尼斯坐在靠近前台的辦公桌前。他沒有抬頭，聳著肩膀，眼睛盯著面前桌子的木紋。

 

 「啊，你來了，」哈羅在電視櫃旁坐下時喊道，臉上掛滿了戲劇性的笑容。他向全班揮動手臂。 “正如我所承諾的，我提到的驚喜。年輕的菲利普斯小姐自願分擔丹尼斯的負擔，並協助我們提供設備，以便我們繼續探索自然世界的奇蹟。”

 

 一個足球員的聲音從後面傳來。一個名叫曼尼的黃鼠狼男孩。 “她可以隨時幫助我使用設備。”

 

 房間裡爆發出一陣竊笑，男孩們互相肘擊，尖刻地笑著。你的心沉了下去。他們的目光注視著你。這正是你所擔心的這堂課。

 

 哈羅沒有斥責曼尼。他只是咯咯笑，舉起一隻手，彷彿在餐桌上開玩笑。 “好了好了，我們來說說正題吧，今天的課程繼續我們對自然的探索，具體來說，就是自然繁衍。”

 

 這句話引發了一片歡呼聲。男孩們的目光猛地轉向你，笑容燦爛，精力更充沛。

 

 哈羅走到你身邊，笑容燦爛，聲音低沉，只有你一個人。 “把磁帶放進去，好嗎？然後站在一邊。事情可能會變得有點吵鬧。畢竟，男孩就是男孩。”

 

 行動

 <>
 
 
 將磁帶滑入到位。機器嗡嗡作響，百葉窗嘎嘎作響，房間陷入陰影。男孩們洗牌，但並沒有真正改變。少數人身體前傾，但大多數人無精打采、雙腿伸開、雙臂交叉

**Charlton School - Under Bleachers**

Underneath the bleachers smells of urine and stale beer. The grass is worn down to dirty mud. It’s covered in cans and litter, either from past spectators or students who sometimes come here to drink, smoke and do other things.

 
 
 
Jake lies peacefully asleep on the dirt where you left him, looking at peace as he breathes shallowly.

passages.name: SchoolBleachersJakeDialogue0
passages.content:
The quarterback’s eyes dart up as you approach him. He relaxes when he sees it’s you.

 

 “Hey sweetheart,” he says. “You come to help me out?”
 
 
 Jake's eyes catch yours, and the smirk that spreads is anything but friendly.

 

 “Mannet,” he drawls, stepping forward. His hand shoves hard against your shoulder, then again, harder, forcing you back a pace. “What are you even doing back here?”

 

 He pushes once more, sharp and deliberate. You raise your hands instinctively, backing away, heart thumping. Jake’s smirk only widens.

 

 That’s enough. You turn and step away quickly, leaving him behind.
 

 Jake stares back at you, waiting for you to speak.
 

 
 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 

 **Actions**

passages.content_zh:
當你接近四分衛時，他的眼睛會猛地睜開。當他看到是你時，他就會放鬆。

 

 「嘿，親愛的，」他說。 “你是來幫我的嗎？”
 
 
 傑克的目光與你對視，臉上露出一絲不友善的笑容。

 

 「Mannet，」他慢吞吞地說，向前走去。他的手用力推在你的肩膀上，然後再次用力，迫使你退後一步。 “你還來這裡做什麼？”

 

 他再次用力，尖銳而從容。你本能地舉起雙手，向後退去，心臟狂跳。傑克的笑容愈來燦爛。

 

 這就夠了。你轉身快步走開，把他拋在了身後。
 

 傑克盯著你，等著你說話。

passages.name: SchoolBleachersTransformJake
passages.content:
The dogtags gleam in your hand. You draw a breath and speak again. "From this item, I see you in your time of need. Now form shape to the person I see within.”

 

 The air tightens around you, humming with magic. You force Jake's image into your mind. Broad and muscled, wearing his quarterback jacket, scarred knuckles and cocky grin, his weight filling space, his swagger unchallenged.

 

 Magic bursts out from your chest, running across your body. Then the pain takes you. Your skin prickles, stretching from within. Bones grind and snap, shoulders grow wider. Ribs flaring, limbs lengthening. Your chest collapses inward and you feel your lungs swell larger as you suck in air. Hair recedes from your vision as though shorn away. Your hands balloon and you feel your veins surge through swollen forearms. As you finally let out a cry from the pain, it twists into a guttural sound, your voice now hoarse and heavy.

 

 Darkness claws at the edges of your vision. You grit your teeth and force yourself to hold on as your organs wrench and settle, your frame remade in his image.

 

 Then, silence.

 

 You stagger forward, panting, slick with sweat. The body feels heavy, rooted, brimming with strength. You glance down at your hands and flex them, feeling power ripple in your biceps.

 

 Glancing down, you catch your reflection in the shards of a broken beer bottle lying on the floor. Jake Duncan stares back at you, dark-haired and blue-eyed. His mouth forming into a sneer as you look at your reflection.

 
 
 Off to the side, Jake - the real one - lies still, breathing evenly, unaware. You feel a sense of pity for him, bested so easily by forces he can't possibly understand. His sleep no longer looking peaceful. He looks weak.

 

 The thought startles you, but you can't shake it. Something about being Jake means that it's all too easy to give into his callous nature. You feel your *assertiveness* has been impacted by your most recent change.

 

 This might actually benef
passages.content_zh:
狗牌在你手中閃閃發光。你吸了一口氣，再次說話。 “通過這個物品，我在你需要的時候看到了你。現在，為我在裡面看到的人塑造形狀。”

 

 你周圍的空氣變得緊繃，充滿了魔法的嗡嗡聲。你把傑克的形象強加在你的腦海裡。身材魁梧，肌肉發達，穿著四分衛夾克，指關節傷痕累累，笑容滿面，他的體重充滿了空間，他的大搖大擺毫無疑問。

 

 魔法從你的胸口爆發出來，流遍你的全身。然後痛苦就會帶走你。你的皮膚刺痛，從內部伸展。骨頭磨斷，肩膀變寬。肋骨向外張開，四肢變長。當你吸入空氣時，你的胸部向內塌陷，你會感覺到肺部膨脹得更大。頭髮就像被剪掉一樣從你的視野中消失。你的雙手膨脹起來，你感覺靜脈在腫脹的前臂中湧動。當你終於因疼痛而發出一聲尖叫時，它變成了喉音，你的聲音現在沙啞而沉重。

 

 黑暗的爪子抓住了你的視野邊緣。當你的器官扭動並穩定下來時，你咬緊牙關，強迫自己堅持下去，你的身體按照他的形象重塑。

 

 然後，沉默。

 

 你踉踉蹌地向前走去，氣喘吁籲，滿頭大汗。身體感覺沉重、紮根、充滿力量。你低頭看著自己的雙手，彎曲它們，感受二頭肌中的力量波動。

 

 低頭一看，你在地板上破碎的啤酒瓶碎片中看到了自己的倒影。傑克鄧肯（Jake Duncan）回望你，黑髮藍眼睛。當你看著自己的倒影時，他的嘴角露出了冷笑。

 
 
 在旁邊，傑克──真正的傑克──一動不動地躺著，呼吸均勻，渾然不覺。你對他感到一種憐憫，這種憐憫很容易就被他無法理解的力量所擊敗。他的睡眠看起來不再平靜。他看起來很虛弱。

 

 這個想法讓你大吃一驚，但你無法擺脫它。成為傑克意味著很容易屈服於他冷酷的本性。您感覺您的「自信」受到了最近的變化的影響。

 

 這實際上可能有好處

passages.name: BusinessStudiesClass0
passages.content:
“Welcome, class!” Mrs. DeBussy says with a shrill tone. “Today we shall continue with this term’s topic. Expectations in the workplace. Now I know you are all such students, but listen carefully and you might hear something that will be new. Even to you bright young things.”

 

 You glance around the room. She has everyone’s attention, at least.

 

 Turning back, you listen as Mrs. DeBussy continues her introduction. “In this lesson, we'll all learn how to perform the roles that you will gain when you leave Charlton. Many of my top students have gone on to be quite successful. I have no doubt that some of you will join them.”

 

 Her voice drops to a whisper, but her enthusiasm still shines through. “In business, you will find many of the skills employers expect are not those we would normally teach. Dictation and typing, for example, are of paramount importance. So today, you’re going to learn how it works. So each of you ladies, turn to the right of you. The men, turn to your left. And meet your study buddy for the day!”

 

 A creeping feeling rises up inside you. Turning to your left, you come face-to-face with a smirking man that you instantly recognise. Logain. Your boss’s son.

 

 “Hey squirt,” he greets you, not letting his grin slip.

 

 Tall, muscular and lean, you notice his striking blue eyes looking back at you. You look away briefly, squirming slightly underneath his stare. He seems to relish in your discomfort. 

 

 **Actions**

「歡迎同學們光臨！」德比西太太用尖銳的語氣說。 「今天我們繼續這個學期的話題，對職場的期望。現在我知道你們都是這樣的學生，但仔細聽，你可能會聽到一些新的東西，甚至對你們這些聰明的年輕人來說。”

 

 你環顧房間。至少她引起了所有人的注意。

 

 回過頭來，您會聽到德比西夫人繼續她的介紹。 “在這節課中，我們都將學習如何扮演離開查爾頓後將獲得的角色。我的許多優秀學生都取得了相當的成功。我毫不懷疑你們中的一些人會加入他們。”

 

 她的聲音變得低沉，但她的熱情仍然閃耀著。 「在商界，你會發現雇主期望的許多技能並不是我們通常教授的技能。例如，聽寫和打字是至關重要的。所以今天，你將學習它是如何運作的。所以，各位女士，轉向你們的右邊。男士們，轉向你們的左邊。和今天的學習夥伴見面！”

 

 一種令人毛骨悚然的感覺在你內心升起。向左轉，你會遇到一個傻笑的男人，你一眼就認出他了。登入.你老闆的兒子。

 

 「嘿，噴子，」他向你打招呼，笑容沒有消失。

 

 高大、肌肉發達、精瘦，你注意到他那雙引人注目的藍眼睛正回頭看著你。你短暫地移開視線，在他的注視下微微蠕動。他似乎很享受你的不適。

passages.content:
“Now,” DeBussy continues. “Gentlemen. I will write sentences on the board that you shall dictate to your counterpart. Ladies, you will type the exact words they say. As fast as you can, but without errors. Not that I expect many from you!”

 

 You look down at the typewriter. Despite its age, the layout is the same as a keyboard. At least you won’t struggle with this lesson.

 

 “If there are no questions,” DeBussy says. “Let us begin. Gentleman, please stand behind your receptionist for the day. Ladies, look down at your typewriter.”

 

 Chair legs scrape across the floor as the boys stand up. You focus on the typewriter in front of you, but feel Logain’s presence close to you. You bristle as you feel his hands rest on the back of your chair.

 

 You hear chalk scratching against the blackboard. Then, you feel Logain’s breath breeze across your ear. 

 

 “A good secretary follows orders,” he whispers.

 

 Your eyes flick up to the front of the room. DeBussy stands to the side of the blackboard, where the sentence Logain uttered is written in stylish handwriting across the top of the board. She looks at you, expectantly.

passages.content_zh:
「現在，」德布西繼續說。 「先生們。我會在黑板上寫下句子，然後你們向對方口述。女士們，你們將準確地輸入他們說的話。盡可能快，但不能有錯誤。我對你們的期望並不高！”

 

 你低頭看著打字機。儘管年代久遠，但佈局與鍵盤相同。至少你在學習本課時不會感到困難。

 

 「如果沒有問題的話，」德比西說。 “讓我們開始吧。先生，請站在今天的接待員後面。女士們，低頭看看你的打字機。”

 

 當孩子們站起來時，椅子腿刮過地板。你專注於眼前的打字機，卻感覺到洛根就在你身邊。當你感覺到他的手放在你的椅背上時，你感到毛骨悚然。

 

 你聽到粉筆刮擦黑板的聲音。然後，你感覺到羅根的呼吸微風吹過你的耳邊。 

 

 「一個好的秘書服從命令，」他低聲說。

 

 你的目光掃向房間的前方。德比西站在黑板旁邊，洛根所說的這句話用時尚的筆跡寫在黑板的頂部。她滿懷期待地看著你。

 

 行動

 <>

 <>
