passages.name: PEClassWatch
passages.content:
You hand over the note that you penned to Coach Beach. He snatches it up, unfolds it, and reads it carefully. Once he’s done, his eyes look up over the note at you, eyeing you.

 

 “Cramps, eh?” He sighs. “A poor excuse to duck out of some exercise, but I’ve been told that I need to make allowances for your ladies."

 

 Behind you, one of the kids in uniform sniggers. He falls silent quickly when Beach gives him a dark look.

 

 “Alright, Phillips. You’ve got a pass. Get over in the sin bin. The rest of you, let’s see if your notes are as convincing.”

 

 You stand up from the bench and move up to the top seat on the rafters in the corner. A few other students join you after Beach verifies their own notes. You smile politely, but they make no efforts to respond. Only Chester, a kid with glazed eyes, meets your stare as Beach reluctantly lets him sit out.

 

 Actions

 <>

 
 
 Beach walks over to the centre of the gym. “Alright kids,” he bellows. “Weatherman says it’s going to drizzle today. And whilst I think you all can survive a bit of rain, I’ve been told that some of you have weak immune systems. I don’t need your moms on the phone yelling at me for getting you sick.”

 

 You notice Beach looks up to the bleachers. For a moment, you think he’s looking at you. But his eyes drift to Chester beside you. The boy gazes vacantly down into the floor, unaware he’s being watched.

 

 “So same old, same old,” Beach says, returning his attention to the class. “Inside activities. Boys with boys. Girls with girls. Pair off into fives and go stand by one of the obstacles on the course. Only rule - don’t come last.”

 

 The students organise themselves into groups - either groups of girls, or groups of boys. As they do so, from the side of your eye you catch Chester staring at you.

 

 “Your hair is shining,” he tells you. “It’s glowing yellow.”

 

 The shriek of Beach’s whistle snaps your gaze back to the gym, as the squeaking and sliding of fifty pairs of sneakers
passages.content_zh:
您將寫下的便條交給 Coach Beach。他抓起它，展開，仔細閱讀。一旦他完成了，他的眼睛就會抬頭看著你，看著你。

 

 「抽筋，嗯？」他嘆了口氣。 “這是逃避鍛煉的糟糕藉口，但有人告訴我，我需要體諒你們的女士們。”

 

 在你身後，一個穿著制服的孩子竊笑著。當比奇用陰沉的眼神看著他時，他很快就沉默了。

 

 “好吧，菲利普斯。你已經通過了。進入垃圾箱。剩下的人，讓我們看看你的筆記是否有說服力。”

 

 你從長椅上站起來，走到角落椽子上的最上面的座位。比奇驗證自己的筆記後，其他一些學生也加入了您。你禮貌地微笑，但他們沒有回應。只有切斯特，一個目光呆滯的孩子，與你的目光對視，比奇不情願地讓他坐在外面。

 

 行動

 <>

 
 
 海灘走到健身房的中心。 「好吧，孩子們，」他吼道。 “天氣預報員說今天會下毛毛雨。雖然我認為你們都能熬過一點雨，但我聽說你們中的一些人免疫系統很弱。我不需要你們媽媽在電話裡對我大喊大叫，因為我讓你們生病了。”

 

 你注意到比奇抬頭看著露天看台。有那麼一刻，你以為他在看著你。但他的目光卻轉向了你身邊的切斯特。男孩茫然地凝視著地板，沒有意識到有人在看著他。

 

 「還是老樣子，老樣子，」比奇說著，將注意力重新集中在課堂上。 “內部活動。男孩和男孩。女孩和女孩。五人一組，站在賽道上的一個障礙物旁。唯一的規則是——不要排在最後。”

 

 學生們把自己分成小組——要嘛是女孩小組，要嘛是男孩小組。當他們這樣做時，你從眼睛的一側看到切斯特正在盯著你。

 

 「你的頭髮閃閃發光，」他告訴你。 “它發出黃色的光。”

 

 比奇的尖叫聲將你的目光拉回健身房，五十雙運動鞋發出吱吱聲和滑動聲

passages.name: PEClass
passages.content:
“Alright kids,” you hear Beach bellow. Like everyone else, you turn to look at him in the centre of the gym, as if it was a stage. “Weatherman says it’s going to drizzle today. And whilst I think you all can survive a bit of rain, I’ve been told that some of you have weak immune systems. I don’t need your moms on the phone yelling at me for getting you sick.”

 

 “So same old, same old. Inside activities. Boys with boys. Girls with girls. Pair off into fives and go stand by one of the obstacles on the course. Only rule - don’t come last.”

 

 You turn around and are accepted into a group forming nearby. Other students aren’t so lucky, but within a minute everyone is separated into squads. The organiser of your group gestures for you to move over to the balance beam. Like everything in here, it smells like a gym sock. Removing the curse hasn't stopped the smell from threatening to overpower you.

 

 Beach’s whistle shrieks and instantly the sounds of fifty sneakers squeaking and sliding fills the air. You clamber up on to equipment, energised by the noise, and move as fast as you can over the beam. It’s enough to remind you that this body is still new to you, with your centre of gravity shifted away from what you are used to.

 

 “Careful, Phillips,” you hear Beach say as he passes by. “You won’t find a knight in shining armour to catch you in here.”

 

 Ignoring him, you make it to the other side. The other girls follow suit and quickly you’re moving around to the next course. So quick, in fact, that the group of boys in front of you hasn’t finished on the vaulting boxes.

 

 You take deep breaths as you feel your heart beating. The natural endorphins from exercise start to circulate through your body. There is something rewarding about the class, especially as you are free of any adverse effects from the equipment. The body odour of teenage boys' is still repellant, but you find some degree of enjoyment in the activity.

 

 The last boy in the group in front
passages.content_zh:
「好吧，孩子們，」你聽到比奇吼道。和其他人一樣，你轉頭看著體育館中央的他，彷彿那是個舞台。 “天氣預報員說今天會下毛毛雨。雖然我認為你們都能熬過一點雨，但我聽說你們中的一些人免疫系統很弱。我不需要你們媽媽在電話裡對我大喊大叫，因為我讓你們生病了。”

 

 “老樣子，老樣子。內部活動。男孩和男孩。女孩和女孩。五人一組，站在球場上的一個障礙物旁。唯一的規則是——不要排在最後。”

 

 你轉身並被接納進入附近形成的群體。其他學生就沒那麼幸運了，但不到一分鐘，每個人就被分成了小隊。小組的組織者示意你移到平衡木上。就像這裡的所有東西一樣，它聞起來像運動襪。解除詛咒並沒有阻止這種氣味對你的威脅。

 

 比奇的哨聲響起，空氣中立刻響起五十隻運動鞋的吱吱聲和滑動聲。你爬上設備，在噪音的刺激下，盡可能快地越過橫樑。這足以提醒你，這個身體對你來說還是陌生的，你的重心已經偏離了你習慣的地方。

 

 「小心點，菲利普斯，」你聽到比奇經過時說道。 “在這裡你找不到穿著閃亮盔甲的騎士來抓你。”

 

 不理他，你就到了另一邊。其他女孩也紛紛效仿，很快你就開始學習下一門課程。事實上，速度太快了，你前面的那群男孩還沒完成跳箱。

 

 當您感覺到心臟跳動時，您會深呼吸。運動產生的天然內啡肽開始在您的體內循環。這門課有一些有益的東西，特別是當你沒有受到設備的任何不利影響時。十幾歲男孩的體味仍然令人厭惡，但你可以在活動中找到一定程度的樂趣。

 

 前面那群人中的最後一個男孩

passages.name: PEClean
passages.content:
After a long few minutes, the doors to the gym swing back open and Coach Beach walks in, carrying a bucket of warm water and an arm full of cleaning products. He drops them next to you.

 

 “Alright, Phillips,” he says to you. “If PE isn’t your specialist subject, maybe you’ll excel at cleaning. I want this gym spick and span by the time I get back.” He glances at the stopwatch dangling around his neck. “One hour should do it. So. Get going.” 

 

 Beach waits for you to fiddle with the cleaning products, then turns and walks out of the gym. Knowing what you have to do, the smells of perspiration and drying sweat seems ever more pungent. You glance up at the clock above the basketball net. Fifty-nine minutes to go.

 

 Picking up the cleaning equipment, you consider how best to tackle this.

 
 Coach Beach walks in after a few minutes, carrying the bucket and a variety of cleaning products.

 

 “Back again, Phillips?” he says to you. “Some might think you’ve found your calling. Get on with it. You know what to do.”

 

 He nods to the bucket, then walks out of the gym. Trying to ignore the putrid smells that surround you, you pick up the equipment and consider what to do.

 
 

 Actions

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 

 You pick up the bucket and soak a cloth inside it, then get down on your hands and knees to start cleaning. 

 

 Getting your nose close to the ground, you can almost taste the smell of bad hygiene and teenage body odour. You scrub vigorously, working up a sweat. Your polo shirt hangs down and more than once you feel it absorb the dirty water on the floor, touching your breasts.

 

 As the minutes tick away, your mind drifts. You find yourself considering how long some of this sweat has been etched into the floor, and how many of the people you know have spilled the perspiration that you are now inhaling. 

 

 
 You scrub hard, thankful that the curse doesn't have any sway over you. Your mind drifts to other topics, thinking of Jenni
passages.content_zh:
漫長的幾分鐘後，健身房的門重新打開，教練海灘走進來，手裡拿著一桶溫水和一隻裝滿清潔產品的手臂。他把它們放在你旁邊。

 

 「好吧，菲利普斯，」他對你說。 「如果運動不是你的專長，也許你會擅長清潔。我希望等我回來時這個健身房能收拾得乾乾淨淨。」他看了一眼掛在脖子上的秒錶。 “一個小時就可以了。那麼，開始吧。”

 

 海灘等你擺弄清潔產品，然後轉身走出健身房。知道自己必須做什麼，汗水和乾汗的氣味似乎變得更加刺鼻。你抬頭看了一眼籃球網上方的時鐘。距離比賽結束還有五十九分鐘。

 

 拿起清潔設備，您考慮如何最好地解決這個問題。

 
 幾分鐘後，教練海灘帶著水桶和各種清潔產品走了進來。

 

 「又回來了嗎，菲利普斯？」他對你說。 “有些人可能認為你已經找到了自己的使命。繼續吧。你知道該怎麼做。”

 

 他向水桶點點頭，然後走出健身房。你試著忽略周圍的腐臭味，拿起設備並考慮該怎麼做。

 
 

 行動

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 

 你拿起水桶，在裡面浸上一塊布，然後用手和膝蓋著地開始清潔。 

 

 把鼻子貼近地面，你幾乎可以嚐到衛生條件差和青少年體味的味道。你使勁擦洗，出了一身汗。你的 Polo 衫垂下來，你不只一次感覺到它吸收了地板上的髒水，接觸到了你的乳房。

 

 隨著時間的流逝，你的思緒開始飄忽不定。你發現自己在思考這些汗水已經在地板上蝕刻了多久，以及你認識的有多少人已經將你現在吸入的汗水灑了出去。 

 

 
 你用力擦洗，慶幸詛咒沒有對你產生任何影響。你的思緒轉移到其他話題，想著珍妮

And whilst I think you all can survive a bit of rain, I’ve been told that some of you have weak immune systems. I don’t need your moms on the phone yelling at me for getting you sick.”

passages.name: PEClassJake
passages.content:
“Alright kids,” you hear Beach bellow. Like everyone else, you turn to look at him in the centre of the gym, as if it was a stage. “Weatherman says it’s going to drizzle today. And whilst I think you all can survive a bit of rain, I’ve been told that some of you have weak immune systems. I don’t need your moms on the phone yelling at me for getting you sick.”

 

 “So same old, same old. Inside activities. Boys with boys. Girls with girls. Pair off into fives and go stand by one of the obstacles on the course. Only rule - don’t come last.”

 
 
 You turn around and unsurprisingly see boys jostling to get into a group forming near you. Those who make it grin with satisfaction. Those who don't sneer, reluctantly forming other groups. "You ready?" one of the accepted ones asks you.

 

 You nod and lead the group over to the vaulting box. Like everything in here, it smells like a gym sock. Removing the curse hasn't stopped the smell from threatening to overpower you.

 You nod and lead the group over to the vaulting box. Like everything in here, it smells like a gym sock. You find it almost overwhelming.

 

 One by one the boys ahead of you sprint forward, springing hard against the board and throwing themselves over the vault. No one cheers. No one encourages. Each jump is measured with smirks or narrowed eyes, every stumble a mark against the boy who falters.

 

 Beside you, Manny bounces on his heels, arms loose at his sides. He turns, smirk sharp, voice pitched like a joke but edged underneath. “Reckon I can vault higher than you, Duncan.”

 

 The words land harder than they should. Your chest tightens, a hot surge running through you. The idea of being shown up, even in passing, bristles under your skin.

 

 Beach growls again, clapping his hands. “Keep it moving! Line up!”

 

 The horse looms closer, Manny still grinning at your side, needling.
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

passages.content_zh:
「好吧，孩子們，」你聽到比奇吼道。和其他人一樣，你轉頭看著體育館中央的他，彷彿那是個舞台。 “天氣預報員說今天會下毛毛雨。雖然我認為你們都能熬過一點雨，但我聽說你們中的一些人免疫系統很弱。我不需要你們媽媽在電話裡對我大喊大叫，因為我讓你們生病了。”

 

 “老樣子，老樣子。內部活動。男孩和男孩。女孩和女孩。五人一組，站在球場上的一個障礙物旁。唯一的規則是——不要排在最後。”

 
 
 你轉身，毫不奇怪地看到男孩們爭先恐後地加入你附近的一群人。那些成功的人都會滿足地微笑。那些不冷笑的人，不情願地組成了其他團體。 「準備好了嗎？」其中一位被接受的人問你。

 

 你點點頭，帶領大家走向跳馬箱。就像這裡的所有東西一樣，它聞起來像運動襪。解除詛咒並沒有阻止這種氣味對你的威脅。

 你點點頭，帶領大家走向跳馬箱。就像這裡的所有東西一樣，它聞起來像運動襪。你會發現這幾乎是壓倒性的。

 

 前面的男孩們一一向前衝刺，用力地跳到木板上，然後縱身躍過跳馬。沒有人歡呼。沒有人鼓勵。每一次跳躍都伴隨著傻笑或瞇起的眼睛，每一次絆倒都給那個步履蹣跚的男孩留下了印記。

 

 在你旁邊，曼尼用腳跟彈跳，雙臂放鬆地垂在身體兩側。他轉過身來，笑容燦爛，聲音聽起來像是在開玩笑，但又很壓抑。 “我想我能跳得比你更高，鄧肯。”

 

 這些話聽起來比應有的還要難。你的胸口收緊，一股熱流流過你的全身。就算只是一閃而過，一想到被人露面，你就會感到毛骨悚然。

 

 海灘再次咆哮，拍著手。 “繼續前進！排隊！”

 

 馬越來越近了，曼尼仍然在你身邊咧嘴笑，針鋒相對。

passages.name: SchoolGymInvestigation
passages.content:
You stand in the centre of the gym, tapping into the pool of magic that you’ve built up inside of you. Forming a ball of energy, you project it out across the gym like a radar. 

 

 You have to shield your eyes as the gymnasium bursts into light. When you look up and your eyes adjust, you freeze in abject horror.

 

 The gym, as you know it, is virtually gone. Instead you are in a jungle from an alien world. In your nightmares.

 

 Tentacles of magical energy protrude from almost every piece of equipment, writhing and wriggling as they seemingly hunt for prey. They hang down from the ceiling, swinging freely in an infinite act of searching out a target. The monkey bars and climbing wall are covered in yellow blisters of pus and magic. And all of it glistens with sweat and body odour.

 

 You crouch to the floor, trying to avoid coming into contact with any of the magic perversions your eyes can’t believe they are seeing.

 

 
 
 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 You stand in the centre of the gym, trying to tap into the pool of magic that resides within you. But try as you might, not even a flicker of energy can be brought up.

 

 Meanwhile, the gym remains unchanged. Only the smells of body odour and sweat affect your senses.

 

 Wincing, you recall the teachings of the dark arts. You need to be a woman to use them. Which, in this form, you are most certainly not.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 

 You are thankful to close your eyes, but you feel your hairs stand on end, all too aware of what you can’t see and can barely believe.

 

 
 “I curse this object with power beyond its power,” you mutter quickly “Unseen and unheard, it affects all but me.”

 

 When you open your eyes, a flash of your magical aura rushes out from your body, illuminating the room. The monstrosities in the gym squirm and shake. Inside the blisters liquid bubbles and presses up against the thin membranes. The lurid smell of the gym grows stronger.
passages.content_zh:
你站在健身房的中央，挖掘你內心累積的魔力。形成一個能量球，你將它像雷達一樣投射到整個體育館。 

 

 當體育館突然亮起時，你必須保護你的眼睛。當你抬起頭並調整眼睛時，你會陷入極度的恐懼之中。

 

 如你所知，健身房實際上已經消失了。相反，你身處外星世界的叢林中。在你的惡夢中。

 

 幾乎每一件裝備上都伸出了魔法能量的觸手，它們扭動著、蠕動著，似乎在尋找獵物。它們從天花板垂下來，自由擺動，無限地尋找目標。單槓和攀岩牆佈滿了膿液和魔法的黃色水泡。所有這些都因汗水和體臭而閃閃發光。

 

 你蹲在地板上，試圖避免接觸任何你的眼睛難以置信的魔法變態。

 

 
 
 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 **行動**

 <>
 
 
 你站在健身房的中央，試著挖掘你內心的魔力。但無論你如何努力，卻連一絲能量都提不起來。

 

 同時，健身房保持不變。只有體臭和汗水的氣味會影響你的感官。

 

 你皺起了眉頭，想起了黑魔法的教義。您需要是女性才能使用它們。在這種形式下，你肯定不是。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 

 你很感激閉上眼睛，但你感覺自己的汗毛都豎起來了，太清楚你看不到、幾乎不敢相信的事情了。

 

 
 “我用超出其力量的力量詛咒這個物體，”你快速地低聲說道，“它影響著除了我之外的所有人。”

 

 當你睜開眼睛的時候，一股神奇的氣息從你的體內湧出，照亮了整個房間。健身房裡的怪物蠕動、顫抖。水泡內部的液體起泡並擠壓薄膜。體育館裡的刺鼻氣味越來越濃。

passages.name: SchoolGymRitual
passages.content:
First looking around to see if anyone is watching, you step towards the gym entrance and reach to take the key out of your bag. The old key feels cold to your touch.

 

 You hesitate, just for a moment, as you consider what awaits you inside. The cheerleaders are at practice right now, so you have no doubt you can sneak in undetected. You’ve even considered a good hiding place - behind the mats trolley in a small alcove that stores all the gym equipment.

 

 But if they are about to perform the ritual, you don’t know what will happen. Whether you are powerful enough to stop it. Whether you can do it undetected. And what will it mean if you fail. Both for Terrence. And Emily. 

 

 Actions

 <>

 <>

 
 You survey the school, eyes sharp for another entrance. You notice a small alley running down the side of the school walls. Halfway along, you spot it. A heavy, rusted emergency door, cracked open by only a sliver.

 

 Your breath catches, considering what awaits you inside. The cheerleaders are at practice right now, so you have no doubt you can sneak in undetected. You’ve even considered a good hiding place - behind the mats trolley in a small alcove that stores all the gym equipment.

 

 But if they are about to perform the ritual, you don’t know what will happen. Whether you are powerful enough to stop it. Can you go undetected? And what will it mean if you fail.

 
And in this current form, you can’t even cast spells. You wonder if this is really such a good idea.

 

 Actions

 <>

 <>

 
 You step towards the gym entrance and reach to the handle, but hesitate. Just for a moment, as you consider what awaits you inside. The cheerleaders are at practice right now, but they will perform the ritual after they’ve finished. You wonder whether you are powerful enough to stop it. And what it will mean if you succeed. Both for Terrence. And Emily.

passages.name: SchoolGymEntranceHeatherDialogue0
passages.content:
Heather glances at you briefly, before turning her attention back to the floor. You try to catch her eye, but she has mastered the art of staring directly downwards, avoiding your attempts to engage in any sort of interaction.
 
 
 As you approach Heather, her large eyes flick up from the floor and land upon you. Her delicate lips cast a sad smile towards you. “Hello,” she says, softly.

 Heather is just outside the gym doors, tugging at the hem of her skirt as if to make sure it sits right. Her gym bag is hugged tight against her chest, damp hair falling loose around her face.

 

 When she sees you, her lips part slightly, then close again. A faint blush creeps across her cheeks.

 

 “Oh… hi, Dennis,” she says, voice quiet but not unkind. Her eyes dart away almost immediately, fixing on the floor tiles. She shifts her bag from one arm to the other, nervous energy radiating from every small movement.

 

 For a moment she looks like she might say more, but then she just tucks a strand of hair behind her ear and offers a tiny, fleeting smile.
 

 Heather glances at you briefly, before turning her attention back to the floor.

 Heather looks up from the floor, expectantly.

passages.content_zh:
希瑟短暫地看了你一眼，然後將注意力轉回地板上。你試圖引起她的注意，但她已經掌握了直接向下凝視的藝術，避免你嘗試進行任何形式的互動。
 
 
 當你走近希瑟時，她的大眼睛從地板上抬起來落在你身上。她嬌嫩的嘴唇向你露出悲傷的微笑。 「你好，」她輕聲說。

 希瑟就在健身房門外，拉著裙子的下擺，好像是為了確保它位置正確。她的健身包緊緊地抱在胸前，濕漉漉的頭髮散落在臉上。

 

 當她看到你時，她的嘴唇微微張開，然後又閉上。她的臉頰上泛起淡淡的紅暈。

 

 「喔……嗨，丹尼斯，」她說，聲音很平靜，但並不刻薄。她的目光幾乎立刻就移開了，固定在地磚上。她把包包從一隻手臂移到另一隻手臂，每個小動作都散發出緊張的能量。

 

 有一瞬間，她看起來似乎還想再說更多，但隨後她只是將一縷頭髮別到耳後，露出一個稍縱即逝的微笑。
 

 希瑟短暫地看了你一眼，然後將注意力轉回地板上。

 希瑟從地板上抬起頭，滿懷期待。

passages.name: SchoolGymPhotos
passages.content:
First checking around to see if anyone is watching, you reach into your bag and take out the photos from the selection you took from the basement.

You find some used gum stuck to one of the lockers and spend a few minutes carefully pinning up the complete spread across the lockers in the corridor. When there are no photos left, you step back and survey your work. 

Almost every locker in this part of the corridor is ‘decorated’ with a photo of Heather. All of which feature her in the school gymnasium. In most of them, she’s naked. In some of them, she’s on her hands and knees. In a few of the most risqué of the lot, she’s on her back, legs spread, as she works to clean under some of the equipment. 

You consider what will happen tomorrow morning. If the teachers find them first, you imagine there'll be some very awkward conversations for Heather and some members of the faculty.

And if they don’t, every student will see what Heather really does when she cleans after school. There will be no coming back from that. Her life, at least at Charlton, will be ruined.

Christina should love this, you can only hope.

**Actions**

<>
passages.content_zh:
先檢查周圍是否有人在看，然後你把手伸進包包裡，從地下室拿出照片。

你發現其中一個儲物櫃上黏著一些用過的口香糖，你花了幾分鐘小心地將整塊口香糖釘在走廊的置物櫃上。當沒有照片留下時，您退後一步並審視您的作品。 

走廊這一部分的幾乎每個儲物櫃都「裝飾」著希瑟的照片。所有這些都以她在學校體育館為主角。在大多數照片中，她都是赤裸裸的。在其中一些照片中，她是用手和膝蓋著地的。在其中一些最有傷風化的場景中，她仰面躺著，雙腿張開，正在清理一些設備下面的東西。 

你考慮明天早上會發生什麼事。如果老師們先找到他們，你可以想像希瑟和一些教職人員將會進行一些非常尷尬的對話。

如果他們不這樣做，每個學生都會看到希瑟放學後打掃時到底做了什麼。那樣就再也回不來了。她的生活，至少在查爾頓的生活，將會被毀掉。

克里斯蒂娜應該喜歡這個，你只能希望。

**行動**

<>

passages.name: SchoolKitchen
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Kitchen**

[image: ./images/School_Kitchen.png]

A stench of fried food and mass-cooked meat threatens to overwhelm your nostrils. The kitchen is large enough to cook on a scale for the entire school, but is cramped and claustrophobic.

 Laid out on every surface is enough food for the day, all freshly prepared and wrapped in cellophane, ready for the rowdy lunch time queues. You notice that each one is wrapped individually, and each one has a name tag of the specific student it has been made for.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 [[Go to the Cafeteria->SchoolCafeteria]] 

 The tables are bare, as people have eaten all the food for the day. You’ll have to come back again early before lunch.

 

 **Actions**

 [[Go to the Cafeteria->SchoolCafeteria]]
passages.content_zh:
**查爾頓學校 - 廚房**

[圖：./images/School_Kitchen.png]

油炸食品和熟肉的惡臭可能會淹沒您的鼻孔。廚房足夠大，可以為整個學校進行大規模烹飪，但廚房很狹窄，令人產生幽閉恐懼症。

 每個表面上都擺滿了一天所需的食物，所有食物都是新鮮準備的，並用玻璃紙包裹起來，準備迎接午餐時間的排隊。您會注意到，每一件都是單獨包裝的，每一件都有一個為特定學生製作的姓名標籤。

 

 **行動**

 
 <>

 [[前往食堂->學校食堂]]

 桌子上空無一人，因為人們已經吃完了一天的食物。你必須在午餐前早點回來。

 

 **行動**

 [[前往食堂->學校食堂]]

passages.name: SchoolKitchenInvestigation
passages.content:
You focus your magical energies to reveal any objects embedded with magic. Sure enough, the food lights up like a fireworks night. Magical wisps snake across the table, passing through each meal over and over again. Residual magic lingers on the food, glittering like gaudy seasoning.

 
 
 Actions

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 <>
 
 
 You try to focus your magical energies to reveal any objects embedded with magic, but instead of the warm stirring of power you just feel dead. The food on the table looks just as it should do.

 

 Sighing, you realise that in this form, you can't use magic. This extends, seemingly, to sensing it too.

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 
 You close your eyes and concentrate, visualising the kitchen in your mind. Like an out-of-body experience, you hover over the kitchen table, watching the magic race through the school meals, over and over again.

 

 
 “I cause this object with power beyond its power,” you whisper. “Unseen and unheard, its effects will be felt by all but me.”

 

 The wisps phase in and out, as if underneath a strobe light. They each move through their routine with increased pace, crackling and flashing with energy. Then they appear to return to normal, slowing to a more leisurely pace than before.

 

 You cautiously prod one of them with your finger. You feel nothing from it.

 
 “I cause this object with power beyond its power,” you say. “Unseen and unheard, its effects will be felt by none.”

 

 The wisps begin to speed up, moving faster and faster. Frantic even. You watch as their tails start to evaporate. As if being sucked up by an unseen hoover, the wisps grow shorter and shorter, racing around in a pattern, trying in vain to escape their fate.

 

 You almost think you hear an inhuman scream when the top of the wisps dissipate, leaving clean, unblemished food behind.

 
 
 <>
 
 

 Actions

 <>
passages.content_zh:
你集中你的魔法能量來揭示任何嵌入魔法的物體。果然，食物像烟花之夜一样亮起来。神奇的小精靈蜿蜒穿過餐桌，一遍又一遍地穿過每餐。殘留的魔法殘留在食物上，像華麗的調味料一樣閃閃發光。

 
 
 行動

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 <>
 
 
 你試圖集中你的魔法能量來揭示任何嵌入魔法的物體，但你並沒有感受到溫暖的力量，而是感到死了。桌子上的食物看起来就理所应当。

 

 嘆了口氣，你發現在這個形態下，你無法使用魔法。看起来，这也延伸到了感知它。

 

 行動

 <>
 
 
 你閉上眼睛，集中註意力，在腦海中想像廚房。就像一種靈魂出竅的體驗，你懸停在廚房的桌子上，一遍又一遍地觀看學校膳食中的魔法比賽。

 

 
 「我讓這個物體擁有了超越其力量的力量，」你低聲說道。 “除了我之外，所有人都會感受到其看不見、聞所未聞的影響。”

 

 縷縷光芒漸進漸進，就像在閃光燈下一樣。他們每個人都加快了節奏，劈啪作響，閃爍著能量。然後他們似乎恢復了正常，放慢了比以前更悠閒的步伐。

 

 你小心翼翼地用手指戳戳其中一个。你从中感觉不到什么。

 
 「我用超出其能力的力量來創造這個物體，」你說。 “看不見、聞所未聞，它的影響無人能感受到。”

 

 那些小精靈開始加速，移動得越來越快。甚至瘋狂。你看着它们的尾巴开始蒸发。就好像被看不見的吸塵器吸走一樣，這些小精靈變得越來越短，以一種圖案的方式到處亂跑，徒勞地試圖逃脫自己的命運。

 

 當小精靈頂部消散，留下乾淨、無瑕疵的食物時，你幾乎會覺得自己聽到了一聲非人的尖叫聲。

 
 
 <>
 
 

 行動

 <>

passages.name: MathsClass0
passages.content:
You flick through to the page in the textbook and are presented with a number of illustrations depicting caricatures of housewives standing in kitchens looking flustered. Wondering if you have the right page, you flick through to the page Rogers told the boys to turn to. Like before, it’s full of illustrations, but this page depicts football players on a field.

 

 “Struggling with instruction again, Miss. Phillips?” you hear from the side of you. You look up and see Rogers looking down over the rim of his glasses.

 

 He reaches down and turns your book back to the pages of the housewives. Then, he resumes walking up and down the class.

 

 “First question on page twenty-seven. For the ladies. Mary is trying to cook her husband a sponge cake. She is following a recipe for a family of five. Her book tells her to use 225g softened butter, 225g golden caster sugar, 4 large eggs and 225g self-raising flour. As her cake is only for two people, how much of each ingredient should she use?

 

 You consider the question. At least it requires maths. You begin to work, writing the answers against the picture in the book. The exercise should be basic for you, but your mind feels fuzzy. The stifling heat is getting to you. Or maybe it’s the presence of the clock that looks over the classroom.

 

 “Now for the boys,” you hear Rogers say. “Using all his energy, Jack can throw a football eighty yards. His teammate, Todd, is forty yards down the pitch. How much energy must Jack use to reach him?”

 

 Assuming you should wait for the next question, you stare down at the book, feeling your eyes grow heavy. The heat is making you more drowsy and Rogers' monotone voice isn’t helping. You feel an urge to look up, at least crack your neck and focus on something that isn’t the blurring textbook in front of you.

 

 But if you look up, you know exactly what you will see. That ticking clock, looming over you.
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 Mr. Rogers moves the class through problem af
passages.content_zh:
你翻到教科書的這一頁，就會看到許多插圖，描繪了家庭主婦站在廚房裡，一臉慌亂的樣子。你想知道你是否找到了正確的頁面，所以你快速瀏覽到了羅傑斯告訴孩子們要翻到的頁面。和以前一樣，它充滿了插圖，但此頁面描繪了球場上的足球員。

 

 「菲利普斯小姐，教學又遇到困難了嗎？」你從你身邊聽到。你抬起頭，看到羅傑斯從眼鏡框往下看。

 

 他彎下腰，把你的書翻回家庭主婦的書頁。然後，他繼續在教室裡走來走去。

 

 「第27頁的第一個問題，女士們。瑪麗正在嘗試為她丈夫做一個海綿蛋糕。她正在按照一個五口之家的食譜做。她的書上告訴她要用225克軟化黃油、225克金砂糖、4個大雞蛋和225克自發麵粉。由於她的蛋糕只適合兩個人吃，她每種材料應該用多少？

 

 你考慮一下這個問題。至少需要數學。你開始工作，根據書上的圖片寫下答案。這項練習對你來說應該是基本的，但你的思維卻感覺模糊。令人窒息的炎熱正向你襲來。也或許是因為時鐘的存在俯瞰著教室。

 

 「現在輪到男孩們了，」你聽到羅傑斯說。 「利用他所有的能量，傑克可以將足球扔出八十碼。他的隊友托德距離球場四十碼。傑克需要消耗多少能量才能到達他身邊？ 」

 

 假設你應該等待下一個問題，你低頭盯著書本，感覺眼睛變得沉重。炎熱讓你更加昏昏欲睡，羅傑斯單調的聲音也無濟於事。你有一種向上看的衝動，至少扭動你的脖子，把注意力集中在你面前模糊的教科書之外的東西上。

 

 但如果你抬頭看，你就會清楚知道會看到什麼。那滴答作響的時鐘，在你頭頂上若隱若現。
 
 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>

 羅傑斯先生透過問題來帶動全班的學習

passages.name: MathsClass
passages.content:
You pick up a pencil and hurriedly write the answer to his question in your exercise book. He looks at you curiously as he passes you, but says nothing.

 

 Letting out a quiet sigh, you spend the rest of the lesson answering the questions to Roger’s maths questions. More than once you find the scenarios demeaning, but you concentrate on the theory behind them.

 

 When the bell rings, you put away your book and stand up, satisfied with your efforts. You try to avoid looking at Rogers as you exit, but see his confused expression out of the corner of your eye.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 
 
 You stare at the pictures in your book, but your mind begins to drift. You wonder what sort of school uses baking and flower decorating to teach maths. Then a voice whispers inside your brain what you already know. This sort of school.

 

 A crack of a hardback book brings you out of your daydream. You look up to see Mr. Rogers towering over you. 
 
 

 "Pay attention, Miss. Phillips," he barks at you. "Or do you want to find yourself back in detention this evening?"

 

 His cold smile may appear normal to the other students, but to you there's something more shared between you. Knowing what you did. What you asked him to do to you.

 

 The moment passes and Mr. Rogers walks away, leaving you to do the lesson. He says nothing more about joining him after school. As if he's content to leave you to decide for yourself.

passages.name: MathsDetention0
passages.content:
After a long few minutes, the silence is broken as the door to the classroom swings open. Mr. Rogers strides in, glancing at you as he enters.

 

 “Ah, Miss. Phillips,” he says coldly. “So glad you could join me.”

 

 Rogers eyes you for a moment longer, then takes out a key and locks the door behind him. You watch as he walks over to his desk, standing behind it. His eyes momentarily roam over your body, lingering on your legs underneath the desk.

 

 “I’ll speak plainly, Miss. Phillips,” he tells you, returning to look at your face. “Your behaviour in class is unacceptable and your marks are well below a passing grade. Fortunately for you, my tutorage after school has proven effective.”

 

 He reaches underneath his desk and you hear a click. When his hands return you see an object resting on his palms. A bamboo school cane, complete with a curved handle.

 

 “It’s quite simple, Miss. Phillips. If you want me to forgive your exploits, and if you wish to pass this class, you will come over here, willingly and without question. And,” he pauses, with a relish for the drama. “Assume the position.”

 

 He taps the cane on the edge of his desk. You feel your heart sink as the situation crystallises for you. Your eyes linger on the cane, and the cold smile of your Maths tutor, as he stares back at you.

 

 Actions

 <>

 <>
 
 You stare at him, feeling the conflict pumping through your body. Emily has to graduate, you think.

 

 *Is this the price you're willing to pay for it?*

 

 With a deep sigh, you push back your desk and stand on your heels. Your eyes from the cane in his hand to his icy smile. You know exactly what is in-store for you.

 

 Wordlessly, you move up to the desk. Rogers watches you from the other side, as you put your palms face down and lean over, feeling your skirt rise up your legs as you do so.

 

 Mr. Rogers watches you for a moment as you look up at him. Then he slowly walks around the desk. You begin to watch him move, but he shakes
passages.content_zh:
漫長的幾分鐘後，教室的門打開了，打破了沉默。羅傑斯先生大步走進來，進來時看了你一眼。

 

 「啊，菲利普斯小姐，」他冷冷地說。 “很高興你能加入我。”

 

 羅傑斯又看了你一會兒，然後拿出一把鑰匙，把門鎖在身後。你看著他走到辦公桌前，站在辦公桌後面。他的目光短暫地掃過你的身體，最後停留在你桌下的雙腿上。

 

 「我會直白地講，菲利普斯小姐，」他告訴你，然後回頭看著你的臉。 “你在課堂上的行為是不可接受的，你的成績遠低於及格分數。對你來說幸運的是，我的課後輔導被證明是有效的。”

 

 他把手伸到桌子底下，你聽到喀喀聲。當他的手回來時，你會看到一個物體放在他的手掌上。竹製學校手杖，配有彎曲手柄。

 

 「這很簡單，菲利普斯小姐。如果你想讓我原諒你的功績，如果你想通過這門課，你就自願地、毫無疑問地來這裡。然後，」他停了下來，帶著對戲劇的津津有味。 “擔任職務。”

 

 他用拐杖輕敲桌子邊緣。當情況對你來說變得具體時，你會感到心沉下去。你的目光停留在拐杖上，還有你的數學老師凝視著你時冷冷的微笑。

 

 行動

 <>

 <>
 
 你盯著他，感覺衝突在你的身體裡湧動。你認為艾米麗必須畢業了。

 

 *這是您願意支付的價格嗎？ *

 

 深深嘆了一口氣，你推開辦公桌，站了起來。你的目光從他手中的拐杖轉向他冰冷的笑容。您確切地知道商店裡有什麼適合您。

 

 你一言不發地走到辦公桌前。羅傑斯從另一邊看著你，當你手掌朝下並傾身時，感覺你的裙子在你這樣做時從腿上抬起。

 

 當你抬頭看羅傑斯先生時，他注視了你一會兒。然後他慢慢地繞著桌子走。你開始看著他移動，但他顫抖著

passages.name: SchoolMathsInvestigation
passages.content:
**Charlton School - Maths Room**

 [image: ./images/School_Maths_Room.png]

 
Walls are covered with formula and maths equations. Single wooden desks are spaced out evenly, each weathered but free of graffiti. A sturdy desk rests in front of a sturdy blackboard, with faded chalk maths questions etched into it.

 

 You enter the maths room, quietly and quickly. You check to make sure it’s empty. When satisfied, you close the door behind you and look around.

 

 The eerie silence keeps you alert. The only sound comes from the clock above the blackboard, its ominous ticking marking each second you spend in danger.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 <>

 
 You stare up at the clock, letting the magical energies inside you bubble up and spread across your body.

 

 With the time to focus, immediately the curse becomes clear. Yellow tentacles made of the arcane arts writhe out of the clock, each swaying around the room as if trying to find a target.

 

 You keep from their reach, backing up against the wall of the classroom.

 

 **Actions**

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 <>
 
 
 You stare up at the clock, trying to summon the magical energies inside you. But try as you might, nothing comes.

 

 The quiet ticking of the clock continues to fill the room. You see nothing more.

 

 The realisation comes quickly. In order to use magic, you need to be a girl.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 

 Closing your eyes, you reach out to the clock with your mind. The tentacles appear to sense you, suddenly diverting their attention from the room and towards you as your mind moves towards the clock.

 

 As your mind edges towards it, one skims past you. You feel a twinge of vacancy in your brain. Your concentration threatens to slip.

 

 Regathering your focus, you push forward, slipping your projection around the tentacle's frenzied movements. Then you reach your goal. Bright and warm, the magic from the curse crackles around the clock.

passages.name: ThompsonsBossOfficeSearch
passages.content:
You move around the desk and begin rifling through the drawers. Fortunately, they aren’t locked. Thompson must be content with just keeping his office door secure. 

In the middle drawer you find what you are looking for. A ring of keys sits on top of some cardboard folders, labelled with locations and addresses. You quickly flick through the keyring, noting that Thompson seems to have access to a wide variety of shops and homes across Charlton.

About halfway around the ring, you see a key labelled ‘Charlton Hotel - Basement’. Hands shaking from nerves, you pull the key off the ring and put it on the office keychain you took from Annie.

 As you do so, you notice the next key on the ring is marked up as ‘Charlton School - Business Studies Room’. As you already have a key for this room, you leave it be. You briefly search for other keys for Charlton School, but strangely you can’t find another one.

 As you do so, you notice the next key on the ring is marked up as ‘Charlton School - Business Studies Room’. You smile at your luck and take it off the ring too. Strangely enough, there aren’t any other keys labelled for Charlton.

As you go to put the keyring back, something in the desk drawer catches your eye. The label on the top folder in the drawer. "Charlton School - Candidates Assessment." 

You glance up at the door back into the office, well aware of what would happen if you were caught. But the folder calls to you.

**Actions**

<>
 
 
 
 
 
 

<>
passages.content_zh:
你在桌子周圍走來走去，開始翻抽屜。幸運的是，它們沒有上鎖。湯普森一定滿足於保持辦公室大門的安全。 

在中間的抽屜裡，您可以找到您想要的東西。一些紙板文件夾的頂部有一圈鑰匙，上面標有位置和地址。您快速瀏覽了一下鑰匙圈，注意到湯普森似乎可以進入查爾頓的各種商店和住宅。

大約在環的中間，您會看到一把標有“查爾頓酒店 - 地下室”的鑰匙。你的手因緊張而顫抖，你把鑰匙從戒指上拔下來，放在你從安妮那裡拿來的辦公室鑰匙圈上。

 當您這樣做時，您會注意到戒指上的下一把鑰匙被標記為“查爾頓學校 - 商業研究室”。由於您已經擁有該房間的鑰匙，因此您可以保留它。您短暫地搜尋了查爾頓學校的其他鑰匙，但奇怪的是您找不到另一把鑰匙。

 當您這樣做時，您會注意到戒指上的下一把鑰匙被標記為“查爾頓學校 - 商業研究室”。你對自己的運氣微笑，然後把它也從戒指上取下來。奇怪的是，沒有任何其他鑰匙標記為查爾頓。

當你準備把鑰匙圈放回去時，書桌抽屜裡的東西吸引了你的注意。抽屜頂部文件夾上的標籤。 “查爾頓學校 - 候選人評估。”

你抬頭看了一眼辦公室的門，很清楚如果你被抓到會發生什麼事。但資料夾在召喚你。

**行動**

<>
 
 
 
 
 
 

<>

passages.name: ThompsonsInvestigation
passages.content:
Your curiosity gets the better of you. You pick up the folder and open it, flicking through the memos and paperwork filed inside it.

 

 A memo instantly catches your eye:

 

 **Subject:** Secretary candidates

 **From:** Peter Stitch

 **To:** Tucker Thompson

 

 As per your correspondence regarding Annie Clancy, it is with sincere regret that I note you are having difficulties. I assure you our assessment during her tenure at Charlton found that her skills evident in her business studies exams made her a good match for your needs. Her reluctance to engage with the ‘softer skills’ of the role (i.e. your use of the word ‘frigid’) is a surprise to all the staff that taught her.

 

 Attached is a number of candidates from our current senior year. If any of these candidates prove interesting, we would be more than happy to set up an interview in advance of The Prom as a replacement for the role ready for when they graduate.
 
 

 Regards,

 

 Peter Stitch,

 Headmaster, Charlton Upper School

 

 As you finish reading the memo, a sound from outside the office makes you jump. You look up, frozen in place, feeling your blood pumping through your body. But you hear nothing more.

 

 Actions

 <>

 <>

 Not wanting to spend any more time than necessary in this room, you decide to leave the folder as it is. You put the keyring back in the drawer and close it shut.

 

 Actions

 <>

 Thumbing your way through the attachments, you see a number of one-page files on students - all women - that you recognise as seniors at Charlton School. Each with a photo, description of their academic achievements, and somewhat disturbingly, a physical description too.

 

 Your heart leaps when you see on a file a picture of your sister, Emily.

 

 
 
 **Candidate summary:** Miss Phillips has demonstrated enthusiasm for Business Studies classes, taking direction from her male students admirably. In her short time at Charlton, she has already been invited to Mrs. DeBussy’s after school
passages.content_zh:
你的好奇心戰勝了你。你拿起資料夾並打開它，快速瀏覽裡面的備忘錄和文件。

 

 一份備忘錄立刻吸引了你的注意：

 

 **主題：** 秘書候選人

 **來自：** 彼得史迪奇

 **致：**塔克·湯普森

 

 根據您關於安妮·克蘭西的信件，我真誠地遺憾地註意到您遇到了困難。我向您保證，我們在她在查爾頓任職期間的評估發現，她在商科考試中表現出的技能使她非常適合您的需求。她不願意接觸這個角色的「軟技能」（即你使用「冷漠」這個詞），這讓所有教過她的員工感到驚訝。

 

 附件是我們當前高年級的一些候選人。如果這些候選人中的任何一個被證明是有趣的，我們非常樂意在畢業舞會之前安排一次面試，作為他們畢業時準備的職位的替代者。
 
 

 問候，

 

 彼得史蒂奇,

 查爾頓高中部校長

 

 當你讀完備忘錄時，辦公室外傳來的聲音讓你嚇了一跳。你抬起頭，僵在原地，感覺血液在全身流動。但你什麼也沒聽到。

 

 行動

 <>

 <>

 由於不想在這個房間裡花費不必要的時間，您決定將資料夾保留原樣。你把鑰匙圈放回抽屜裡，然後關上抽屜。

 

 行動

 <>

 翻閱附件，您會看到一些關於學生（所有女性）的單頁文件，您認為這些學生是查爾頓學校的高年級學生。每個人都有一張照片，描述他們的學術成就，還有一些令人不安的外表描述。

 

 當您在文件中看到您妹妹艾米麗的照片時，您的心會感到興奮。

 

 
 
 **候選人摘要：** 菲利普斯小姐表現出了對商業研究課程的熱情，令人欽佩地接受了男學生的指導。在查爾頓短暫的時間裡，她已經被邀請去德比西夫人的放學後

**Thompson's Handyman Repairs - Office**

[image: ./images/Thompsons_Office.png]

The small room is full of desks and ancient computers that you somehow have to make work as a contemporary office.

 

 <>
 <>
 
Annie sits behind a cramped desk by the side of Thompson's office, staring at a bulky computer screen intensely. Whatever she now does seems to take a little more of her attention. She doesn't even look up as you enter.
 
 
 
You enter the back office and walk over to your desk. Your boss, Mr. Thompson, has been particularly angry lately thanks to the constant bugs in the booking system you’re trying to reprogram. You have a lot of work that you should be getting on with, but thoughts of Emily’s plight fill your mind. Perhaps doing some online research would be a better use of your time.

passages.name: ThompsonsOfficeAnnieDialogue0
passages.content:
She glances up at you, but uncharacteristically doesn’t keep your eye. “Hey,” she says to you, looking down.

 
 
 She looks up at you, but doesn’t smile. “Oh hi, .” She seems reluctant to look at you, but tries to meet your eye.

 
 
 
 Annie looks at you coldly. She doesn't want to speak to you.

 
 
 You stand over the small desk next to Thompson's office and glance down at Annie. “Hey Annie, how are you?”

 

 
 She looks up at you and politely smiles. “Oh hi, ." Her eyes flicker from the screen to you, distracted.

 
 She looks up at you and smiles quickly. “Oh hi, .” She glances at the monitor again, but appears to make a decision and pushes her chair back slightly, revealing her short-skirt and bare legs from underneath the desk.

 
 
 
 Annie glances back at the monitor, then looks back to you, waiting impatiently.

 
 Annie looks at the monitor again, but then turns her attention back to you, bouncing one leg on top of the other.

 
 
 
 

**Actions**

 Speak to Annie about what happened at the library - This probably should be discussed in private. Visit Annie's apartment in the evening to talk about this.

passages.content_zh:
她抬頭看了你一眼，但一反常態地沒有看你的眼睛。 「嘿，」她低頭對你說。

 
 
 她抬頭看著你，但沒有微笑。 「喔嗨，」她似乎不願意看你，但試圖與你對視。

 
 
 
 安妮冷冷地看著你。她不想和你說話。

 
 
 你站在湯普森辦公室旁邊的小桌子旁，低頭看著安妮。 “嘿，安妮，你好嗎？”

 

 
 她抬頭看著你，禮貌地微笑。 「喔嗨，」她的目光從螢幕上閃爍到你身上，心不在焉。

 
 她抬頭看著你，很快就笑了。 「哦嗨，」她又看了一眼顯示器，但似乎做出了決定，將椅子稍微向後推了一些，露出了桌子下面的短裙和裸露的雙腿。

 
 
 
 安妮回頭看了一眼顯示器，然後又看了看你，不耐煩地等待著。

 
 安妮再次看著監視器，然後將注意力轉回你身上，將一條腿放在另一條腿上。

passages.name: WorkGeneric
passages.content:
Free of distractions from anyone else in the office, you boot up the antique machine and get on with some work. The backlog of bugs seems only to be increasing, so you pick the top of the priority list. You begin to analyse what could be going wrong.

 

 
 [Ditzy] *The silence of the office helps, but not much. Your mind keeps slipping away - thinking about what was muttered on the breakfast show you recently watched. What should be obvious now just feels frustratingly out of reach.*

 
 
 You sit down in front of the ancient machine that Thompson has assigned you to undertake your work. The cheap fan in the desktop case sputters when you switch it on. The CRT monitor takes ages to heat up.

 

 Finally, once the old system boots up, you start by looking through the list of bugs and analysing the top issue, mulling over what the cause could be.

 

 
 [Ditzy] *You rub your temples. Concentration feels like wading through treacle. The error messages blur into each other, and for some reason you keep thinking about your hair. You know this stuff - or at least, you used to. So why is it so hard to focus now?*

 
 

 Your fingers move fast, intuition guiding each keystroke. One bug collapses, then another, then a third. Everything lines up like you planned it. For once, the system seems to respond to your work. You lean back, the rare feeling of victory settling over you.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 It takes effort, but your concentration holds. One fix, then another. You're in the zone - methodical, focused, capable. By the time you glance at the clock, you've dealt with two significant problems. Thompson might even be pleased.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You manage to get to the bottom of one of the persistent issues. It isn't pretty, but it works. That's all that matters right now. One bug down. Too many left to go.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You make a little progress - some logs understood, a stack trace followed - but the solution's just out of reach. You've started the wor
passages.content_zh:
不受辦公室其他人的干擾，您啟動古董機器並繼續工作。積壓的錯誤似乎只會增加，因此您選擇了優先順序清單的頂部。你開始分析可能出了什麼問題。

 

 
 [Ditzy] *辦公室的寧靜有幫助，但作用不大。你的思緒不斷分心——想著你最近觀看的早餐節目中嘀咕的內容。現在應該顯而易見的事情卻令人沮喪地感到遙不可及。 *

 
 
 你坐在湯普森分配給你的古老機器前進行你的工作。當您打開桌上型電腦機箱中的廉價風扇時，它會發出嘶嘶聲。 CRT 顯示器需要很長時間才能加熱。

 

 最後，一旦舊系統啟動，您首先查看錯誤清單並分析最重要的問題，思考可能的原因。

 

 
 [Ditzy] *你揉揉太陽穴。集中註意力就像涉過糖蜜一樣。錯誤訊息相互模糊，並且出於某種原因，你一直在思考你的頭髮。你知道這些東西——或者至少，你曾經知道。那為什麼現在很難集中註意力呢？ *

 
 

 您的手指快速移動，直覺引導著每次按鍵。一個錯誤崩潰了，然後另一個，然後是第三個。一切都按照你的計劃進行。這一次，系統似乎對你的工作做出了回應。你向後靠去，罕見的勝利感籠罩著你。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 這需要努力，但你的注意力會保持不變。一個修復，然後另一個。你處於最佳狀態——有條理、專注、有能力。當您看向時鐘時，您已經解決了兩個重大問題。湯普森甚至可能會感到高興。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 您設法查清了一個長期存在的問題的真相。它不漂亮，但很有效。這就是現在最重要的事。下降了一個錯誤。剩下的人太多了。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 您取得了一些進展 - 理解了一些日誌，隨後出現了堆疊追蹤 - 但解決方案卻遙不可及。你已經開始工作了

passages.name: WorkResearch
passages.content:
You decide to put work aside for the moment and see if you can find something to help you understand what’s going on.

 

 Your first attempts display pages of results about what you knew about magic before that amulet came into your life. Wikipedia pages on its place in fiction, new age movements selling crystals, pseudoscience articles on affirmations and social media content from bored teenagers.

 

 Trying to narrow your results, you decide to focus your search around Charlton and related topics.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 Unsurprisingly, Charlton has little presence online. Search results mention some of the local businesses and brief summaries of the history of the town that are little more than Wikipedia stubs. The town has existed in one form or another for hundreds of years, serving as a small farming community before the industrial revolution caused it to shrink to little more than a hamlet. 

 

 One thing that does draw your attention is the town being referenced in a history of rural counterculture movements of the 1960s. The surrounding fields and valleys were a hot spot for impromptu gatherings of pagans, hippies and flower children. Free love festivals used the town as a base, filling the sleepy town with Volkswagen vans and the shops and hotel with children of the revolution. 

 

 Then, just as the ‘60s ended, results fall off. The only references to Charlton between then and now are the businesses, the school, the shops. The picture of a sleepy town, untouched by modernity.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 The results from combining Charlton and magic brings up a number of pages detailing the town’s history with counterculture movements and pagan festivals in the ‘60s. Whilst noteworthy, nothing strikes you as being relevant, or indeed real. Even when you take into consideration that ‘real’ to you means something very different than before.

 

 That is until a result stands out. You click on a result page buried in the articles about the town’s hi
passages.content_zh:
你決定暫時把工作放在一邊，看看是否能找到一些東西來幫助你了解正在發生的事情。

 

 你的第一次嘗試會顯示幾頁結果，告訴你在護身符進入你的生活之前你對魔法的了解。維基百科頁面介紹了它在小說中的地位、銷售水晶的新時代運動、關於肯定的偽科學文章以及無聊青少年的社交媒體內容。

 

 為了縮小結果範圍，您決定將搜尋重點放在查爾頓及相關主題上。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 毫不奇怪，查爾頓在網路上的影響力很小。搜尋結果提到了一些當地企業和該鎮歷史的簡短摘要，這些只不過是維基百科的存根而已。這個小鎮已經以這樣或那樣的形式存在了數百年，在工業革命導致它縮小成一個小村莊之前，它一直是一個小型農業社區。 

 

 確實引起您注意的一件事是，這座小鎮在 20 世紀 60 年代農村反文化運動的歷史中被提及。周圍的田野和山谷是異教徒、嬉皮士和花童臨時聚會的熱門場所。自由愛情節以鎮上為基地，沉睡的小鎮擠滿了大眾貨車，商店和旅館裡擠滿了革命的孩子們。 

 

 然後，就在 60 年代結束時，結果開始下滑。從那時到現在，查爾頓唯一的參考就是企業、學校和商店。這是一座沉睡的小鎮，沒有受到現代文明的影響。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 查爾頓與魔術相結合的結果帶來了許多頁面，詳細介紹了該鎮 60 年代反文化運動和異教節日的歷史。雖然值得注意，但沒有什麼讓你覺得相關或真實。即使你考慮到「真實」對你來說意味著與以前非常不同的東西。

 

 直到結果顯露出來。你點擊了一個結果頁面，該頁面埋藏在有關該鎮的嗨的文章中

passages.name: ThompsonsOfficeAnnieWork
passages.content:
You step around Annie's new desk, taking in the clutter and chaotic mess that is Annie's filing system. Folders are sprawled out over the desk. Brightly coloured post-it notes line the edges of the monitor. A dull mouse is livened up by the glittery pink mouse mat it rests on.

 

 Booting up her computer, you check her inbox. Half a dozen emails came in requesting meetings with Thompson overnight. You check his calendar only to find large blocks are blocked out. The subject blocked from view.

 

 Before you can schedule the first meeting, the phone interrupts you. "Good morning, Thompson's Repairs," you say, echoing the words you've heard from Annie. "Mr. Thompson's office. How might I help you?"

 

 Over the next thirty minutes, you try to answer emails in-between the constant calls. Every time you answer one email, two more arrive. Most of them are minor. Mundane.

 

 “Hey darling, can I get a phone call in with Tucker this afternoon? Need to have a chat about the bill that hit my doormat today.”
“Your boss free for golf Friday?”
“Can you ask the owner if he's interested in changing his stationary order?”
 

 Even the phone calls feel off. It's mostly Thompson's friends and business acquaintances looking for him, but at the sound of your voice they begin to chat. It almost goes without saying that it's exclusively men.

 

 You’re halfway through scheduling a lunch meeting when you hear boots on the carpet.

 

 Gordon appears in the doorway. "Annie," he says, not waiting for a reply. "Where's Phillips?"

 

 "I'm not sure," you feign, trying to think of an excuse.

 

 Gordon grunts. "Need someone to do my receipts. No one's on reception."

 

 You try not to grimace. "Can I help?"

 

 He shakes his head. "Not your job, anymore. Phillips is slacking. Need to have a word with Thompson. He free?"

 

 You eye him with concern. If you don't act now, you'll risk Thompson's wrath. Not to mention his anger at you not showing up to work.

passages.content_zh:
你在安妮的新辦公桌周圍走來走去，觀察安妮的文件系統雜亂無章的情況。文件夾散落在桌子上。顯示器的邊緣排列著色彩鮮豔的便利貼。一隻沉悶的老鼠被它所棲息的閃閃發光的粉紅色滑鼠墊變得活躍起來。

 

 啟動她的計算機，您檢查她的收件匣。連夜收到六封電子郵件，要求與湯普森會面。你檢查他的日曆卻發現大塊的內容都被擋住了。主體被擋住了視線。

 

 在您安排第一次會議之前，電話打斷了您。 「早安，湯普森維修店，」你說，呼應著你從安妮那裡聽到的話語。 “湯普森先生的辦公室。有什麼可以幫您的嗎？”

 

 在接下來的三十分鐘裡，您嘗試在不斷的通話之間回覆電子郵件。您每回覆一封電子郵件，就會收到另外兩封電子郵件。其中大多數都是輕微的。世俗的。

 

 “嘿，親愛的，今天下午我可以和塔克打個電話嗎？需要談談今天落在我門墊上的賬單。”
“你的老闆週五有空去打高爾夫球嗎？”
“你能問問店主是否有興趣改變他的固定訂單嗎？”
 

 連打電話都覺得不舒服。大部分是湯普森的朋友和生意上的熟人在找他，但一聽到你的聲音他們就開始聊天。幾乎不用說，這都是男性。

 

 當你聽到地毯上有靴子的聲音時，你正在安排午餐會議。

 

 戈登出現在門口。 「安妮，」他說道，沒有等待回覆。 “菲利普斯在哪裡？”

 

 「我不確定，」你假裝，試著想出一個藉口。

 

 戈登咕噥道。 “需要有人幫我做收據。接待處沒人。”

 

 你盡量不要扮鬼臉。 “我可以幫忙嗎？”

 

 他搖搖頭。 “不再是你的工作了。菲利普斯偷懶了。需要和湯普森說幾句話。他有空嗎？”

 

 你關切地看著他。如果你現在不採取行動，你就會冒著湯普森憤怒的風險。更別說他對你不去上班的憤怒了。

**Thompson Handyman Repairs - Reception**

[image: ./images/Thompsons_Reception.png]

No more than a room with a cheap desk opposite the entrance and a door on either side. One entrance to the warehouse, where electricians and plumbers keep parts and their vans. The other to the office where bookings come in and, at least in your experience, where people work on persuading customers their quotes are legitimate.

<>

 
 

 
 <>
 
 
Annie looks at you as you approach. Her usual smile fades quickly. “Hello Jennifer,” she says curtly.

 
”Hello," Annie eyes you cautiously. “Can I help you?”
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
"Morning ,” Annie says curtly, her icy glare betraying her professional demeanor. "You should leave."

 
”Hey ,” Annie says with a clipped tone. "The door's just behind you."
 
 
 
"Morning ,” Annie says, wincing. “You shouldn't be here."

 
”Hey ,” Annie says with a grimace. "You should leave before Thompson sees you."
 
 
 
 
It's probably not a good idea to be here after getting fired. You should do what you need to do and leave.

[圖：./images/Thompsons_Reception.png]

只不過是一個房間，入口對面有一張廉價的桌子，兩邊都有門。倉庫的一處入口，電工和水管工存放零件和貨車。另一個是辦公室，在那裡預訂，至少根據您的經驗，人們致力於說服客戶他們的報價是合法的。

當你走近時，安妮看著你。她平常的笑容很快就消失了。 「你好，詹妮弗，」她簡短地說。

 
「你好。」安妮小心翼翼地看著你。 “我可以幫你嗎？”
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
「早安，」安妮簡短地說，她冰冷的目光背叛了她的職業風範。 “你該離開了。”

 
「嘿，」安妮語氣斷斷續續地說。 “門就在你身後。”

被解僱後留在這裡可能不是一個好主意。你應該做你需要做的事情然後離開。

passages.name: ThompsonsReceptionIntern
passages.content:
Annie wastes no time. She begins walking you through the basics - how to answer the phones, greet customers, schedule appointments for Clive and Gordon, and manage Mr. Thompson’s calendar. None of it is particularly hard. It’s repetitive. Monotonous. But there’s something quietly satisfying about it.

 

 Then the bell above the front door rings.

 

 Dan strolls in, loud as ever. You can smell the alcohol on his breath from across the desk. "Afternoon," he says, grinning.

 

 Annie doesn’t miss a beat. "Been busy at lunchtime, Dan?"

 

 He lets out a chuckle, leaning against the counter. "Liquid lunch with John Kaplar. Talking renovations for the country club. Although he mostly wanted to ask how I avoid slicing the ball on the tennis court."

 

 Annie raises an eyebrow, barely concealing her amusement. "Isn’t that a golf term?"

 

 Dan stiffens. "No, no, definitely tennis. Girls wouldn’t understand. Bit advanced, that one."

 

 She offers him a saccharine smile. "Of course."

 

 Then Dan’s eyes land on you. His grin widens. "Well, well. Look who it is. The elusive Miss Phillips. I was starting to think your kept you locked up somewhere."

 

 You shift uncomfortably in your seat.

 

 He leans in slightly. "Didn’t expect to see you behind the desk. Coffee girl now, huh? I like it. Adds a little charm to the place."

 

 Annie gently steps in front of the desk, blocking Dan from you. "Emily’s still being assessed. She doesn’t have time to chat.”

 

 Dan waves a dismissive hand. "Come on, just being friendly. In fact,” he says, stepping around her and leaning over the desk, his face coming close to yours. “She and I should have a little chat. I can tell her about how things really work around here. And Friday afternoons are so dull.”

 

 Annie looks back at you, then to Dan. Her smile fades. “If Mr. Thompson sees her slacking off, it’ll go straight back to the school."

 

 
 “Don’t worry about Thompson,” he replies. Then his voice drops, just a little. "Oth
passages.content_zh:
安妮沒有浪費時間。她開始引導您了解基礎知識 - 如何接聽電話、問候客戶、為克萊夫和戈登安排約會以及管理湯普森先生的日曆。這些都不是特別難。這是重複的。單調。但其中有一些令人悄悄滿足的東西。

 

 然後前門上方的鈴響了。

 

 丹走進來，一如既往地大聲。隔著桌子你都能聞到他呼吸中的酒味。 「下午，」他笑著說。

 

 安妮不會錯過任何一個節拍。 “午餐時間很忙嗎，丹？”

 

 他靠在櫃檯上，輕笑一聲。 “與約翰·卡普拉爾共進午餐。談論鄉村俱樂部的整修。儘管他主要想問我如何避免在網球場上切球。”

 

 安妮揚起一邊眉毛，幾乎掩飾不住她的興致。 “這不是高爾夫術語嗎？”

 

 丹身體僵硬。 “不不，絕對是網球。女孩聽不懂。那個有點高級。”

 

 她給了他一個甜蜜的微笑。 “當然。”

 

 然後丹的目光落在你身上。他的笑容擴大了。 “好吧，好吧。看看是誰。難以捉摸的菲利普斯小姐。我開始認為你把你鎖在某個地方了。”

 

 你在座位上不舒服地移動。

 

 他微微傾身。 “沒想到會在桌子後面看到你。現在是咖啡女孩吧？我喜歡它。為這個地方增添了一點魅力。”

 

 安妮輕輕地走到辦公桌前，擋住了丹。 “艾米麗還在接受評估。她沒有時間聊天。”

 

 丹不屑地揮揮手。 「來吧，只是表現出友好。事實上，」他說，繞過她，靠在桌子上，他的臉靠近你的臉。 “我和她應該聊聊。我可以告訴她這裡的情況是怎樣的。週五下午太無聊了。”

 

 安妮回頭看看你，然後又看看丹。她的笑容消失了。 “如果湯普森先生看到她偷懶，就會直接送回學校。”

 

 
 「不用擔心湯普森，」他回答。然後他的聲音低了下來，只是一點點。 「奧特

passages.name: ThompsonsReceptionLunchtimeCover
passages.content:
As you approach, Annie spots you and practically leaps up from her chair.

 

 "Oh, thank god," she grins. "I’ve been dying to swing by Evans’ - they’ve got this clearance sale on coats, and I swear I saw one with fur trim."

 

 She grabs her long coat from the stand, swishing it around her like a movie star. Even on the soft carpet, her heels click with purpose.

 

 "Maybe I’ll grab a coffee, have a natter with Flo. I’ll be back in an hour… or so." She winks. "Shouldn’t be too hard. Just some emails to write back to."

 

 Without waiting for a reply, she breezes out the door, leaving the faintest trace of expensive perfume in her wake.

 
 You glance over to the reception desk. Annie’s already gone. A large pastel post-it note is stuck to the monitor, the handwriting unmistakably hers - loopy, elegant, and overly familiar:

 

 *“Thanks ! See if you can make a dent in my inbox. See you soon x”
*
 

 The screen is awake, the mail client open and buzzing with unread messages. You sigh and pull out the chair.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 

 
 
 You sit down at Annie’s desk and immediately understand why she was so happy to hand it off.

 

 Her computer hums like an old fridge, the monitor slightly discolored in one corner. The plastic is yellowed with age. And the logo? *“Curstons.”*

 
 
 You frown. You’ve never heard of it. Wait, weren’t they a typewriter manufacturer? That would explain a lot. The keyboard clacks like it’s trying to bite your fingers.

 

 Your own work computer is bad, sure. This one feels like a relic. You half-expect it to ask for a floppy disk. When you tap the mouse, the screen flickers and then loads Annie’s email inbox.

 

 It’s... overflowing.

 

 Dozens of messages sit flagged, unopened, or half-replied to. Most are from customers: people chasing invoices, asking about timelines, confused about missed appointments. You start to read through a few threads.

 

 **From:** Ron Denver

passages.content_zh:
當你走近時，安妮發現了你，幾乎從椅子上跳了起來。

 

 「哦，謝天謝地，」她笑著說。 “我一直很想去埃文斯店 - 他們正在進行大衣清倉促銷，我發誓我看到了一件帶有毛皮裝飾的。”

 

 她從架上抓起長外套，像電影明星一樣在身上晃來晃去。即使在柔軟的地毯上，她的高跟鞋也會有意無意地發出喀噠聲。

 

 「也許我會去喝杯咖啡，和弗洛聊聊。我會在一小時後回來……左右。」她眨了眨眼。 “應該不會太難。只需寫一些電子郵件即可。”

 

 不等回應，她輕快地出了門，身後留下了淡淡的名貴香水味。

 
 你看向接待台。安妮已經走了。顯示器上貼著一張大大的粉彩便利貼，字跡毫無疑問是她的——瘋狂、優雅，而且過於熟悉：

 

 *“謝謝！看看你是否可以在我的收件匣中留下痕跡。很快再見 x”
*
 

 螢幕被喚醒，郵件用戶端打開並充滿未讀訊息。你嘆了口氣，拉開椅子。

 

 **行動**

 <>
 

 
 
 你坐在安妮的辦公桌前，立刻明白為什麼她這麼高興把它交給你。

 

 她的電腦像一台舊冰箱一樣嗡嗡作響，顯示器的一個角落稍微變色了。隨著時間的推移，塑膠會泛黃。還有標誌？ *「柯斯頓」。 *

 
 
 你皺起眉頭。你從來沒有聽過它。等等，他們不是打字機製造商嗎？這可以解釋很多事情。鍵盤發出嘎嘎聲，就像要咬住你的手指一樣。

 

 當然，你自己的工作電腦很糟糕。這個感覺就像是個遺跡。你有一半期望它會要求一張軟碟。當您點擊滑鼠時，螢幕會閃爍，然後載入安妮的電子郵件收件匣。

 

 它……溢出來了。

 

 數十條訊息被標記、未開啟或回覆了一半。大多數來自客戶：人們追索發票，詢問時間表，對錯過的約會感到困惑。你開始閱讀一些線索。

 

 **來自：** 羅恩·丹佛

passages.name: ThompsonsReceptionSecretaryJob
passages.content:
You’re juggling the phones, trying to triage a packed inbox that just won’t stop growing. Every time you answer one email, two more arrive. Most of them are minor. Mundane.

 

 “Can I push my appointment by fifteen minutes?”
“Where do I park?”
“Can you pop a reminder in my calendar?”
 

 You were once a programmer. Now you’re being asked to babysit inboxes and book diary slots like someone’s flustered personal assistant.

 

 Even the phone calls feel off. Customers want to chat. "How's your day going?" they ask, like you're just another sunny voice on the other end. They don’t care about the work. They just want the performance.

 

 You’re halfway through flagging a broken link in the supplier portal when you hear boots on the carpet.

 

 Gordon appears in the doorway. "Phillips," he says, not waiting for a reply. He’s already striding toward you, a creased folder tucked under his arm.

 

 He stops in front of the desk and sets it down without ceremony.

 

 "Expenses," he says. "Thompson says they won’t go through unless they’re done today."

 

 He taps the folder once, then looks at you.

 

 "I don’t do forms. Computers either. This stuff," he adds, gesturing at the monitor and keyboard. "Not for me." He shrugs. "Women’s work, really. But you can do it, yeah?"

 

 There’s no edge in his tone. No apology, either. Just a task. Like handing you a wrench. You're here. You’ve got fingers. And he’s got receipts.

 
 You’ve barely sat down before the inbox pings. Three new queries. All trivial. Someone wants to push an appointment back by ten minutes. Another can’t find your street on Maps. Someone else wants to know if you can send a technician who’s "good with cats."

 

 Then come the boots. The door to the office opens. Gordon. Again.

 

 He steps into view with that same folder tucked under one arm. All creased, bulging, edges frayed. The same as last time. Maybe literally the same.

 

 "Phillips," he says, flat. Nods at the desk. "Expenses. Thompson says
passages.content_zh:
你一边摆弄着手机，一边试图对不断增长的已装满的收件箱进行分类。您每回覆一封電子郵件，就會收到另外兩封電子郵件。其中大多數都是輕微的。世俗的。

 

 “我可以把預約推遲十五分鐘嗎？”
“我該把車停在哪裡？”
“你能在我的日曆中彈出提醒嗎？”
 

 你曾經是一名程式設計師。现在，你被要求像某人慌乱的私人助理一样照看收件箱并预订日记栏位。

 

 連打電話都覺得不舒服。客戶想聊天。 “你今天过得怎么样？”他们问道，就好像你只是电话那头另一个阳光明媚的声音一样。他們不關心工作。他們只是想要表演。

 

 当您在供应商门户中标记损坏的链接时，您听到了靴子踩在地毯上的声音。

 

 戈登出現在門口。 “菲利普斯，”他说，没有等待答复。他已经大步朝你走来，腋下夹着一个有折痕的文件夹。

 

 他在桌子前停下来，毫不客气地把它放下。

 

 「費用，」他說。 “汤普森说，除非今天完成，否则他们不会通过。”

 

 他點擊了文件夾一次，然後看著你。

 

 “我不做表格。也不做电脑。这些东西，”他指着显示器和键盘补充道。 「不適合我。」他聳聳肩。 “确实是女人的工作。但你能做到，是吗？”

 

 他的語氣中沒有任何鋒芒。也沒有道歉。只是一個任務。就像遞給你一把扳手一樣。你在這裡。你有手指。他有收據。

 
 你剛坐下，收件匣就響起了鈴聲。三個新查詢。一切都是微不足道的。有人想將約會延後十分鐘。另一個人在地圖上找不到您的街道。其他人想知道您是否可以派出一位“擅長貓”的技術人員。

 

 然後是靴子。辦公室的門打開了。戈登。再一次。

 

 他走進視野，一隻手臂下夾著同一個文件夾。所有的東西都有摺痕、鼓起、邊緣磨損。和上次一樣。也許字面意思是一樣的。

 

 「菲利普斯，」他平淡地說。對著辦公桌點點頭。 「費用。湯普森說

passages.name: ThompsonsReceptionSecretaryResponses
passages.content:
You give Gordon a small, playful smile. "Maybe I’ll have a word with Mr. Thompson," you say, voice light. "Tell him you’re flat out and could use a hand."

 

 Gordon doesn’t blink. Doesn’t smile. "Just get it done," he mutters, already turning back toward the shop floor.

 

 So much for charm.

 

 You glance down at the paper stack of receipts he’s left you. Then at the mounting wave of emails flooding the inbox. You need to act fast. Opening a new message, you start typing.

 

 Hi Mr. Thompson. Gordon’s totally snowed under today. He asked me to help with his expenses, and of course I didn’t want to let the company down 😉 Just wondering if I could take a little time to get them sorted properly. Promise I’ll be very grateful… x
 

 You hover for a moment. Then hit send. The reply comes back faster than expected.

 

 End of the week’s fine. If you need to stay late to catch up… I’ll be working late too. We can keep each other company.
 

 You stare at the screen. The reply. Something in your stomach turns, but you push it down.

 

 The receipts will get done. The emails will get answered. You got through a tricky situation without letting anything fall apart. Even if you had to pander to the boss.

 

 You try not to think about it too much.

 
 You already know Gordon won’t react to flirting. So you switch it up. "Alright," you say, keeping your tone breezy. "I’ll have a word with Thompson. Buy you a bit of time."

 

 Gordon just grunts. Not impressed. But not arguing either. “Thanks,” he mutters, walking off.

 

 You take the folder and settle back at the desk, already typing up a message.

 

 Hi Mr. Thompson. Just letting you know Gordon’s still up to his neck today. He’s asked me to handle his expenses, but I didn’t want to slow anything down. If we could have a bit of leeway to get them in, I’d really appreciate it 💋
 

 Thompson’s reply lands a minute later.

 

 That’s fine. Maybe you should come into my office later. We can discuss how to make things
passages.content_zh:
你給戈登一個俏皮的小微笑。 「也許我可以和湯普森先生談談，」你輕聲說。 “告訴他你已經精疲力竭了，需要幫忙。”

 

 戈登沒有眨眼。不笑。 「把它完成吧，」他低聲說道，已經轉身走向車間。

 

 魅力就這麼多了。

 

 你低頭看了一眼他留給你的一疊收據。然後，電子郵件如潮水般湧入收件匣。你需要快速行動。開啟一條新訊息，您開始輸入。

 

 嗨，湯普森先生。戈登今天完全被雪覆蓋了。他讓我幫忙支付他的開支，當然我不想讓公司失望😉只是想知道我是否可以花一點時間來妥善整理它們。我保證我會非常感激...x
 

 你懸停了一會兒。然後點擊發送。回覆的速度比預期的快。

 

 週末就好了。如果你需要熬夜趕進度……我也會工作到很晚。我們可以互相陪伴。
 

 你盯著螢幕。回覆.你的胃裡有東西在轉動，但你把它壓下去了。

 

 收據將完成。電子郵件將得到答案。你度過了一個棘手的局面，沒有讓任何事情崩潰。即使你必須迎合老闆。

 

 你盡量不要想太多。

 
 你已經知道戈登不會對調情做出反應。所以你把它切換一下。 「好吧，」你說，保持輕鬆的語氣。 “我會和湯普森談談。給你爭取一點時間。”

 

 戈登只是咕噥了一聲。沒有留下深刻的印象。但也不爭論。 「謝謝。」他低聲說道，走開了。

 

 你拿起文件夾，坐回辦公桌前，已經在輸入訊息了。

 

 嗨，湯普森先生。只是讓你知道戈登今天仍然很緊張。他要求我處理他的開支，但我不想拖延任何事。如果我們能給他們一點餘地讓他們進來，我真的很感激💋
 

 一分鐘後，湯普森回覆了。

 

 沒關係。也許你應該稍後後來我的辦公室。我們可以討論如何製作東西

passages.name: ThompsonsReceptionAnnieWork
passages.content:
**Thompson Handyman Repairs - Reception**

 [image: ./images/Thompsons_Reception.png]

 

 
 
 You step around the front desk, taking in the clutter and chaotic mess that is Annie's filing system. Folders are sprawled out over the desk. Brightly coloured post-it notes line the edges of the monitor. A dull mouse is livened up by the glittery pink mouse it rests on.

 

 The phone begins to ring. You sit down and answer it. "Good morning," you say, echoing the words you've heard from Annie. "Thompson's Repairs."

 

 Over the next thirty minutes, you try to answer emails in-between the constant calls. Every time you answer one email, two more arrive. Most of them are minor. Mundane.

 

 “Can I push my appointment by fifteen minutes?”
“Where do I park?”
“Can you pop a reminder in my calendar?”
 

 Even the phone calls feel off. Customers want to chat. "How's your day going?" they ask, like you're just another sunny voice on the other end. They don’t care about the work. They just want the performance.

 

 You’re halfway through flagging a broken link in the supplier portal when you hear boots on the carpet.

 

 Gordon appears in the doorway. "Annie," he says, not waiting for a reply. "Booking system's broken again."

 

 You frown at him, curious. "What's wrong with it?"

 

 "Doesn't say where to go," he replies flatly. "No address attached. Theodore's making it worse."

 

 You eye him with interest. This could be a chance to win over support in getting Thompson to rehire you. You just need to think of a way to do it that will convince Gordon without raising suspicions.

passages.content_zh:
**湯普森雜工維修 - 接待處**

 [圖：./images/Thompsons_Reception.png]

 

 
 
 你在前台走了一圈，看到了安妮雜亂無章的文件系統。文件夾散落在桌子上。顯示器的邊緣排列著色彩鮮豔的便利貼。一隻沉悶的老鼠被它所棲息的閃閃發光的粉紅色老鼠變得活躍起來。

 

 電話開始響起。你坐下來回答。 「早安，」你說，呼應你從安妮那裡聽到的話。 “湯普森修理廠。”

 

 在接下來的三十分鐘裡，您嘗試在不斷的通話之間回覆電子郵件。您每回覆一封電子郵件，就會收到另外兩封電子郵件。其中大多數都是輕微的。世俗的。

 

 “我可以把預約推遲十五分鐘嗎？”
“我該把車停在哪裡？”
“你能在我的日曆中彈出提醒嗎？”
 

 連打電話都覺得不舒服。客戶想聊天。 「你今天過得怎麼樣？」他們問道，就好像你只是電話那頭另一個陽光明媚的聲音一樣。他們不關心工作。他們只是想要表演。

 

 當您在供應商入口網站中標記損壞的連結時，您聽到了靴子踩在地毯上的聲音。

 

 戈登出現在門口。 「安妮，」他說道，沒有等待回覆。 “預訂系統又壞了。”

 

 你皺著眉頭看著他，一臉好奇。 “怎麼了？”

 

 「沒有說要去哪裡，」他淡淡地回答。 “沒有附上地址。西奧多讓事情變得更糟。”

 

 你饒有興趣地看著他。這可能是贏得支持、讓湯普森重新僱用你的機會。你只需要想出一種方法來說服戈登而不引起懷疑。

 

 **行動**

 <>

**Charlton Town Centre**

 [image: ./images/Town_Centre_night.png]

 [image: ./images/Town_Centre.png]

The centre of Charlton is nothing more than a road of shops and offices, barely built up beyond the second floor. There are no skyscrapers, no bustling malls, no signs of the noise and bustle you used to call home in the city. Only the sounds of pigeons perching on the rooftops can be heard regularly.

A weathered newsrack stands on the pavement, branded with the logo for the ‘Charlton Gazette’. Thin newspapers can be seen through the glass drawer.

passages.name: YourHomeEmilyDialogue0
passages.content:
Emily looks up as you approach, pulling her knees up to her chest. You notice a book nearby, but the pages are still on the same spot you’ve seen before. A deep crease is set between her brows.

 Emily sits, absentmindedly stirring a cup of tea that’s already gone cold. She looks up when she notices you, tucking a stray strand of hair behind her ear.

 Emily looks up as you approach, idly playing with her nails. She doesn’t seem as tense anymore. In fact, she doesn’t seem emotionally engaged at all.

 Emily lounges back, her eyes drifting over towards a nearby copy of A New Woman magazine. She hums a soft tune to herself, her movements light and unbothered.

 Emily crosses her legs elegantly as she glances up at you, a smile playing on her lips. She’s adjusting a bracelet on her wrist, her movements slow and deliberate. 

 
 

 As you approach, she looks disinterested, almost as if pretending not to notice you. When she finally does, her gaze is distant, disinterested. "Did you need something?" Her tone is clipped, her patience thin.

 As you approach, there’s a flicker of hesitation before she speaks. "Oh. Hey." She smiles, but it doesn’t quite reach her eyes. There’s something careful about the way she watches you, like she’s trying to gauge your intentions before deciding how to respond.

 As you approach, her expression is unreadable. "Hey." She gives you a polite smile, but you can see in her expression that her mind is on other things that you can’t draw her away from.

 As you approach, she perks up slightly, shifting in her seat to face you properly. "Hey ! Got a minute?" There’s an easy familiarity in the way she greets you, as if she’s relieved to have you around.

 As you approach she beams, setting aside whatever she was doing without hesitation. "! How are you?" Her voice is warm, her expression open. There’s no guard, no hesitation. Just a sense of comfort in having you near.

 

 Emily stares back at you, waiting for you to speak.

當你走近時，艾蜜莉抬起頭來，將膝蓋拉到胸前。您注意到附近有一本書，但書頁仍然位於您之前見過的同一位置。她的眉間有一道深深的皺紋。

 艾蜜莉坐下來，心不在焉地攪拌著一杯已經冷了的茶。當她注意到你時，她抬起頭，將一縷頭髮別到耳後。

 當你走近時，艾蜜莉抬起頭來，懶洋洋地擺弄著她的指甲。她看起來不再那麼緊張了。事實上，她似乎根本沒有感情投入。

 艾蜜莉靠在椅背上，目光飄向附近的一本《新女性》雜誌。她自言自語地哼著輕柔的曲子，動作輕快而無拘無束。

 艾蜜莉優雅地交叉雙腿，抬頭看著你，嘴角掛著微笑。她正在調整手腕上的手鐲，動作緩慢而從容。 

 
 

 當你走近時，她看起來漠不關心，幾乎假裝沒注意到你。當她最終這樣做時，她的目光是遙遠的、漠然的。 「你需要什麼東西嗎？」她的語氣很短促，耐心也很薄弱。

 當你走近時，她在說話前猶豫了一下。 「哦。嘿。」她笑了，但笑容並沒有完全到達她的眼睛。她觀察你的方式有些謹慎，就像她在決定如何回應之前試圖判斷你的意圖一樣。

 當你走近時，她的表情難以捉摸。 「嘿。」她給了你一個禮貌的微笑，但你可以從她的表情中看出她的心思在其他你無法吸引她的事情上。

 當你走近時，她稍微振作起來，在座位上移動以正確面對你。 「嘿！有時間嗎？」她問候你的方式有一種輕鬆的熟悉感，就好像她因為有你在身邊而感到欣慰。

 當你走近時，她微笑著，毫不猶豫地拋開了她正在做的事情。 「！ 你好嗎？」她的聲音溫暖，表情開放。沒有警衛，沒有猶豫。只是一種有你在身邊的舒適感。

 

 艾米莉回望你，等待你說話。

行動

<>

<>

<>

passages.name: YourHomeTerrenceDialogue0
passages.content:
A few minutes pass before the doorbell rings. You usher your sister away and open the door to find Terrence standing there with a stack of textbooks in one hand and a pen tucked behind his ear. He offers you an easy grin. “Hey,” he says as he steps inside, eyes lingering on you.

 

 You lead him to the kitchen table. He sets the books down but keeps his gaze on you, flipping open his notebook with deliberate slowness. “So,” you prompt, “which problems are giving you trouble?”

 

 He leans forward, the book angled so he can sneak peeks at your face between the equations. “This one,” he says, tapping the page. But then his finger trails off as he catches your eye, smiling. “But honestly, I’m more interested in the teacher than the question.”

 

 You ignore his words and show him the steps through the problems. He nods, but he also reaches out, brushing the back of his hand against yours when he turns the page. “Wow, that actually makes sense,” he murmurs, voice low. “I could listen to you explain math all evening.”

 

 He closes the notebook with a soft thud and meets your gaze directly. “Mind if I take a short break? Maybe… chat a bit?”

 
 A little time passes before the doorbell rings. You usher your sister up to her room to avoid any complications and answer the door. Terrence stands awkwardly outside, a number of books stacked up in his hands. “Hey,” he nervously says to you.

 

 You invite him in and the two of you sit down at the kitchen table. He looks around, surveying the living space cautiously. “Nice house,” he comments after some time, as he finally relaxes.

 

 “So,” you begin. “What areas of maths are you struggling with?”

 

 Terrence opens an exercise book and talks you through some of the maths exercises that are proving challenging for him. You are happy that it not only seems simple to you, but that you can see where he’s tripping up.

 

 Time passes as Terrence diligently follows your tutorage. With a little encouragement, he soon improves
passages.content_zh:
幾分鐘過去了，門鈴響了。你把姊姊引開，打開門，發現泰倫斯站在那兒，一手拿著一疊課本，耳後夾著一支筆。他給你一個輕鬆的微笑。 「嘿，」他走進去時說道，眼睛一直停留在你身上。

 

 你把他帶到廚房的桌子旁。他放下書，但目光一直看著你，故意慢慢翻開筆記本。 “那麼，”你提示道，“哪些問題給你帶來了麻煩？”

 

 他向前傾身，書本傾斜，這樣他就可以在方程式之間偷偷地瞥見你的臉。 「這個，」他一邊說，一邊敲擊著頁面。但當他微笑著與你對視時，他的手指漸漸消失了。 “但說實話，我對老師比對問題更感興趣。”

 

 你無視他的話並向他展示解決問題的步驟。他點點頭，但也伸出手，在翻頁時用自己的手背擦過你的手背。 「哇，這確實有道理。」他低聲說。 “我可以整個晚上聽你解釋數學。”

 

 他輕輕合上筆記本，直視你的目光。 「介意我休息一下嗎？也許…聊一會兒？”

 
 過了一會兒門鈴就響了。你把妹妹帶到她的房間以避免任何併發症並開門。特倫斯尷尬地站在外面，手裡摞著幾本書。 「嘿，」他緊張地對你說。

 

 你邀請他進來，然後你們兩個坐在廚房的桌子旁。他環顧四周，小心翼翼地審視起居室。 「房子不錯。」過了一會兒，他終於放鬆下來，說。

 

 「所以，」你開始說。 “你在哪些數學領域遇到困難？”

 

 泰倫斯打開一本練習冊，向您介紹一些對他來說具有挑戰性的數學練習。你很高興這不僅對你來說看起來很簡單，而且你能看到他哪裡出錯了。

 

 時間隨著特倫斯孜孜不倦地遵循您的指導而流逝。只要一點點鼓勵，他很快就會進步

passages.name: YourHomeEmilyFirstDay
passages.content:
"Hi ," Emily says hesitantly. "How did you sleep?"

 

 You look down at your younger sister and find yourself reminded of how small and vulnerable you felt yesterday. No longer is Emily just another person. Something has permanently changed in your mind. Even if it's just a memory, you feel in some way that you are looking at a reflection of you.

 

 "Good thanks," you reply, pushing the uncanny feeling aside in your mind. "Or as well as can be expected."

 

 She nods, silent. Then looks down at the table, not meeting your eyes. "So. What do you want to do today?"

 

 You mull over your response. "I'm not even sure I know where to begin, Em," you begin. "This time yesterday I thought we lived in a world where magic was something in fantasy books and kids' tales. And now this." You take out the amulet from your pocket, showing Emily its shining brilliance.

 

 She looks between the amulet and you. "You could try finding out more about that amulet. Where Liz got it from and how it works."

 

 You look at Emily, quizzically. "Do you have her number?"

 

 Emily shakes her head. "But she'll be at school today. Plus, we need to understand what's happening at school."

 

 "You want me to transform again and go back again today, don't you?" you ask her.
 
 

 She bites her lip, saying nothing.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>
 
 You sigh and start to talk. "This isn't an easy decision, but I think it's best if I go to work. We need to keep up appearances. I can investigate the town to see if I can find anything about magic, the amulet or the school whilst you go there today.

 

 Emily's eyes glisten, but she holds back tears. She nods. "I understand," she murmurs.

 

 "Just keep your wits about you and try to avoid anything that feels odd," you say, trying to provide comfort. "Until we know more about what is going on, we have to assume that anything you do or say at school could have an effect on you."

 

 "I'll try," she says, standing up. She picks up her school sat
passages.content_zh:
「嗨，」艾蜜莉猶豫地說。 “睡得怎麼樣？”

 

 你低頭看著妹妹，發現自己想起了昨天的自己是多麼的渺小和脆弱。艾米麗不再只是另一個人。你的想法已經發生了永久性的改變。即使這只是一段記憶，你也會在某種程度上感覺到你正在看到自己的倒影。

 

 「很好，謝謝。」你回答道，把腦海中那種不可思議的感覺拋在了一邊。 “或者說，正如我們所預料的那樣。”

 

 她點點頭，沉默不語。然後低頭看著桌子，不看你的眼睛。 “那麼，你今天想做什麼？”

 

 你仔細考慮你的回應。 「我甚至不確定從哪裡開始，Em，」你開始說道。 「昨天的這個時候，我以為我們生活在一個魔法只是奇幻書籍和兒童故事中的東西的世界。而現在是這樣。」你從口袋裡拿出護身符，向艾米麗展示它閃閃發光的光彩。

 

 她看著護身符和你。 “你可以嘗試了解更多關於那個護身符的信息。莉茲從哪裡得到它以及它是如何工作的。”

 

 你疑惑地看著艾米莉。 “你有她的電話號碼嗎？”

 

 艾米麗搖搖頭。 “但她今天會去學校。另外，我們需要了解學校發生的事情。”

 

 「你想讓我今天再次變身，再次回去是嗎？」你問她。
 
 

 她咬著嘴唇，沒有說話。

 

 **行動**

 <>

 <>
 
 你嘆了口氣，開始說話。 「這不是一個容易的決定，但我認為最好還是去工作。我們需要保持形象。今天你去的時候，我可以調查一下這個城鎮，看看是否能找到任何關於魔法、護身符或學校的信息。

 

 艾米莉的眼睛閃閃發光，但她強忍著淚水。她點點頭。 「我明白，」她低聲說。

 

 「保持頭腦清醒，盡量避免任何感覺奇怪的事情，」你說，試圖提供安慰。 「在我們了解更多正在發生的事情之前，我們必須假設你在學校所做或所說的任何事情都可能對你產生影響。 」

 

 「我會嘗試的，」她站起來說。她坐去學校

passages.name: YourHomeTransformEmily
passages.content:
You put the necklace around your neck and clutch Emily's lipgloss, bracing yourself. The amulet tingles, almost as if the trinket is anticipating the change to come.

 

 
 Holding on to the lipgloss, you visualise Emily in your mind. You think of what she looks like now. 

 Holding on to the lipgloss, you visualise Emily in your mind. You think of what she looks like now. Dressed in her school uniform, ready for school.

 
 Holding on to the lipgloss, you visualise Emily in your mind. You think of what she looks like now. 

 Holding on to the lipgloss, you visualise Emily in your mind. You think of what she looks like now. Still dressed in her school uniform.

 
 

 Remembering the note, you speak the words out loud. “From this item, I see you in your time of need. Now form shape to the person I see within.”

 

 The emerald warms your chest against your shirt as you picture her. You take a breath and not a moment later you feel warm energy pulsate out from the emerald and spread across your body.

 

 Your hair stands up on edge as the pain cascades across your skin. You hold on to the mental picture of Emily. Her slender figure. Fair complexion. Her large expressive brown eyes. Her hair, straight and long.

 
 
You picture Emily's increased chest size and decreased height, remembering the changes Nurse Rancy made. You visualise how her uniform fits differently now. Tighter across the bust, looser at the hem. How she seems to take up less space, but draw more attention. A compact, exaggerated version of herself.

 
 
You picture Emily's increased chest size, remembering the changes Nurse Rancy made. You visualise how her shirt now clings more tightly to her bust, the weight altering her posture just slightly.

 
 
You picture Emily’s decreased height, remembering the changes Nurse Rancy made. You visualise how she now stands just that bit smaller. Unmistakably petite. You recall the way her feet barely touched the floor when she sat, or how she looked up at taller
passages.content_zh:
你把項鍊掛在脖子上，抓著艾蜜莉的唇彩，做好了準備。護身符發出刺痛感，幾乎就像是在期待即將到來的改變。

 

 
 你拿著唇彩，腦海中浮現出艾蜜莉的樣子。你想想她現在的樣子。 

 你拿著唇彩，腦海中浮現出艾蜜莉的樣子。你想想她現在的樣子。穿著校服，準備去學校。

 
 你拿著唇彩，腦海中浮現出艾蜜莉的樣子。你想想她現在的樣子。 

 你拿著唇彩，腦海中浮現出艾蜜莉的樣子。你想想她現在的樣子。還穿著她的校服。

 
 

 記住這張紙條，你大聲說出這些話。 “通過這個物品，我在你需要的時候看到了你。現在為我在裡面看到的人塑造形狀。”

 

 當你想像她時，祖母綠在你的襯衫上溫暖著你的胸口。你吸了一口氣，不一會兒，你就感覺到一股溫暖的能量從祖母綠中脈動而出，蔓延到你的全身。

 

 當疼痛傳遍你的皮膚時，你的頭髮都豎起來了。你的腦海裡一直浮現著艾蜜莉的畫面。她那苗條的身材。膚色白皙。她那雙富有表情的棕色大眼睛。她的頭髮又直又長。

 
 
你想像艾米莉的胸部增大和身高降低，還記得蘭西護士所做的改變。你可以想像她的製服現在變得多麼合身了。胸部收緊，下擺寬鬆。她似乎佔用的空間更少，但卻吸引了更多的注意力。一個緊湊而誇張的她自己。

 
 
你想像艾蜜莉胸部變大的樣子，還記得蘭西護士所做的改變。你可以想像她的襯衫現在如何更緊地貼在她的胸部上，重量稍微改變了她的姿勢。

 
 
你想像艾米莉身高的下降，想起了蘭西護士所做的改變。你可以想像她現在站得小了一點。明顯嬌小。你還記得她坐下時腳幾乎沒有接觸地板的樣子，或是她抬頭看著更高的人的樣子。

passages.name: YourHomeLipgloss
passages.content:
Emily’s riffles through her school satchel and hands you the lipgloss - a now treasured possession for her. You nod and take it.

艾米莉翻遍了她的書包，遞給你唇彩——現在這對她來說是一件珍貴的財產。你點點頭並接受它。
